Position: Backhoe Loader Operator
No. of Vacancy: 1
Location: Makwanpur

UWS Nepal is a charity organisation dedicated to improving educational opportunities for underprivileged children. We started operations in 2015 as a non-profit, the same year our country witnessed a 7.8 magnitude earthquake. Post-earthquake UWS Nepal has constructed 636 earthquake-resistant classrooms across 321 communities, educating 20,690 students in our Sankhuwasabha, Gulmi, Bhojpur, Taplejung and Tanahun districts. We aim to provide quality education to all children, and to reach that goal; we work to improve educational opportunities for children living in marginalised communities and remote regions. Our education transformation philosophy is maintained by developing and introducing child-friendly pedagogies that allow for more significant innovation in the classroom and greater engagement between the in-class lessons and real life, parents-teacher relationship, and harmonised relationship between local government and local communities in a shared mission to ensure a future for children in the communities where we work. Our vision is Zero Education Poverty.

Details / requirements:

Posiiton: Senior Global MEAL Associate

Location: Kathmandu, Nepal or Antananarivo/Toliara, Madagascar

Help us turn data into insight, and insight into impact.

This is an exciting time to join UWS.

As we roll out our ambitious 2030 Programme Strategy, we’re strengthening how we measure and demonstrate impact across Cambodia, Madagascar, Myanmar and Nepal. Our MEAL team ensures high-quality data drives learning, decision-making and programme improvement.

We’re looking for a talented and driven Senior Global MEAL Associate to join our global team and help advance our monitoring, evaluation, accountability and learning systems.

About the role

This is a fantastic opportunity for someone who loves working with data and wants to use their skills to create meaningful change.

Working with colleagues across four countries, you’ll strengthen data systems, improve data quality, analyse performance and develop clear dashboards, visualisations and reports that help turn evidence into action.

Data collection platforms, analysis, data visualisation and reporting are at the core of this role. We’re looking for someone who can turn complex data into clear, accessible insights that support better decisions.

We’re looking for someone with:

  • Strong skills in data analysis, reporting and visualisation.
  • Confidence using tools such as Excel, Power BI and data collection platforms.
  • The ability to turn complex data into clear, practical insights.
  • A collaborative, organised and detail-oriented approach.
  • A commitment to evidence-led programmes that create meaningful change.

Why join UWS?

You’ll join a supportive global team working towards Zero Education Poverty, helping shape how UWS measures and demonstrates impact across our programmes.

Most importantly, your work will directly contribute to improving programmes and expanding educational opportunities for children in some of the world’s most marginalised communities.

How to apply

Please submit:

Your CV
A cover letter (maximum two pages) outlining your suitability for the role

We also encourage candidates to share an example of previous work showing their skills in data analysis, reporting or data visualisation, such as a dashboard, report extract, slide or anonymised sample.

For full details and how to apply, please see the Job Pack and application link below. We may close applications early if we receive enough suitable applications.

The detailed Job Pack of the role can be accessed here: https://shorturl.at/urnpK

Apply Here: https://shorturl.at/cg0jN

Sindhuli Integrated Development Services Nepal (SIDS/Nepal) is a national, non-profitmaking, non- political and secular organization. SIDS/Nepal is continuously being supported by different national, international donors and I/NGOs as well as government of Nepal.

In partnership with Childaid Network Foundation Nepal, Sindhuli Integrated Development Services (SIDS Nepal) is implementing the Kopila II Project in Sindhuli District. The project aims to promote equitable access to quality education by fostering safe, inclusive, and child-friendly learning environments, strengthening teaching and learning practices, empowering parents and communities, and enhancing the responsiveness of local education systems.

SIDS invites applications from qualified, dynamic, result-oriented, and experienced Nepali citizens for the following position:

Posiiton: Sr. Finance Officer -1 No.

Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Management, or a related field from a recognized institution.
  • Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related discipline will be an added advantage.

Experience

  • Minimum 3–5 years of progressively responsible experience in finance, administration, and operations management within NGOs, INGOs, or donor-funded projects.
  • Demonstrated experience in financial planning, budgeting, accounting, financial reporting, cash flow management, and audit coordination.
  • Proven experience in managing procurement, administrative systems, asset and inventory management, and ensuring compliance with organizational and donor requirements.
  • Experience working with financial management software (preferably FAMAS) and advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other MS Office applications.
  • Experience coordinating with project teams, donors, auditors, government authorities, vendors, and other stakeholders to support effective project implementation.

Major Roles and Responsibilities

  • Ensure that all financial transactions and data are correctly implemented according to donor/ organization’s policies and procedures.
  • Support and ensure all the payments are made following proper financial procedures.
  • Ensure proper supporting documents for expense verification
  • Preparation and administration of payroll adhering the required compliance.
  • Issuance and settlement of of employee advance.
  • Reconciliation of payable and receivable ledger.
  • Preparation of bank reconciliation, Payment authorization sheet and, Keep the scan copy of voucher and submit to donor.
  • Identifying alternatives and facilitating access to the ‘best value for money’ for all products and services.
  • Ensure major / recurring procurements are under strategic sourcing initiative;
  • Propose innovative solutions to increase efficiency through evidence-based analysis for consumption by financial and operational dimensions, including price, quality and service.
  • Support in improving spending behavior, process, utilization, and optimize total throughout yield.
  • Ensure internal control measures are in place and are followed appropriately.
  • Assist in all type of audit (Internal, donor, and external)
  • Monitor budget utilization and expenditure trends to ensure cost-effective and efficient use of project resources.
  • Ensure compliance with donor agreements, organizational policies, financial procedures, and applicable laws and regulations.
  • Oversee day-to-day administrative functions of the project office and ensure a safe, efficient, and productive working environment.
  • Coordinate closely with finance team at Childaid Network Foundation Nepal, project staff, auditors, government agencies, financial institutions, and service providers.
  • Perform any other duties assigned by the Program manager or management in line with project and organizational priorities.

Application Procedure

Interested and qualified candidates are requested to submit their cover letter, copy of citizenship, copies of educational degrees and CV in a single pdf via email to sidsjobvacancy@gmail.com or to the office of SIDS Nepal Sindhuli, Kamalamai-6, Sindhuli by 26th July, 2026.

In your email, please include.

  • Your cover letter, outlining why you believe you would be the perfect fit for the job.
  • Your updated Curriculum Vitae with three recent references.
  • Copies of your key relevant educational degrees and citizenship.
  • Mention the name of the position in the subject of the email.

Only Shortlisted candidates will be called for written as well as interview. The Recruitment Committee reserve the right to reject any or all applications without assigning any reason and can withheld the entire result or partly.

content.rendered:
International Nepal Fellowship (Nepal) is a Non-Government Organization established in 1952 and serves the poor and disadvantaged people of Nepal to enable life to its fullness through health and development services. INF Nepal is currently working in health and community development services in the Gandaki, Lumbini, Karnali, and Sudurpashchim Provinces of Nepal.

For the following positions, INF Nepal is seeking competent, skilled, service oriented, and devoted Nepali citizens who shares our vision, mission, and values.

Position: Dermatology Registrar (Readvertised)

Required Number: 1

Location: Green Pastures Hospital, Pokhara

Purpose of the Job:

The post holder is responsible for providing comprehensive dermatological care under the supervision of Consultant Dermatologists. The role involves assessment, diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up of patients with skin, hair, nail, sexually transmitted diseases & Leprosy, while contributing to clinical education, quality improvement, and research activities within the department.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

  • Must have MD in Dermatology along with valid specialty registration at Nepal Medical Council.
  • Candidates with experience or freshers are encouraged to apply. Candidates having skills in dermoscopy and dermatological procedures is an advantage.
  • Training in cosmetic dermatology and laser procedures is an advantage.
  • Strong clinical and diagnostic skills in clinical dermatology, venereology and leprosy practice.
  • Must have knowledge of dermatological procedure and treatment.
  • Excellent communication and patient counselling skills.
  • Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team and maintain hierarchy.
  • Competence in medical documentation and electronic medical records.
  • Ability to work under pressure and manage clinical priorities.
  • Compassion and empathy towards patient, and has professional integrity and accountability.
  • Commitment to continuous learning and professional development.
  • Good understanding of and agreement with the values which are important to INF Nepal.

Position: Medical Officer- PC

Required Number: 1

Location: Green Pastures Hospital, Pokhara

Purpose of the Job:

The post holder is a part of the Palliative Care (PC) team providing a holistic clinical Palliative Care Services- inpatient, outpatient consultations; community home visits; day therapy support enabling adults and children with life limiting illnesses and their families. The Medical Officer will work collaboratively and closely with Green Pastures Hospital’s multidisciplinary team; is involved with the various ongoing research and audit projects undertaken by the service; will be expected to participate in appropriate Palliative Care training and will contribute to strategic Palliative Care education and training.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

  • Must have an MBBS Degree, registered at Nepal Medical Council.
  • Must have Minimum of one year of experience in hospital service.
  • Experience in pediatric care, palliative care, rehabilitation or community health programmes is an advantage.
  • Excellent clinical assessment and patient management skills.
  • Strong facilitation and training skills along with excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
  • Maintains confidentiality and high ethical standards.
  • Strong report writing and documentation skills. Good command of spoken and written English and Nepali.
  • Good computer skills (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Email).
  • Demonstrates compassion, empathy and resect when caring for children with life- limiting illnesses and their families.
  • Good planning, coordination and problem-solving skills.
  • Good understanding of and agreement with the values which are important to INF Nepal.

Position: Medical Officer

Required Number: 1

Location: Green Pastures Hospital, Pokhara

Purpose of the Job:

The post holder is responsible for providing safe, timely, and evidence-based medical care to patients admitted to the Accident & Trauma Room (ATR), Intensive Care Unit (ICU), and Inpatient Department (IPD). The role focuses on initial patient assessment, stabilization, ongoing clinical management, accurate documentation, and performance of essential procedures within competency, effective communication with consultants and multidisciplinary teams, and participation in shift-based service delivery.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

  • Must have an MBBS Degree, registered at Nepal Medical Council.
  • Freshers are encouraged to apply.
  • Certification in Basic Life Support (BLS), Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support (ACLS) is an advantage.
  • Experience in emergency medicine, ICU or inpatient care is preferred.
  • Must demonstrate sensitivity and confidentiality.
  • Must have leadership skills, cross-cultural skills, interpersonal and communication skills.
  • Good clinical history-taking, examination, and making relevant differential diagnosis skills.
  • Must have basic computer skill and knowledge of software/systems related to hospitals.
  • Good English Language skills both written and spoken.
  • Must have proactive approach to patient care and must be honest and demonstrate integrity.
  • Good understanding of, and agreement with the values which are important to INF.

Position: Project Supervisor

Required Number: 1

Location: Green Pastures Hospital, Pokhara

Purpose of the Job:

The post holder need to ensure the effective implementation of the Comfort Beyond Cure for Children with Life-Limiting Illness (CBCCLI) Project by coordinating day-to-day project activities, facilitating trainings, community engagement, caregiver support, beneficiary follow-up and project documentation, thereby contributing to the achievement of the project’s objectives, targets and expected outcomes.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

  • Must have diploma in General Medicine (Health Assistant) or Diploma in Proficiency Certificate Level in Nursing (PCL Nursing) with minimum of 1 year of experience in health, rehabilitation, community development or project implementation.
  • Bachelors degree in Public Health, Nursing, Social Work or related discipline is an advantage.
  • Experience working with children, persons with disabilities or vulnerable communities is an advantage.
  • Experience in organizing health trainings and community programmes is an advantage.
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal and organizational skills.
  • Strong coordination and stakeholder engagement skills.
  • Good computer skills (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Email and Internet). Good command of spoken and written English and Nepali language.
  • Demonstrates compassion, empathy and respect towards children with life limiting illnesses and their families.
  • Ability to organize multiple activities and work independently with minimum supervision.
  • Willing to travel frequently within the project area and work flexibly accordingly to programmes needs.
  • Demonstrates commitment to INF Nepal Vision, Mission and Values and actively promotes dignity, inclusion and compassionate care for vulnerable children and their families.

Position: Senior Medical Officer

Required Number: 1

Location: Shining Hospital INF Banke

Purpose of the Job:

The job holder is responsible for contributing towards setting up, developing, maintaining and enhancing the technical standard of the dermatology and leprosy care of Shining Hospital INF Banke, aiming to make SHIB a specialized hospital for care of Leprosy and dermatology in western part of Nepal.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

  • Must have an MBBS Degree, registered at Nepal Medical Council with a minimum of one year of post-qualification clinical experience. However, freshers are also encouraged to apply.
  • Experience in teaching, research, and community health is an advantage.
  • Must have good people skills and good communication skills.
  • Must demonstrate sensitivity and be able to keep all matters related to patient and work confidentiality.
  • Must have leadership skills, cross-cultural skills, interpersonal and communication skills.
  • Good clinical history-taking, examination, and making relevant differential diagnosis skills.
  • Must have basic computer skill and knowledge of software/systems related to hospitals.
  • Good English Language skills both written and spoken.
  • Must have proactive approach to patient care and must be honest and demonstrate integrity.
  • Good understanding of, and agreement with the values which are important to INF.

Position: Pharmacy Assistant

Required Number: 1

Location: Shining Hospital INF Banke

Purpose of the Job:

The post holder is responsible to dispense drugs and medicines to patients of SHIB (OPD, IPD) and others with doctor’s prescription with proper health education and counselling to patients. He/ She is responsible to maintain stocks, proper storage of medicines, labelling and keep all medicine inventory records and sales records including daily sales transactions.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

  • Must have a diploma in Pharmacy, registered at Nepal Pharmacy Council with a minimum of one year of experience in relevant work.
  • Good communication skills, knowledge of Nepali, Awadhi and Tharu language.
  • A genuine interest in helping people.
  • Able to keep confidential in all matters related to patient and work.
  • To be honest and demonstrate integrity.
  • Must have basic computer skill and knowledge of software/systems related to hospitals.
  • Good English Language skills both written and spoken.
  • Must have proactive approach to patient care and must be honest and demonstrate integrity.
  • Good understanding of, and agreement with the values which are important to INF.

All eligible and interested candidates who meet the specified criteria are encouraged to apply via the link below by 25th July, 2026.

Apply here: https://career.inf.org.np

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted to participate in the further assessment process.

INF Nepal encourages applications from persons with disabilities, disadvantaged and vulnerable groups.

INF Nepal reserves the right to hold partial or whole applications process at any time without giving any reason whatsoever. Any effort by the candidates to influence the selection process may result in disqualification.

For more information on INF Nepal please visit our website: https://www.inf.org.np

Posiiton: Head of Programs

FAIRMED is a Swiss-based international development NGO with its motto, “Health for the Poorest.” The organization works to improve access to health care for marginalized communities and individuals affected by disease, disability, and living in poverty. In Nepal, FAIRMED collaborates with government and other stakeholders on prevention, management and control of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs), and Maternal and Neonatal Health (MNH) as a thematic area. Gender Equality and Social Inclusion (GESI) and Disability-Inclusive Development (DID) are integrated as cross-cutting themes across FAIRMED’s programs. FAIRMED invites applications for the role of Head of Programs, who will lead FAIRMED Nepal’s programs working with Country Coordinator and other team members. The position will be based in the Country Office in Lalitpur with field travels to project sites.

Details / requirements:

About the position

The Head of Programs coordinates and technically leads FAIRMED Nepal’s program portfolio under the supervision of the Country Coordinator and in close coordination with FAIRMED Switzerland. The role is responsible for program quality, planning, implementation, monitoring, evaluation, learning and reporting, as well as project-level budget monitoring and compliance within the program portfolio, in line with FAIRMED’s strategy, project cycle management principles, donor requirements and international standards. The role provides line management to assigned program staff, technical backstopping to project teams, supports proposal development and institutional fundraising, strengthens program partnerships at technical and operational level, and contributes to evidence-based learning, documentation and knowledge management.

As a member of the management team, the position supports Country Coordinator in overall responsibility for strategic leadership, organizational management, representation, legal compliance, duty of care, risk management and overall accountability for FAIRMED Nepal.

Major job responsibilities

  • Lead the quality planning, implementation, monitoring, evaluation, learning, and reporting of FAIRMED Nepal programs in line with organizational strategy, donor requirements, and international standards.
  • Support the adaptation and implementation of FAIRMED’s global strategy, policies, and cross-cutting priorities (Leave No One Behind, Right to Health, Disability Inclusion, Gender Equality, and Social Inclusion) in the Nepal context.
  • Provide technical leadership in health system strengthening, neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), maternal and newborn health (MNH), disability inclusion, and community-based health programs.
  • Strengthen program quality through evidence generation, knowledge management, implementation research, and regular field monitoring.
  • Coordinate program partnerships, contribute to resource mobilization, and support the development of high-quality concept notes and proposals.
  • Oversee project-level budget monitoring, compliance, safeguarding, accountability, and risk management in collaboration with the finance and operations team.
  • Supervise and mentor program staff, promote capacity development, and foster a positive, inclusive, and safe working environment.
  • Support the Country Coordinator in representing FAIRMED with government, donors, UN agencies, INGOs, and other stakeholders, and perform other program-related duties as assigned.

Required Qualifications and Experiences

  • Master’s degree in public health, or any other health relevant degree, with at least 10 years of relevant experience in program development and management in the development or health sector.
  • Sound knowledge and experience of working with Nepal’s health system, government structures and relevant coordination mechanisms at federal, provincial and local levels.
  • Experience in formulating and implementing program strategy, project cycle management, proposal development, monitoring, evaluation, learning and reporting.
  • Strong skills in team leadership, supervision, mentoring and capacity development.
  • Good understanding of budget monitoring, financial analysis, forecasting and donor compliance.
  • Excellent analytical, facilitation and communication skills in English and Nepali.
  • Commitment to FAIRMED’s values, Code of Conduct, safeguarding standards, anti-corruption principles and PSEAH requirements.
  • Willingness to travel regularly to project areas and work in challenging contexts when required.
  • Ability to prioritize, manage work pressure effectively in complex or challenging circumstances.
  • Ability to represent FAIRMED’s technical work effectively in delegated forums and contribute to constructive partnerships.

Deadline for Application Submission : 5 August 2026

Application Submission: Interested Nepalese candidates are requested to fill up the google form in the link below:

Application Form: https://forms.gle/o6TwjVTDJTA4KiRn9

Detailed Job Description: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1GxZ7yk_AwUp-L6ARc74nu63jg-hhwCAT/view?usp=sharing

Note: FAIRMED is committed to promoting gender equality and strongly encourages qualified female candidates to apply. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Posiiton: Psychological Counselor

Key Responsibilities

1. Individual Counseling

  • Conduct confidential one-on-one counseling sessions for students experiencing emotional, behavioral, social, academic, or psychological challenges.
  • Assist students in developing coping strategies, resilience, emotional regulation, and problem-solving skills.
  • Support students dealing with anxiety, stress, depression, self-esteem concerns, relationship issues, adjustment difficulties, and identity-related challenges.

2. Group Counseling and Student Support Programs

  • Design and facilitate group counseling sessions addressing common student concerns.
  • Conduct psychoeducational programs focused on:
  • Stress and anxiety management
  • Study skills and time management
  • Emotional intelligence
  • Healthy relationships
  • Bullying prevention
  • Self-esteem and confidence building

3. Mental Health Awareness and Prevention

  • Develop and implement preventive mental health initiatives across the school.
  • Organize workshops, seminars, and awareness campaigns for students, parents, and staff.
  • Promote positive mental health practices and emotional well-being throughout the school community.

4. Crisis Intervention and Safeguarding

  • Provide immediate support to students facing emotional crises, self-harm risks, suicidal ideation,
    abuse, trauma, or other urgent concerns.
  • Coordinate with school leadership, parents, and external professionals when necessary.
  • Follow established safeguarding, child protection, and crisis management procedures.

5. Consultation and Collaboration

  • Provide guidance and consultation to teachers, Academic Coordinators, Grade Coordinators, and parents regarding students’ psychological and behavioral needs.
  • Recommend appropriate intervention strategies and classroom accommodations.
  • Participate in multidisciplinary meetings to support student welfare.

6. Assessment and Referral

  • Conduct preliminary assessments to identify students requiring psychological support.
  • Maintain referral networks with psychologists, psychiatrists, therapists, and other mental health professionals.
  • Monitor referred cases and coordinate follow-up support.

7. Documentation and Record Management

  • Maintain accurate, confidential, and secure counseling records.
  • Prepare periodic reports, case summaries, and intervention records while maintaining professional confidentiality.
  • Ensure compliance with organizational policies and ethical counseling standards.

8. Research and Professional Development

  • Stay updated on current developments, research, and best practices in school counseling and child psychology.
  • Recommend evidence-based interventions and programs to enhance student well-being.
  • Participate in professional development activities and trainings.

9. Teamwork and Institutional Support

  • Collaborate effectively with academic and administrative departments.
  • Support school events, student welfare programs, and institutional initiatives when required.
  • Maintain professional relationships with all stakeholders.

10. Professional Conduct and Discipline

  • Demonstrate professionalism, integrity, confidentiality, and ethical behavior at all times.
  • Adhere to the school’s code of conduct, dress code, and workplace policies.
  • Wear official identification during working hours.

Qualifications and Experience

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Psychology, Counseling Psychology, Clinical Psychology, Educational Psychology, or a related field. Master’s Degree in a relevant field shall be preferred.
  • Minimum 2 years of relevant counseling experience, preferably in an educational institution.
  • Strong understanding of child and adolescent psychology.
  • Knowledge of counseling theories and therapeutic approaches including CBT, Solution-Focused Therapy, and Mindfulness-Based Interventions.
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal, and active listening skills.
  • Proficiency in English and Nepali communication.

Salary, Allowances & Perks

Highly attractive and negotiable

Application Expiry Date: 2026-08-16

Social Awareness Centre (SAC) Nepal is a NGO dedicated to upholding the rights of marginalized and excluded communities through its interventions on Health, Education, Livelihood, Protection and Climate Change, Disaster Management and Humanitarian Support including Organizational Development and GEDSI. It works with a vision for capable and prosperous society for quality life of women, children and adolescents. SAC Nepal is implementing the different projects partnership with different partners. The projects covered all 10 districts of Karnali Province.

SAC Nepal is going to implementing the UPLIFT (BVLGARI) project from July 2026 in Birendranagar Municipality of Surkht district in partnership with Save the Children funded by BVLGARI and coordination with local and provincial governments as well as other stakeholders. Therefore, SAC Nepal invites applications from interested qualified and professional Nepali candidates for the following full-time positions.

The Youth Acting for Change Nepal (YAC Nepal) is a non-political and non-profit making social development organization. This organization was established in 2057 BS (2000 AD) by dedicated young professionals with extensive experience in the field of social development. It was registered in the District Administration Office, Kailali, on the 26th of Ashoj, 2061 BS (12th October 2004) as per Organization Registration Act, 2034 BS and affiliated with Social Welfare Council, with registration number 16923, as per the Social Welfare Act, 2049 BS and has been working in Sudurpaschim province of Nepal since 2004 with focus on promotion of Human Right to Food, improving livelihood of poor vulnerable and socially excluded people through community led action for Governance and justice.

In consortium with CARE Nepal, iDE Nepal, and YAC Nepal is implementing the Nurture Every Future: Poshan Nepal project in five municipalities of Kailali district (2 Rural Municipalities: Kailari and Janaki and 3 Municipalities: Bhajani, Ghodaghodi, and Godawari). The project leverages expertise in food, nutrition, health, water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH), market systems, and Gender Equality and Social Inclusion (GESI) sectors and governance.

In this connection, YAC Nepal invites applications from eligible and qualified candidates for the following positions under the Nurture Every Future: Poshan Nepal Project who meet the specified criteria.

Posiiton: Admin and Logistic Assistant
No. of Vacancy: 1
Location: Kailali

Required Qualification and Experience

  • Minimum Intermediate level in Management (Major subjects: Finance or Account) with at least 2-year experience in admin and logistics management, or Bachelor level in Management (preferable)
  • Preferably in NGOs, INGOs, government projects, or private organizations.
  • Experience in office administration, including record keeping, filing systems, correspondence, and document management.
  • Proven experience in procurement processes, vendor management, quotation collection, and inventory control.
  • Experience in managing travel arrangements, vehicle coordination, accommodation booking, and event logistics.
  • Knowledge of asset management, stock management, and office supply tracking.
  • Experience in organizing meetings, workshops, trainings, and conferences, including venue, catering, and logistical arrangements.
  • Familiarity with financial and procurement policies, compliance requirements, and organizational procedures.
  • Strong skills in coordination and communication with staff, suppliers, government offices, and stakeholders.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and administrative reporting.
  • Ability to work under pressure, manage multiple tasks, and meet deadlines effectively

Preferred Requirement: Candidate have, prior experience working with office administration and logistics management.

Detailed Job Description

Required documents to be submitted along with application.

  • Application letter.
  • Copy of Citizenship.
  • Copy of Academic and training Certificates.
  • Experience letter and copy of appointment letter
  • Updated CV with references of full details of two people who are willing to act as a reference.
  • Two-wheeler license is mandatory for the candidates applying for Officer Level.

Application Submission Guidelines

  • All supporting documents should be combined and compiled into a single PDF file, preferably arranged in chronological order. The total file size must not exceed 5 MB.

How to apply:

Qualified and eligible national citizens are highly encouraged to apply directly by submitting the required documents to career@yacnepal.org.np or can also submit their hardcopies at Youth Acting for Change Nepal (YAC Nepal) office no later than 5:00 PM by 22nd July, 2026. We encourage application from women, vulnerable and marginalized groups. The institution will not be obliged to accept the applications that have been submitted after the deadline, do not meet the specified educational qualifications, do not have required experience, do not meet the specified criteria or are not completed correctly. The organization reserves the right to accept or reject any application, fully or partially.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further selection process. No telephone inquiries will be entertained and any inappropriate recommendation might automatically disqualify the candidate from further processing. The remuneration and other benefits package shall be as of YAC Nepal Human Resource Management policy and guidelines.

Selection Process: Written and Interview (Physical)

POSITION: Admin & Finance Officer
REPORTS TO: Admin & Finance Manager
Location: Central Office

The Admin & Finance Officer (Junior Level) is responsible for supporting the organization in day-to-day financial, administrative, and operational functions of projects and the central office. The position assists in accounting, documentation, financial transactions, logistics coordination, reporting, compliance, and administrative support in accordance with organizational policies, donor requirements, and government regulations.

The position requires a motivated and detail-oriented professional with at least 2 years of experience in accounting, finance, NGO operations, or auditing-related work.

MAJOR DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

A. Administration Responsibilities

  • Support the maintenance and updating of fixed assets and inventory records of projects and the organization.
  • Conduct periodic inventory and asset verification.
  • Support procurement, logistics, and supply management activities as per organizational procedures.
  • Coordinate office administration and filing/documentation systems.
  • Assist in SWC related work
  • Assist in organizing meetings, workshops, training, and field visits.
  • Coordinate travel arrangements, accommodation, and logistics for staff and consultants.
  • Support policy orientation and administrative compliance for staff as required.
  • Carry out other administration-related assignments provided by the supervisor.

B. Finance & Accounting Responsibilities

  • Accounting and reporting of projects as per the guidelines and format provided by the partner Organization in coordination with the finance manager.
  • Review and check the reimbursement/settlement requests of staff, vendors, and consultants.
  • Follow the organization and partner’s policies and guidelines to ensure compliance with all the requirement and necessary documents for financial transactions.
  • Prepare vouchers and maintain systematic documentation on a timely basis.
  • Prepare bank reconciliations statement of project account monthly.
  • Handle petty cash of the project.
  • Prepare and submit monthly financial reports to the finance manager on a monthly, quarterly, & Semi Annual basis.
  • Timely tax deduction, tax deposit, and ETDS filing as per prevailing laws.
  • Assist in budget preparation, budget tracking, and fund forecasting in coordination with supervisor.
  • Support monitoring of project expenditures and budget utilization.
  • Maintain proper financial documentation for audit purposes.
  • Assist external and internal audits by preparing required documents and information.
  • Identify documentation or accounting issues and report them to the supervisor for corrective Action.
  • Maintain coordination and effective communication with project teams and finance department staff.
  • Perform other finance-related duties assigned by the supervisor.

C. Compliance & Documentation

  • Ensure all financial and administrative documents are complete, accurate, and properly archived.
  • Maintain confidentiality of organizational and financial information.
  • Support compliance with donor agreements, organizational policies, and statutory requirements.

D. Safety & Security

  • Support safe handling and security of cash, financial documents, and organizational assets.
  • Report potential operational or financial risks to the supervisor.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Management, Finance, Accounting, or related field.

Experience

  • Minimum 2 years of professional experience in accounting, finance, NGO operations, or auditing field.
  • Experience working in NGOs or audit firms will be preferred.

Required Skills & Competencies

  • Basic knowledge of accounting principles and financial management.
  • Familiarity with taxation, ETDS filing, and financial documentation.
  • Good understanding of administrative and logistics processes.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Package (especially Excel and Word) and accounting software(Saralbooks, FAMAS).
  • Good communication and coordination skills.
  • Ability to work in a team and under pressure.
  • Strong organizational and documentation skills.
  • High level of honesty, integrity, and confidentiality.

Safeguarding and Code of Conduct:

  • Ensure compliance with organizational safeguarding policies including Prevention of Sexual Exploitation, Abuse, and Harassment (PSEAH).
  • Maintain professional conduct and contribute to a safe and respectful working environment.

Applying Procedure: Interested candidates are requested to fill the application form CLICK HERE

We will be reviewing the applications on a Rolling Basis, however the last date to submit your application by COB- 25TH July 2026.

Note:

Eligible candidates with disabilities, women and from the local area are encouraged to apply. We are committed to ensuring the safety, dignity, and well-being of all individuals and maintains zero tolerance for sexual exploitation, abuse, harassment, or any other form of harm. All shortlisted candidates will be required to comply with KOSHISH’s Safeguarding Policy and Code of Conduct and will undergo appropriate safeguarding checks KOSHISH reserves the right to accept or reject any or all applications without assigning any reason.

JOB POSITION: Front Office Receptionist
REPORTS TO: HR Coordinator
LOCATION: Central Office

KOSHISH, established in 2008, is a non-governmental organization dedicated to promoting mental health and psychosocial well-being in Nepal. Embracing the spirit of “NOTHING ABOUT US WITHOUT US,” KOSHISH advocates for the inclusion and meaningful participation of individuals with mental health conditions and psychosocial disabilities. KOSHISH actively engages in community-based mental health programs, awareness campaigns, and advocacy efforts. Our overarching goal is to mainstream mental health within the broader health system and societal domains, such as social protection, education, inclusion, and employment.

Overall Purpose:

The Receptionist serves as the first point of contact for KOSHISH, creating a professional, welcoming, and inclusive experience for all visitors, partners, and callers. The role is responsible for managing the front office efficiently by receiving and directing telephone calls, welcoming and assisting visitors, maintaining visitor records, coordinating appointments, and ensuring smooth day-to-day front desk operations. The Receptionist also provides administrative support, handles incoming and outgoing correspondence, and help maintain an organized reception area.

We are looking for a smart, confident, and customer-oriented individual with excellent communication and interpersonal skills. The ideal candidate should be proactive, well-organized, able to multitask, and capable of handling inquiries with professionalism, courtesy, and confidentiality while representing KOSHISH’s values and commitment to quality service.

Major Duties and Responsibilities:

Front Office and Visitor Management

  • Welcome and assist visitors, clients, and stakeholders in a courteous and professional manner.
  • Maintain visitor records and ensure visitors are directed to the appropriate staff member.
  • Keep the reception area clean, organized, and presentable at all times.

Telephone & Communication Management

  • Receive incoming telephone calls, respond to general enquiries, and direct calls to the appropriate staff members promptly.
  • Take accurate messages when staff are unavailable and ensure timely communication.
  • Handle incoming and outgoing correspondence, including emails, letters, and courier services, as required.

Administrative Support

  • Provide day-to-day administrative support, including scheduling appointments, coordinating meeting room bookings, maintaining office records, inventory of goods etc.
  • Support staff with routine administrative tasks and assist in the smooth functioning of office operations.
  • Maintain confidentiality while handling sensitive information and represent KOSHISH with professionalism and integrity at all times.

Safeguarding and Ethical Compliance

  • Ensure compliance with KOSHISH’s Safeguarding and Prevention of Sexual Exploitation, Abuse and Harassment (PSEAH) policies in all administrative and financial processes.
  • Promote a culture of respect, integrity, accountability, and ethical conduct within all financial and administrative activities.
  • Maintain confidentiality and ensure responsible handling of organizational and personal information.
  • Report any safeguarding concerns, suspected misconduct, or policy violations through appropriate channels in line with organizational procedures.

Personal Competency

  • Professional and welcoming attitude.
  • Good interpersonal and customer service skills.
  • Smart, confident, and presentable.
  • Ability to multitask and prioritize tasks effectively.
  • Responsible, reliable, and detail-oriented.
  • Must possess a valid two-wheeler riding license and be willing to travel for official administrative tasks when required.

Technical Competency

  • Minimum +2 level education with 1–2 years of experience in reception, front desk, or administrative roles.
  • Good communication skills in Nepali and English with proficiency in MS Office and email communication.
  • Ability to manage calls, visitors, records, and administrative tasks with professionalism and confidentiality.

Applying Procedure:

Interested candidates are requested to fill the application form- CLICK Here

We will be reviewing the applications on a Rolling Basis, however, the last date to submit your application is 25th July 2026.

Note:

Eligible candidates with disabilities, women and from the local area are encouraged to apply. We are committed to ensuring the safety, dignity, and well-being of all individuals and maintains zero tolerance for sexual exploitation, abuse, harassment, or any other form of harm. All shortlisted candidates will be required to comply with KOSHISH’s Safeguarding Policy and Code of Conduct and will undergo appropriate safeguarding checks KOSHISH reserves the right to accept or reject any or all applications without assigning any reason.

  •  Minimum 3–5 years of experience in a manufacturing
  •  Hands-on experience with PLC, SCADA, VFDs & motor control systems
  •  Strong troubleshooting, analytical & technical problem-solving skills

Pravasi Nepali Coordination Committee (PNCC) is a non-profit, non-political organization established in 2009 by returnee migrant workers. PNCC is dedicated to protecting and promoting the rights and well-being of Nepali migrant workers and their families. PNCC operates nationally and internationally with a wide network and in collaboration with government bodies, community organizations, and international agencies to facilitate the issues of distressed migrant workers.

PNCC invites applications from competent Nepali citizens for the following position to implement paralegal services for the Safer Migration (SaMi) Programme:

Position Title: Programme Officer (Full -time)

Location: PNCC head office, Kathmandu, Nepal

Application Deadline: 23 July, 2026 before 5pm

Duration: 11 Months (with possibility of extension)

Organization: Pravasi Nepali Coordination Committee (PNCC)

Position Summary

This position role is focuses on providing para-legal and remedial support to distressed Nepali migrant workers and their families refer by various source such as Migrant Resource Centers (MRCs), Employment Service Centers (ESCs) and others. The position involves handling cases from intake to resolution by coordinating with different stakeholder’s such as PNCC’s outreach coordinator, Nepal embassies, Social organization in Countries of Destination, DoFE, FEB, Consular department, Recruitment Agencies, local government and other related stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

  • Responsible for providing possible para-legal remedial services support to the distressed Nepali migrant workers primarily referred by Migrant Resource Centers and SaMi national partners.
  • Record the details of cases in the Case Management Information System (CMIS), ensuring documentation of evidence from victims or perpetrators (using video/audio whenever possible) and maintain records of the progress made in the cases.
  • Prepare process documentation, including translations, and writing applications on behalf of migrant workers and their families, to respond to the cases effectively and promptly.
  • Support families of destitute Nepali migrant workers in obtaining compensation and benefits guaranteed by the existing law of Nepal and destination countries.
  • Provide regular updates to Migrant Resources Centers, migrant workers, and members of their families, and referrers. .
  • Establish communication channels with the Nepalese mission through emails and phone calls to both offer and seek support for migrant workers, including the immediate referrals of the cases to the embassies and follow-up on their progress.
  • Prepare and provide working progress reports to the supervisor/management of PNCC regularly.
  • Make regular communication and follow-ups with respective country outreach coordinators, volunteers, embassies, and relevant stakeholders.

Required Qualifications and Skills

  • Minimum of a Bachelor’s degree in Social Sciences, Development Studies, Public Administration, Migration Studies, or a related field with a minimum of 3 years of relevant work experience in rescue and repatriation/paralegal support for migrant workers and their families.
  • Practical experience in case management and ability to handle MW cases smoothly,
  • Strong knowledge of labor migration issues, migration services, and Nepal’s foreign employment policies.
  • Competency in data entry, collection, analysis, and reporting.
  • Average command of written and spoken English and Nepali.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and digital communication tools.
  • Adaptability and flexibility of responding distressed migrant worker’s cases whenever required

Application Procedure

Interested candidates are requested to submit a cover letter indicating their interest in the position, their suitability for the role, and their expected remuneration. In addition, candidates must submit an updated CV along with a recent passport-sized photograph. They are also required to provide the contact details of two professional referees.

Applications should be sent by email to info@pncc.org.np, clearly mentioning in the subject line: Application for the required position. The application deadline is 23th July, 2026 before 5pm. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further steps.

Position: MEAL Officer [Entry Level]

Application Deadline: July 31, 2026

Human Development and Community Services (HDCS) is a faith-based NGO working in three main sectors of health, education, and community development since 1991. Currently, it manages various projects across Nepal especially in
rural regions and provides services through hospitals, health camps, public health programs, disaster preparedness and response, school development programs, teacher training, educational daycare centres for special needs children, and community radio stations. HDCS is committed to attracting and retaining the best employees from all ethnicities, gender and backgrounds in our vision to transform the communities.

About the Position

Location: Central Office with frequent and weeks-long stays in project locations

Employment Type: Full-time 40 working hours per week. The employment contract will be on a yearly basis.

We are looking for a motivated and curious individual to join our team as a MEAL Officer (Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability and Learning). This position is ideal for someone who enjoys working with data, learning new technologies, understanding community perspectives, and helping projects make better decisions through evidence and insights.
The MEAL Officer will support project monitoring, data collection and analysis, reporting, learning processes, and the development of practical digital solutions. The role offers great opportunities for professional growth in MEAL, data management and technology.

Key Responsibilities

  •  Support project monitoring, data collection, and field visits.
  •  Manage, clean, verify, and organize project data.
  •  Support development and improvement of data collection tools and monitoring systems.
  •  Analyze data and contribute to dashboards, reports, and visualizations.
  •  Support evaluations, assessments, and community feedback processes.
  • Document lessons learned, best practices, and project insights.
  • Contribute to knowledge management and organizational learning.
  •  Support colleagues in using data collection tools and digital systems.
  •  Explore and test technology and AI-based solutions that improve organizational workflows.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent with specialization such as Administration, Project Management, Engineering, IT Programming, Social Science, Public Health, Environmental Science, Economics, or other.
  • Course work in data analysis, programming, etc are expected.
  •  At least one experience related to project monitoring, evaluation, research, data collection, data analysis, or community-based work through employment, internship, volunteering, academic projects, or similar activities.
  •  Strong verbal and written communication skills in English
  • Willingness to travel regularly to project locations.

Desired Competencies

We are particularly looking for candidates who demonstrate:

  •  Strong learning ability and curiosity.
  •  Analytical and problem-solving skills.
  •  Integrity, honesty, and attention to detail.
  •  Interest in data, technology, and digital tools.
  •  Understanding of or interest in rural development and community transformation.
  •  Good communication and teamwork skills.
  •  Ability to learn new software and systems quickly.

Experience with Excel, Google Sheets, digital data collection tools, dashboards, research, or AI tools such as ChatGPT is an advantage but not required.
We highly want to encourage female candidates to apply for this technical position as well.

Company: Shaurya Cement Industries Limited
Position: Assistant Manager- Finance & Accounts
Location: Corporate Office, Gairidhara, Kathmandu

Position Overview

Shaurya Cement Industries Limited is seeking a competent and experienced Finance & Accounts professional with a strong background in Manufacturing or Trading organizations. The ideal candidate should have an MBS or MBA in Finance/Accounting and possess strong practical knowledge of accounting, financial reporting, MIS, costing, taxation, and preparation of complete financial statements.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage day-to-day financial accounting and reporting functions.
  • Prepare and finalize complete financial statements, including the Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow Statement, and related financial reports.
  • Prepare and analyze regular MIS reports for management decision-making.
  • Prepare budgets, forecasts, variance analysis, and financial performance reports.
  • Conduct product-wise costing, cost analysis, and profitability analysis.
  • Monitor and control manufacturing and operational costs, including raw materials, fuel, power, packing, and production expenses.
  • Manage inventory accounting, valuation, stock verification, and reconciliation through ERP/SAP systems.
  • Ensure timely compliance with VAT, Income Tax, TDS, and other applicable statutory requirements.
  • Coordinate with internal, tax, and statutory auditors.
  • Maintain and strengthen accounting procedures, internal controls, and financial governance.
  • Analyze financial data and provide insights to management for effective business decisions.
  • Lead, supervise, and mentor the Finance & Accounts team.
  • Coordinate with factory and other business units regarding financial and accounting matters.

Qualification & Experience

  • Master’s degree in Business Studies (MBS) or MBA in Finance/Accounting is mandatory.
  • Minimum 5 years of relevant experience in Finance & Accounts.
  • Candidates must be currently working in a Manufacturing or Trading organization.
  • Strong practical experience in preparing complete financial statements and management reports.
  • Strong knowledge and hands-on experience in MIS preparation and analysis.
  • Sound knowledge of financial accounting, cost accounting, budgeting, inventory management, taxation, and financial controls.
  • Experience with ERP/SAP or other accounting systems will be preferred.
  • Experience in the cement, steel, FMCG, trading, or other manufacturing/process industries will be an added advantage.
  • Strong analytical, leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.
  • Willingness to travel frequently to the factory located in Mirchaiya, Siraha.

Posiiton: Front Desk Officer
No. of Vacancy: 1

The Front Desk Officer serves as the first point of contact for students, parents, visitors, and external stakeholders. The role is responsible for providing professional reception services, managing communication and documentation, supporting administrative functions, maintaining records, ensuring visitor management and security compliance, and contributing to the smooth day-to-day operation of the school.

Key Responsibilities

1. Reception and Communication Management

  • Receive, answer, and route incoming telephone calls promptly and professionally.
  • Provide accurate, timely, and courteous information to parents, students, staff, and visitors.
  • Take messages and relay information to concerned personnel without delay.
  • Handle walk-in inquiries and direct visitors to the appropriate departments.

2. Visitor Management and Customer Service

  • Welcome parents, visitors, guests, and stakeholders in a professional and friendly manner.
  • Maintain visitor sign-in and sign-out records.
  • Issue visitor passes and ensure compliance with school security procedures.
  • Guide visitors regarding school facilities, offices, and services.
  • Ensure a positive and professional experience for all visitors.

3. Document Control, Filing, and Record Keeping

  • Receive, record, and distribute incoming letters, couriers, and official documents.
  • Maintain registers for incoming and outgoing correspondence.
  • Preserve copies of outgoing documents for future reference.
  • Manage internal reference numbering systems and filing mechanisms.
  • Maintain organized physical and digital records for easy retrieval.

4. Administrative Support

  • Provide administrative assistance to school leadership, teachers, and staff.
  • Support photocopying, printing, scanning, filing, data entry, and document preparation.
  • Assist in preparing reports, presentations, meeting materials, and official communications.
  • Coordinate routine administrative tasks as assigned.

5. Stationery and Student Supply Management

  • Issue notebooks, registers, hats, and other approved items to students.
  • Maintain proper records of issued items in the designated software system.
  • Coordinate with the store and billing departments to ensure accurate inventory and billing records.
  • Issue sanitary pads to students when required and maintain confidential records of distribution.

6. Appointment and Calendar Management

  • Schedule appointments and meetings for administrators, counselors, teachers, and other staff.
  • Maintain appointment calendars and coordinate meeting arrangements.
  • Ensure effective utilization of staff schedules and meeting spaces.

7. Confidentiality and Information Security

  • Handle student records, employee records, health information, and confidential documents with utmost discretion.
  • Maintain strict confidentiality and comply with organizational policies regarding data privacy and information security.
  • Ensure sensitive information is disclosed only to authorized personnel.

8. Emergency and Safety Response

  • Follow established school protocols during emergencies, including medical incidents, fire alarms, evacuation procedures, and security situations.
  • Support visitor and student safety by ensuring compliance with school safety procedures.
  • Immediately report emergencies or suspicious activities to the concerned authority.

9. Time Management and Work Coordination

  • Prioritize tasks based on urgency and importance.
  • Manage multiple responsibilities simultaneously while maintaining service quality.
  • Complete assigned tasks within stipulated deadlines.

10. Professional Conduct and Discipline

  • Maintain punctuality and regular attendance.
  • Demonstrate professionalism, courtesy, and ethical conduct at all times.
  • Maintain a clean, organized, and professional reception area.
  • Adhere to all organizational policies, procedures, and code of conduct requirements.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Management, Business Administration, Hospitality Management, or a related field.
  • Minimum one year of experience in Front Desk Operations, Reception, Customer Service, Administration, or a similar role. Candidates with prior experience in the hospitality industry will be given preference.

Salary, Allowances & Perks

Highly attractive and negotiable

Posiiton: Associate Investment Officer

Description

Join Our Mission to Foster Prosperity in Asia

Are you ready to make a difference in the future of Asia and be a catalyst for positive change? We are thrilled to offer a unique opportunity to become an important part of the prestigious Asian Development Bank (ADB). At ADB, our mission is simple yet powerful: to alleviate poverty and promote sustainable development in the Asia-Pacific region.

Organizational Setting and Reporting Relationship
The position of Associate Investment Officer is assigned to the Private Sector Infrastructure Finance Division 1 (PSIF1) within the Private Sector Operations Department (PSOD). You will be based at our Resident Mission Office in Kathmandu, Nepal (NRM).

You will report to the Country Director, NRM and to the designated PSIF1 International Staff and Officer, as applicable.

Your Role

As an Associate Investment Officer, you will provide technical support and coordination in the processing of ADB’s private sector investments and technical assistance in Nepal and other South Asian countries. You will also assist and participate in the identification, development, approval, implementation, and administration of private sector infrastructure financing projects.

You will:

a. Project Processing

  • Provides analytical support in the areas of credit and financial due diligence, review of contracts and project documents, development of financial models and in general assessing the risk and credit profile of proposed infrastructure investments in the form of debt, equity and guarantees.
  • Performs analysis of financial statements and financial projections/models, performs sensitivity analysis and stress testing, in co-ordination with team leaders and external clients.
  • Participates in selected due diligence missions and provides support to Team Leaders in negotiations with project developers, sponsors, and financiers to facilitate the financial closing of transactions and implement projects.
  • Provides logistical support and interacts with external technical advisors, consultants and lawyers representing ADB in specific transactions.
  • Under the guidance of the Team Leaders, undertakes relevant industry and country research, as needed, as part of transaction risk assessment.
  • Assists in drafting initial and final credit memorandum, preparing summaries of project documents, risk assessment analyses and other required documents as may be needed from time to time throughout the project processing cycle.
  • Interfaces as required with internal departments within ADB, including the Office of Risk Management and the Safeguards Specialists, and assist the Team Leader in addressing credit-related and other issues identified by such departments in respect of transactions under processing.
  • Assists in creating, maintaining and updating comprehensive credit files related to transactions under processing.
  • Assists in Investment Committee meetings and other meetings.

b. Project Administration

  • Maintains a database of all projects and financing documents relating to each project. Liaises with the central file keeper to maintain orderly electronically filed documents for each project.
  • Undertakes all activities required to process disbursement requests, including verification of compliance with disbursement conditions and liaison within ADB and with cofinanciers. Verify fee computation.
  • Monitors billing statements of the Controller’s department and payments from the borrowers, and follows up on any delays in payments.
  • Ensures borrowers’ compliance with covenants. Monitors and follows up on the submission of financial statements, technical, insurance, safeguard, and other reports. Review these reports and draw to the attention of dealing officers any perceived issues.
  • Prepares memoranda to obtain management approval for waivers and consents requested by the borrower. Liaises with the Office of the General Counsel, the Office of Risk Management, the technical advisor, and other relevant parties to consider such waivers and requests. Responds to other requests and needs of the borrower.
  • Assists with the preparation of terms of reference (TOR) for the recruitment of consultants for the due diligence.
  • Provides support in identifying a suitable consultant to address the specificities of the country.
  • Provides comments / inputs on draft due diligence reports, bringing to bear PSOD’s experience in operations in India and make appropriate recommendations.

c. Others

  • Assist and participate in the identification and development of projects, undertaking the needed due diligence, risk analysis and mitigation work towards the structuring of loans, equity investment, guarantees or other forms of assistance.
  • Provides inputs for updates on PSOD activities to the Country Director, Nepal Resident Mission.
  • Perform other tasks as assigned and reflected in the workplan.

Qualifications

You will need:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance and/or Accounting, preferably with advanced training.
  • At least 5 years of relevant experience in the field of corporate financing or project finance with a leading international or local financial institution, and/or investment bank.
  • Proven ability to undertake research and analysis on difficult but well-defined tasks, collect and organize required data and information, apply appropriate computer skills in organizing and analyzing the data, and prepare notes, papers, and sections of reports.
  • Good analytical thinking with ability to comprehend causal links, make pros and cons analysis, and analyze factors to reach a decision.
  • Effective report-writing and communication skills in English.
  • Able to guide Administrative Staff to facilitate work planning, delivery and quality review.
  • Able to work collaboratively with teams as a constructive team member.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills.
  • Please refer to the link for ADB Competency Framework for Technical Local grade TL4.

Benefits

ADB offers a rewarding salary and a comprehensive benefits package. The salary will be based on ADB’s standards and comparator markets, taking into account your qualifications and experience.

  • Retirement plan
  • Medical and health benefits
  • Paid leave (including parental)
  • Life and other insurance plans
  • Staff development
  • Additional Information

This appointment is open to internal and external applicants.

This opening is a local staff position. It is open only to nationals and permanent residents of Nepal.

This is a fixed term appointment with option to renew, initially for a period of up to 3 years, or up to the Normal Retirement Date (NRD), whichever comes earlier, for the purpose(s) and conditions determined by ADB, in accordance with Administrative Order No. 2.01 (Recruitment and Appointment). After the initial fixed-term period, ADB may choose to renew the appointment for up to an additional 3 years or not renewed. This decision will be made in the overall interest of ADB, based on factors including but not limited to the requirement of Staff’s particular blend of skills and experience for the medium-term work program of the organizational unit, sufficient funding to cover the renewed period, and Staff’s performance and suitability for employment. There is no limit to the number of renewals up to NRD. However, this appointment is not convertible to a regular appointment.

About Us

ADB is a leading multilateral development bank supporting inclusive, resilient, and sustainable growth across Asia and the Pacific. Working with its members and partners to solve complex challenges together, ADB harnesses innovative financial tools and strategic partnerships to transform lives, build quality infrastructure, and safeguard our planet. Founded in 1966, ADB is headquartered in Manila and owned by 69 members—50 from the region.

ADB only hires nationals of its 69 members.

To view ADB Organizational Chart, please click here.

ADB wants to ensure that everyone is treated with respect and given equal opportunities to work in an inclusive environment. ADB encourages all qualified candidates to apply regardless of their racial, ethnic, religious and cultural background, gender, sexual orientation or disabilities. Women are highly encouraged to apply.

Visit ADB Careers FAQ for more information.

UNICEF works in over 190 countries and territories to save children’s lives, defend their rights, and help them fulfill their potential, from early childhood through adolescence.

At UNICEF, we are committed, passionate, and proud of what we do for as long as we are needed. Promoting the rights of every child is not just a job – it is a calling.

UNICEF is a place where careers are built: we offer our staff diverse opportunities for professional and personal development that will help them reinforce a sense of purpose while serving children and communities across the world. We welcome everyone who wants to belong and grow in a diverse and passionate culture, coupled with an attractive compensation and benefits package.

Visit our website to learn more about what we do at UNICEF.

For every child, the right to Opportunity

UNICEF is the world’s leading organization working globally for children’s rights. Child rights begin with safe shelter, nutrition, protection from disaster and conflict and traverse the life cycle. UNICEF strive to ensure that all children are born alive, stay safe and keep learning.

UNICEF Nepal Country Office is a medium size office that operates across multiple locations in Kathmandu and four field offices based in Janakpur, Surkhet, Bhairahawa and Dangadhi. The office size and structure of over 200 personnel comprising staff, UN Volunteers, and consultants are assigned to work in these different strategic locations to implement programme priorities to deliver results for children. Providing efficient and effective comprehensive operational services, support, innovations including specifically Information, Communication & Technology (ICT) and digital solutions in the different local/office contexts is critical and indispensable.

As part of ongoing organizational realignment following the recent Programme Budget Review (PBR), UNICEF Nepal is seeking a Digital Impact Officer on temporary contract to bridge temporary gap in the ICT unit.

How can you make a difference?

Join our team as the Digital Impact Officer to carry out broad ICT and digital impact functions and enable the provision of ICT and digital solutions and services and assist in the delivery of results in UNICEF Nepal.

You will lead UNICEF Nepal’s ICT function, provide strategic oversight and manage the ICT unit and team in close consultation with the direct supervisor/Deputy Representative Operations. You will collaborate with different programme sections to guide and support Technology for Development (T4D) initiatives that will strengthen programme delivery and create greater impact for children.

Key functions and responsibilities:

  • Manage ICT infrastructure, networks, telecommunications and enterprise technology platforms.
  • Ensure effective ICT service delivery, technical support, system availability and business continuity.
  • Strengthen information security, risk management, system maintenance and compliance.
  • Support the implementation of digital solutions and Technology for Development (T4D) initiatives.
  • Provide leadership, supervision, technical guidance and capacity building for ICT personnel.
  • Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders to advance ICT priorities, innovation and collaboration.
  • If you would like to know more about this position, please review the complete Terms of Reference here: Download File ToR – Didital Impact Officer KTM.pdf

To qualify as a champion for every child you will have…

Minimum requirements:

Education: A university degree (bachelor’s degree or equivalent) in computer science, software engineering, information technology management, business administration, or related social science field is required.
Work Experience: A minimum of one year of professional work experience in information technology management and business operations in a large international organization and/or corporation is required. Work experience in a UN organization is required.
Skills: Hands on experience managing wide-area-networks (WAN) including Meraki networks, knowledge and experience working on and deploying Microsoft Windows platforms and productivity products such as Microsoft Office 365, Sharepoint, Power platforms, Power BI and Intune are a requirement.
Technical knowledge in using and setting up video conferencing systems such as Polycoms is required.
Certification and/or proven experience in at least one of these several ICT technical competencies: ICT Project Management, Business Analytics, Information Security, ICT Audit and Risk Management, Telecommunications, Networks Information Security and Software Engineering and Programming is required.
Language Requirements: Proficiency in English and Nepali is required.

Desirables:

Language: Knowledge of another official UN language (Arabic, Chinese, French, Russian or Spanish) or a local language is an asset.
Experience leading and supervising a team is an asset.
UNICEF experience is an asset.
Relevant experience at country level, particularly in development, fragile settings and humanitarian contexts is desirable.
For every Child, you demonstrate…

UNICEF’s Core Values of Care, Respect, Integrity, Trust and Accountability and Sustainability (CRITAS) underpin everything we do and how we do it. Get acquainted with Our Values Charter: UNICEF Values

(1) Builds and maintains partnerships
(2) Demonstrates self-awareness and ethical awareness
(3) Drive to achieve results for impact
(4) Innovates and embraces change
(5) Manages ambiguity and complexity
(6) Thinks and acts strategically

(8) Nurtures, leads and manages people

UNICEF promotes and advocates for the protection of the rights of every child, everywhere, in everything it does and is mandated to support the realization of the rights of every child, including those most disadvantaged, and our global workforce must reflect the diversity of those children. The UNICEF family is committed to include everyone, irrespective of their race/ethnicity, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, socio-economic background, minority, or any other status.

UNICEF encourages applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of gender, nationality, religious or ethnic backgrounds, and from people with disabilities, including neurodivergence. We offer a wide range of benefits to our staff, including paid parental leave, breastfeeding breaks and reasonable accommodation for persons with disabilities. UNICEF provides reasonable accommodation throughout the recruitment process. If you require any accommodation, please submit your request through the accessibility email button on the UNICEF Careers webpage Accessibility | UNICEF. Should you be shortlisted, please get in touch with the recruiter directly to share further details, enabling us to make the necessary arrangements in advance.

UNICEF does not hire candidates who are married to children (persons under 18). UNICEF has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and UNICEF, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. UNICEF is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children. All selected candidates will, therefore, undergo rigorous reference and background checks, and will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles. Background checks will include the verification of academic credential(s) and employment history. Selected candidates may be required to provide additional information to conduct a background check, and selected candidates with disabilities may be requested to submit supporting documentation in relation to their disability confidentially.

UNICEF appointments are subject to medical clearance.  Issuance of a visa by the host country of the duty station is required for IP positions and will be facilitated by UNICEF. Appointments may also be subject to inoculation (vaccination) requirements, including against SARS-CoV-2 (Covid). Should you be selected for a position with UNICEF, you either must be inoculated as required or receive a medical exemption from the relevant department of the UN. Otherwise, the selection will be canceled.

Remarks:

As per Article 101, paragraph 3, of the Charter of the United Nations, the paramount consideration in the employment of the staff is the necessity of securing the highest standards of efficiency, competence, and integrity.

UNICEF is committed to fostering an inclusive, representative, and welcoming workforce. For this position, eligible and suitable female candidates and candidates from under-represented ethnic groups are encouraged to apply.

Government employees who are considered for employment with UNICEF are normally required to resign from their government positions before taking up an assignment with UNICEF. UNICEF reserves the right to withdraw an offer of appointment, without compensation, if a visa or medical clearance is not obtained, or necessary inoculation requirements are not met, within a reasonable period for any reason.

UNICEF does not charge a processing fee at any stage of its recruitment, selection, and hiring processes (i.e., application stage, interview stage, validation stage, or appointment and training). UNICEF will not ask for applicants’ bank account information.

Humanitarian action is a cross-cutting priority within UNICEF’s Strategic Plan. UNICEF is committed to stay and deliver in humanitarian contexts. Therefore, all staff, at all levels across all functional areas, can be called upon to be deployed to support humanitarian response, contributing to both strengthening resilience of communities and capacity of national authorities.

Visa residence requirements: i.e. UNICEF shall not facilitate the issuance of a visa and working authorization for candidates under consideration for positions at the national officer and general service category.

All UNICEF positions are advertised, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and advance to the next stage of the selection process. An internal candidate performing at the level of the post in the relevant functional area, or an internal/external candidate in the corresponding Talent Group, may be selected, if suitable for the post, without assessment of other candidates.

Additional information about working for UNICEF can be found here.
