Opportunity Village Nepal (OVN) advocates for the rights of women and children and for their economic and social justice. The focus of its programs continues to be on the abolition of child labor, prevention of human trafficking, promotion of girl child education and empowerment of women through various innovative strategies working closely with the communities they live in.

Under its Anti-Human Trafficking Project (Kathmandu, Rupendehi and Kaski ), promote the rights of vulnerable Nepali girls Project (CP-Home) and Vocational Training Centre (Promoting Entrepreneurship among Women and Girls Vocational Skills Development) with a particular focus on internal and cross border trafficking and child protection respectively, OVN invites applications for the following position externally:

Positions: Program Manager
No. of Vacancy: 1
Location: Kathmandu

Qualification

  • Master’s with a minimum 5-years of experience in Social work with a recognized certificate
  • Minimum of two years’ direct experience of direct service provision with vulnerable and or exploited children youth, particularly those who have been victimized by trafficking.
  • Experience in child protection, Child rights, anti-human trafficking, and livelihood programmes and youth participation methodologies and experience of coordinating youth activities.
  • Efficiency in using word, excel, PPT together with other MS packages and internet.
  • Fluency in written and spoken English and Nepali.
  • Experience providing written reports, case study/success story and documentation of project activities.
  • Proven leadership, communication, problem-solving, and people management skills, with the ability to lead diverse teams and foster collaborative working environments.
  • Sound knowledge of safeguarding, gender equality, and social inclusion, and Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability, and Learning (MEAL), with the ability to integrate these principles throughout programme implementation.
  • Ability to build productive partnerships with government agencies, donors, civil society organizations, and community stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities (but not limited hereby)

  • Preparing proposals and strategic plan of OVN in close consultation and coordination with the OVN Director. Ensure legal provisions as of Government of Nepal are met in implementing projects.
  • Preparing all documents for Social Welfare Council Nepal
  • Researching and preparing proposals to seek grant opportunities run by Nepal government, institutional donors (UN bodies or INGOs) on a regular basis.
  • Formulating a tool for MEAL (Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning) for each thematic program and project.
  • Writing timely narrative and financial reports or active grants and preparing progress reports according to the requirements of the donors.
  • Preparing a national advocacy plan for Nepal in collaboration with the relevant coalitions/networks, like minded NGOs and INGOs.
  • Formulating tools/ training modules for TOT on advocacy at various levels.

The above position is initially for 3 months, with the possibility of extension upon satisfactory performance, and the selected candidates are required to begin their job from 1st August 2026. Interested candidates are requested to send an application (including salary expectation) and curriculum vitae with a subject specifying the position applied and send it to the following Email ID: recruitment@ovnnepal.org by 21st July 2026

Child Protection and Safeguarding: OVN recognizes that it has the responsibility to respect and promote gender equality as well as the safety and protection of all children.
OVN has a ZERO-tolerance policy regarding child abuse. OVN is an equal opportunity employer who considers applicants for this position regardless of race, color, religion, creed, gender, age, disability or marital status.

Position: Physiotherapist
Project Location: West Rukum (Chaurjahari municipality), Nepal
Service type: People with disabilities
Type of Contract: Yearly contract for two years
Post Level: Field-level management
Reports to: Project coordinator
Supervisory role: 1-3 staff (Occasional
Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree or higher in Physiotherapy from a recognized institution. Registration with the Nepal Health Professional Council (NHPC) is required
At least two (1) year of relevant professional experience in providing physiotherapy services to persons with disabilities and in rehabilitation programs

Organisation Background:

Human Development and Community Services (HDCS) is a faith-based NGO working in three main sectors of health, education, and community development since 1991. Currently, it manages various projects across Nepal especially in rural regions and provides services through hospitals, health camps, public health programs, disaster preparedness and response, school development programs, teacher training, educational daycare centres for children with special needs, and community radio stations. HDCS is committed to attract and retain the best employees from all ethnicities, gender and backgrounds in its vision to transform the communities.

Purpose:

The Physiotherapist is responsible for assessing the functional needs of persons with disabilities and providing quality facility-based and home-based physiotherapy and rehabilitation services to improve their mobility, independence, participation, and quality of life. The position provides technical guidance, training, coaching, and mentoring to persons with disabilities, caregivers, and family members on rehabilitation, home-based care, assistive device use, positioning, exercise therapy, and home modification. The Physiotherapist facilitates referrals, follow-up care, and multidisciplinary rehabilitation services; collaborates closely with health facilities, local governments, and rehabilitation stakeholders; and promotes the social and economic inclusion of persons with disabilities through community-based rehabilitation (CBR) and appropriate livelihood support.

Responsibilities:

1. Physiotherapy Assessment, Treatment & Rehabilitation Services (40%)

  • Conduct comprehensive functional assessments to identify the rehabilitation needs of persons with disabilities.
  • Develop and implement individualized physiotherapy and rehabilitation plans based on assessment findings.
  • Provide quality facility-based and home-based physiotherapy services using appropriate rehabilitation techniques and interventions.
  • Monitor rehabilitation progress and adjust treatment plans to improve mobility, independence, and quality of life.
  • Promote safe rehabilitation practices and the appropriate use of exercise therapy, positioning, and assistive devices.

2. Case Management, Referral & Technical Support (20%)

  • Facilitate timely referrals and follow-up services to ensure continuity of rehabilitation and healthcare.
  •  Provide technical guidance on home-based rehabilitation, assistive devices, positioning, mobility techniques, and home modifications.
  •  Coordinate with health facilities and rehabilitation professionals to ensure comprehensive rehabilitation services.
  •  Support caregivers and family members with practical skills to continue rehabilitation at home.

3. Community-Based Rehabilitation, Capacity Building & Disability Inclusion (20%)

  • Conduct awareness, education, and behavior change activities on disability-inclusive rehabilitation and healthy practices.
  •  Train and coach social mobilizers, persons with disabilities, caregivers, community members, and local stakeholders on rehabilitation and home-based care.
  •  Promote community participation, disability inclusion, and access to rehabilitation services.
  •  Advocate for the rights, inclusion, and participation of persons with disabilities in community development.

4. Stakeholder Coordination & Partnership (10%)

  •  Collaborate effectively with social mobilizers, Project Coordinator, project staffs, local governments, and multidisciplinary teams.
  •  Participate in case discussions and contribute technical inputs to improve rehabilitation outcomes.
  •  Coordinate with health facilities, rehabilitation centers and hospital for rehabilitation outcomes. • Build and maintain effective partnerships to strengthen rehabilitation service delivery.

5. Documentation, Reporting, Compliance & Safeguarding (10%)

  •  Maintain accurate and up-to-date assessment, treatment, referral, and follow-up records in accordance with organizational standards.
  • Prepare and submit timely reports, progress updates, and project data to the Project Coordinator in compliance with organizational and donor requirements.
  • Document case studies, impact stories, photographs, videos, and key lessons learned to support project learning and knowledge management.
  •  Ensure compliance with organizational policies, safeguarding standards, professional ethics, and quality assurance requirements.
  •  Contribute to project monitoring, organizational learning, and continuous quality improvement through accurate documentation and reporting.

Essential skills:

  •  Clinical Rehabilitation & Disability Inclusion: Sound knowledge of physiotherapy, community-based rehabilitation (CBR), disability inclusion, functional assessment, assistive devices, referral mechanisms, home modifications, and safeguarding.
  •  Communication & Interpersonal Skills: Strong communication, counselling, behavior change, and relationship-building skills with the ability to work effectively with persons with disabilities, families, communities, and stakeholders.
  •  Planning & Problem-Solving: Ability to plan and organize rehabilitation services, manage field activities, solve problems, and adapt to diverse community contexts.
  •  Stakeholder Coordination & Social Inclusion: Ability to work effectively with multidisciplinary teams, health facilities, local governments, and community stakeholders while promoting disability inclusion and cultural sensitivity.
  •  Documentation & Digital Skills: Ability to maintain accurate records of Person with disability, services, prepare reports, and use digital and hard copy both data collection and reporting tools such as KOBO and MS Office.
  •  Collaboration & Teamwork: Ability to work effectively with multidisciplinary teams, HDCS staff, health facilities, and other stakeholders to provide coordinated and holistic rehabilitation services. • Cultural Sensitivity: A physiotherapist should be able to engage with and respect local cultures, particularly when working in remote or diverse communities. Cultural sensitivity ensures that treatment plans and rehabilitation activities are tailored to meet the unique needs of the community.
  •  Driving & Field Mobility: Valid Category “A” driving license with at least one year of driving experience and willingness to undertake frequent field travel and home visits.

Position: Technical Facilitator (Livelihood)
Project Location : West Rukum (Tribine Rural Municipalities), Nepal
Service type : People with disabilities
Type of Contract : Yearly contract for one year
Post Level : Community level field Staff
Reports to : Field Officer/ Project Coordinator
Qualifications : Minimum Intermediate (Grade 12) passed from a recognized institution. JTA/VTA, or equivalent certification in Agriculture, Livestock, or a related field.
Professional Experience : At least two (2) years of relevant professional experience

Organisation Background:

Human Development and Community Services (HDCS) is a faith-based NGO working in three main sectors of health, education, and community development since 1991. Currently, it manages various projects across Nepal especially in rural regions and provides services through hospitals, health camps, public health programs, disaster preparedness and response, school development programs, teacher training, educational daycare centres for children with special needs, and community radio stations. HDCS is committed to attract and retain the best employees from all ethnicities, gender and backgrounds in its vision to transform the communities.

Purpose:

The Social Mobilizer (Livelihoods) will serve as a facilitator, mobilizer, and catalyst for community action by engaging with Agriculture Farmers Groups (AFGs), Livestock Farmers Groups (LFGs), individual farmers, entrepreneurs, and other community structures to promote sustainable agricultural production, entrepreneurship, and income-generating Apply here https://lnk.ink/iRIUC activities. The position will support the effective implementation of livelihood interventions through community mobilization, capacity strengthening, household engagement, facilitation of technical linkages, and coordination with local governments, service providers, project staff, and other stakeholders to enhance household economic empowerment.

Professional Experience

  • Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in community mobilization, livelihoods, agriculture, entrepreneurship, income generation, or community development programmes.
  •  Demonstrated experience working with farmer groups, community groups, women’s groups, cooperatives, local governments, and vulnerable or marginalized populations.
  • Experience in implementing livelihood and entrepreneurship interventions, facilitating group meetings, awareness sessions, household visits, skills development activities, and community mobilization initiatives.
  • Experience in supporting agriculture-based livelihoods, small enterprise development, savings groups, market linkages, or income-generating activities will be an added advantage.
  • Candidates from the project rural municipalities or those with a good understanding of the local language, culture, and socio-economic context will have an added advantage.

Responsibilities:

Professional Experience

  •  Facilitate the formation, revitalization, strengthening, and regular functioning of Agriculture Farmers Groups (AFGs), Livestock Farmers Groups (LFGs), entrepreneurs, and other community livelihood groups in the designated project areas.
  • Serve as a facilitator, mobilizer, and catalyst for community action to promote sustainable agriculture, livestock development, entrepreneurship, and improved livelihood practices among target households and community groups.
  •  Conduct orientation, awareness, and counseling sessions for AFGs, LFGs, farmers, and entrepreneurs on improved agricultural practices, livestock management, entrepreneurship, income-generating activities, and available livelihood opportunities.
  •  Conduct regular household visits, field follow-ups, and community interactions to identify needs, provide motivation, and support the adoption of improved livelihood practices.
  •  Mobilize and encourage the meaningful participation of women, marginalized groups, persons with disabilities, and vulnerable households in livelihood interventions to promote inclusive economic empowerment.
  •  Coordinate closely with project staff, farmer groups, individual farmers, entrepreneurs, local governments, agriculture and livestock service providers, and other stakeholders for effective implementation of project activities.
  •  Promote climate-resilient agriculture, sustainable farming practices, livestock management, and environmentally responsible livelihood approaches within target communities.
  •  Support the identification, registration, and regular updating of information on farmers, farmer groups, entrepreneurs, and project participants.
  •  Facilitate linkages between farmers and entrepreneurs with technical service providers, markets, financial services, and relevant government resources to improve productivity, income, and sustainability.
  • Support the distribution and proper utilization of agricultural inputs, seeds, livestock-related materials, equipment, and other project resources in coordination with the Field Officer.
  •  Support the organization and facilitation of trainings, demonstrations, meetings, workshops, exposure visits, and community events in collaboration with the project team.
  •  Collect and document case studies, success stories, beneficiary feedback, challenges, and lessons learned to contribute to project learning, visibility, and knowledge management.
  •  Maintain accurate field records and prepare timely monthly reports through KOBO and submit progress updates to the Field Officer/Project coordinator.
  •  Support project monitoring, data collection, reflection, and review processes in accordance with project plans and organizational guidelines.
  • Perform other project-related duties assigned by the Field Officer, Project Coordinator, or management team

Essential Skills

  • Communication & Facilitation Skills: Ability to communicate clearly, facilitate community discussions, conduct awareness sessions, and effectively convey messages to diverse groups and individuals.
  • Community Mobilization & Engagement: Ability to mobilize communities, strengthen local groups, encourage participation, and promote community ownership of project activities.
  • Interpersonal & Relationship-Building Skills: Ability to build trust, actively listen, understand community needs, address concerns, and maintain positive relationships with beneficiaries and stakeholders.
  • Planning & Organizational Skills: Ability to organize field activities, coordinate community meetings, manage time effectively, maintain records, and ensure timely completion of assigned tasks.
  • Cultural Sensitivity & Social Inclusion: Ability to respect local culture, values, and practices while promoting meaningful participation of women, marginalized groups, persons with disabilities, and vulnerable households.
  • Behavior Change Communication Skills: Ability to motivate individuals and groups, promote positive practices, and support communities to adopt improved health, nutrition, agriculture, and livelihood behaviors.
  • Problem-Solving & Adaptability: Ability to identify field-level challenges, develop practical solutions, adapt approaches based on local contexts, and work effectively in remote and changing environments.
  • Documentation & Digital Skills: Ability to collect, verify, and maintain accurate field information, prepare reports, document learning, and use digital data collection tools such as KOBO.

Posiiton: Field Rehabilitation Team Leader
Project Location : West Rukum (Chaurjahari municipality), Nepal
Service type : People with disabilities
Type of Contract : Yearly contract for two years
Post Level : Mid-Level
Reports to : Project coordinator
Qualifications : Health Assistant (HA)/PCL Nursing/CMA qualification under CTEVT or equivalent from recognized institution.
Professional Experience : At least three (3) years of relevant professional experience in providing physiotherapy services to persons with disabilities and in rehabilitation programs

Organisation Background:

Human Development and Community Services (HDCS) is a faith-based NGO working in three main sectors of health, education, and community development since 1991. Currently, it manages various projects across Nepal especially in rural regions and provides services through hospitals, health camps, public health programs, disaster preparedness and response, school development programs, teacher training, educational daycare centres for children with special needs, and community radio stations. HDCS is committed to attract and retain the best employees from all ethnicities, gender and backgrounds in its vision to transform the communities.

Purpose:

The purpose of a Field Rehabilitation Team Leader is to provide basic healthcare services, support, and health-related guidance to persons with disabilities and other vulnerable individuals in the community. A Field Rehabilitation Team Leader conducts school health programs, facilitates health awareness and counseling sessions, and supports referral cases by connecting individuals with appropriate health institutions and specialized services when needed. They also Apply here https://lnk.ink/iRIUC assist in assessing the health condition of persons with disabilities, support home modification initiatives to improve accessibility and safety, and help identify and recommend appropriate assistive devices based on individual needs. In addition, Field Rehabilitation Team Leader works closely with individuals, families, and communities to promote better health practices, improve access to healthcare services, and enhance the overall well-being, independence, and quality of life of persons with disabilities.

Responsibilities:

1. Health Assessment, Basic Rehabilitation Support & Case Management (35%)

  • Conduct health and functional assessments of persons with disabilities and identify rehabilitation and support needs.
  • Develop basic health support plans and regularly monitor and reassess individuals’ health status.
  •  Provide basic healthcare guidance, rehabilitation support, and promote safe health and rehabilitation practices.
  •  Identify and facilitate appropriate referrals and ensure timely follow-up for continuity of care. • Support home modification initiatives and recommend appropriate assistive devices based on individual needs.

2. Community Health Promotion & Disability Inclusion (25%)

  •  Conduct school health assessments, awareness sessions, counselling, and community education on health, hygiene, nutrition, and disability-inclusive practices.
  • Promote positive behavior change and community participation to improve health and well-being.
  •  Support community meetings, workshops, and awareness campaigns on health and disability issues.
  • Advocate for improved access to health services and the inclusion of persons with disabilities in communities.

3. Coordination, Stakeholder Engagement & Family Support (20%)

  • Coordinate with the Project Coordinator, Physiotherapist, Social Mobilizers, health facilities, local governments, and other stakeholders to ensure integrated rehabilitation services.
  •  Provide regular reports on disability rehabilitation and project target, activities and outcomes to the Project coordinator.
  • Work closely with persons with disabilities, caregivers, families, and community members to strengthen homebased care and rehabilitation.
  • Build effective partnerships and promote collaboration to improve access to quality rehabilitation and health services.

4. Documentation, Reporting, Compliance & Knowledge Management (20%)

  • Maintain accurate records of assessments, health services, referrals, and rehabilitation progress.
  • Prepare and submit timely reports and project updates to the Project Coordinator in accordance with organizational and donor requirements.
  • Document case studies, impact stories, photographs, videos, and lessons learned to support project learning and knowledge management.
  • Ensure compliance with organizational policies, safeguarding standards, professional ethics, and quality assurance requirements.

Essential skills:

  •  Technical Rehabilitation & Disability Inclusion: Sound knowledge of community-based rehabilitation (CBR), disability inclusion, basic health assessment, assistive devices, referral mechanisms, home modifications, and safeguarding principles to support persons with disabilities and other vulnerable individuals.
  •  Interpersonal & Relationship-Building Skills: Ability to build trust, actively listen, understand community needs, address concerns, and maintain positive relationships with beneficiaries and stakeholders.
  •  Planning & Organizational Skills: Ability to organize field activities, coordinate community meetings, manage time effectively, maintain records, and ensure timely completion of assigned tasks. • Cultural Sensitivity & Social Inclusion: Ability to respect local culture, values, and practices while promoting meaningful participation of women, marginalized groups, persons with disabilities, and vulnerable households.
  •  Behavior Change Communication Skills: Ability to motivate individuals and groups, promote positive practices, and support communities to adopt improved health, nutrition, agriculture, and livelihood behaviors.
  • Problem-Solving & Adaptability: Ability to identify field-level challenges, develop practical solutions, adapt approaches based on local contexts, and work effectively in remote and changing environments.
  •  Documentation & Digital Skills: Ability to collect, verify, and maintain accurate field information, prepare reports, document learning, and use digital data collection tools such as KOBO.
  • Driving license: Must have at least one year plus category “A” driving license.

Posiiton: Field Officer (Livelihood and MNCH)
Project Location : West Rukum (Bafikot and Tribeni Rural Municipalities), Nepal
Service type : Mother and Child Health Integrated with Livelihoods
Type of Contract : Yearly contract for five years
Post Level : Mid-level management
Reports to : Project Coordinator
Supervisory role : 4-5 staff
Qualifications : Bachelor’s degree in agriculture, Public Health, Nursing, or a related field
Professional Experience : Minimum of 3 years of relevant work experience in agriculture, livelihoods, entrepreneurship, maternal, newborn and child health (MNCH), or community development programs

Human Development and Community Services (HDCS) is a faith-based NGO working in three main sectors of health, education, and community development since 1991. Currently, it manages various projects across Nepal especially in rural regions and provides services through hospitals, health camps, public health programs, disaster preparedness and response, school development programs, teacher training, educational daycare centres for children with special needs, and community radio stations. HDCS is committed to attract and retain the best employees from all ethnicities, gender and backgrounds in its vision to transform the communities.

Purpose:

The Field Officer (Livelihood and MNCH) provides technical leadership, technical training, supportive supervision, and field-level coordination to ensure the quality implementation of the SHINE Project in Rukum-west. Reporting to the Project Coordinator, the position strengthens the capacity of Social Mobilizers/Technical Facilitators, local governments, health facilities, community groups, and other stakeholders through technical training, coaching, mentoring, and supportive supervision to improve maternal, newborn and child health (MNCH), nutrition, livelihoods, Apply here https://lnk.ink/iRIUC agriculture, and entrepreneurship outcomes. The Field Officer supports project planning, quality assurance, monitoring, documentation, and reporting while promoting safeguarding, gender equality, disability inclusion (GEDSI), accountability, and community ownership throughout project implementation.

Responsibilities:

1. Technical Leadership, Planning & Quality Implementation (25%)

  • Lead the technical planning, coordination, and quality implementation of MNCH, nutrition, livelihoods, agriculture, livestock, and entrepreneurship interventions in line with the Detailed Implementation Plan (DIP) and project objectives.
  • Provide technical guidance to ensure project interventions are implemented according to organizational standards, government policies, and donor requirements.
  • Support the Project Coordinator in preparing annual, quarterly, and monthly work plans and implementation schedules.
  • Identify implementation gaps, risks, and technical issues, and recommend appropriate corrective actions to improve programme quality.
  • Ensure timely and effective delivery of project activities while maintaining high standards of quality and accountability.

2. Capacity Building, Coaching & Supportive Supervision (20%)

  • Design, organize, and facilitate technical training, workshops, orientations, demonstrations, and learning events for Social Mobilizers/ Technical Facilitators, technical facilitators, local governments, health workers, FCHVs, farmer groups, and other stakeholders.
  • Provide regular coaching, mentoring, and supportive supervision to Social Mobilizers/ Technical Facilitators to strengthen technical knowledge, facilitation skills, and quality service delivery.
  • Develop and adapt technical training materials, facilitation guides, job aids, and learning resources to support effective project implementation.
  •  Conduct regular field monitoring visits and provide constructive feedback to improve staff performance and programme quality.
  • Promote continuous learning, knowledge sharing, and capacity strengthening among project staff and community stakeholders.

3. Community Engagement & Stakeholder Coordination (15%)

  • Strengthen collaboration and maintain effective working relationships with local governments, health facilities, schools, cooperatives, community groups, farmer groups, and other relevant stakeholders.
  • Facilitate transparent beneficiary identification and selection processes in accordance with project criteria and organizational policies.
  •  Promote community participation, ownership, and inclusive engagement in project planning and implementation.
  •  Coordinate multi-sectoral activities with government line agencies and community institutions to enhance programme effectiveness and sustainability.
  • Represent the project in local coordination meetings and technical forums as delegated by the Project Coordinator.

4. Monitoring, Reporting & Knowledge Management (15%)

  •  Monitor project progress against approved plans, indicators, and targets, and recommend corrective actions where necessary.
  •  Ensure timely and accurate collection, verification, analysis, and reporting of project data using KOBO and other organizational systems.
  •  Maintain accurate project records, beneficiary databases, monitoring information, and supporting documentation.
  • Prepare high-quality monthly, quarterly, and annual reports, including case studies, success stories, lessons learned, and best practices.
  • Contribute to organizational learning through documentation, reflection, and knowledge-sharing initiatives.

5. Quality Assurance, Compliance & Technical Support (15%)

  •  Ensure compliance with organizational policies, donor requirements, government regulations, and project implementation guidelines.
  • Promote safeguarding, gender equality, disability inclusion (GEDSI), accountability, and protect
    ion principles across all project activities.
  •  Support the effective management and utilization of project resources, including technical equipment, agricultural inputs, and training materials.
  • Identify implementation challenges, provide practical technical solutions, and ensure timely follow-up to improve programme performance.
  • Support project reviews, monitoring visits, assessments, evaluations, and audit processes as required.

6. Organizational Support & Representation (10%)

  • Support the Project Coordinator in planning, coordination, and implementation of project priorities and organizational initiatives.
  • Foster teamwork, collaboration, and effective communication within the project team and with partner organizations.
  • Maintain professionalism, integrity, confidentiality, and ethical standards in all aspects of work.
  • Represent HDCS professionally in meetings, workshops, and community events when delegated by line manager
  •  Perform other duties and responsibilities assigned by the line manager or management in support of organizational priorities.

Essential Skills & Competencies:

  • Technical Leadership: Demonstrated ability to provide technical guidance and ensure quality implementation of MNCH, nutrition, livelihood, agriculture, livestock, and entrepreneurship interventions.
  • Training, Facilitation & Capacity Building: Proven ability to assess learning needs, develop training materials, facilitate technical training, and strengthen the capacity of project staff, local governments, health workers, community groups, and other stakeholders.
  • Coaching & Supportive Supervision: Strong skills in coaching, mentoring, supportive supervision, performance improvement, and providing constructive feedback to enhance staff performance and programme quality.
  •  Stakeholder Coordination & Partnership: Ability to build collaborative relationships and effectively coordinate with local governments, health facilities, line agencies, community institutions, development partners, and other stakeholders.
  •  Planning & Organizational Skills: Strong planning, coordination, prioritization, and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple activities and meet deadlines.
  •  Cultural Sensitivity: Respect for local cultures and the ability to work effectively with women, children, persons with disabilities, and marginalized communities in Rukum West.
  • Monitoring, Documentation & Reporting: Ability to monitor project performance, ensure data quality, analyse information, prepare technical reports, and maintain accurate documentation using digital platforms such as KOBO and MS Office.
  • Knowledge Management & Learning: Ability to document and share lessons learned, best practices, case studies, and impact stories to support organizational learning.
  • Integrity & Inclusion: Demonstrated commitment to HDCS values, ethical conduct, safeguarding, gender equality, disability inclusion, social inclusion, accountability, and the HDCS Code of Conduct.
  • Communication & Interpersonal Skills: Excellent verbal, written, presentation, facilitation, and relationshipbuilding skills with the ability to communicate effectively with diverse audiences.
  •  Problem Solving & Decision Making: Ability to analyse field-level challenges, make sound technical decisions, recommend practical solutions, and adapt interventions to changing contexts.
  •  Computer & Digital Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), KOBO Collect, email communication, virtual meeting platforms, and other digital tools required for project implementation and reporting.
  •  Driving License: Valid Category “A” motorcycle driving license with a minimum of two years of driving experience.

Position: Store Officer (spares/Allied Parts)
Location: Sunsari, Nepal

We are looking for an experience and proactive Store Manager (Spares/Allied Parts) to lead store operations for our edible oil manufacturing unit, ensuring accurate inventory control, documentation and stock management

Job Specification:

  • Manage end-to end store operations for spares, allied parts and consumables at the manufacturing unit
  • Oversee receipt, inspection, storage and issuance of materials; maintain accurate inventory records
  • Prepare and verity GRNs (Goods Receipt Notes) and ensure proper documentation of all inward and outward movements
  • Coordinate with the purchase team on orders and receipts, follow up on pending orders and verify quantify dues and received materials against purchase orders
  • Conduct periodic stock counts, and coordinate with procurement and maintenance teams to avoid shortages and overstocking
  • Implement and monitor store SOPs, safety standards and internal controls
  • Supervise and guide store SOPs, safety standards and internal controls
  • Supervise and guide store officers and staff; manage material planning and reporting

Job Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Management, or a related field (or equivalent experience)
  • 8+ years of work experience, preferably in a store or inventory management role in a manufacturing environment
  • Hands-on proficiency in Tally, Swastik and MS Excel for inventory management and reporting
  • Strong experience with GRN preparation, stock counts and physical verification
  • Working knowledge of spares and allied parts inventory in an industrial or manufacturing setup
  • Good leadership, organizational, coordination and problem-solving skills

To Apply: Interested candidates are invited to submit their updated CV, along with a cover letter highlighting their relevant experience and qualifications, to pktu.ssu@gmail.com

Please mention “Store Manager” – PKTU (Sunsari) in the subject line of your email

title.rendered: Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability and Learning (MEAL) Coordinator

content.rendered:
Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability and Learning (MEAL) Coordinator ( SAHAYATRA-III) (Maternity Cover)

The Opportunity

Save the Children believes every child deserves a future. In Bhutan and around the world, we give children a healthy start in life, the opportunity to learn and protection from harm. We do whatever it takes for children – every day and in times of crisis – transforming their lives and future we share. Save the Children expects that anyone associated with it abides by its Safeguarding and other Zero Tolerance Policies.

Save the Children is the leading independent organization for children. We save children’s lives; we fight for their rights; we help them fulfill their potential. We work together, with our partners from civil society and government, to inspire breakthroughs in the way the world treats children and to achieve immediate and lasting change in their lives.

The primary responsibility of the post holder is to lead the MEAL component in the SC Norway funded projects to be managed by Field Office-Surkhet. S/he will be responsible for ensuring the global MEAL framework including Knowledge Management systems are operating in different theme of the project. program. Similarly, the position holder will provide technical support to conduct baseline, midterm and final evaluation and periodical indicator survey of the assigned projects. The post holder will also be responsible to manage information/data and update regularly; will be responsible to ensure country as well as SCI global reporting requirements. Similarly, the position holder will be responsible for developing PNGO staff capacity on MEAL functions under the field office. In the event of a major humanitarian emergency, the role holder will be expected to work outside the normal role profile and be able to vary working hours accordingly.

KEY AREAS OF ACCOUNTABILITY:

Program Design:

  • Take lead in conducting situation analysis as per requirements of project.
  • Review project log-frame/result frameworks and provide technical inputs
  • Support to develop and implement MEAL plan of project
  • Ensure project has MEAL plan Baselines and Evaluations:
  • Prepare Terms of Reference (ToR) for baseline, mid-term, final evaluation and operational research of projects.
  • Support to design study including sampling frame, methodology and data collection tools
  • Ensure and support conducting annual indicator surveys of the projects periodically.

Monitoring and Reporting:

  • Support project team in developing/ updating quality benchmarks
  • Conduct periodic program monitoring visits against quality benchmarks and provide feedback to the program team
  • Follow up with project team for implementing agreed actions plan developed after monitoring
  • Provide technical support and guidance to program team in planning, monitoring and reporting
  • Involve with project staff to collect case studies/success stories of different projects
  • Provide technical guidance in conducting annual indicator surveys (including sampling, data analysis and reporting)
  • Develop/review monitoring checklist and tool of project activities.
  • Support to project team for quality reporting, documentation and case story collection.
  • Track, monitor and document monitoring recommendations and ensure timely implementation and closure of agreed action points.

Data/Information Management:

  • Review and support development of OPMIS for project; monitor OPMIS data regularly, ensure quality and timely data updates, and provide technical input to project teams as required
  • Develop and maintain sectoral database on beneficiary coverage (total reach) and share with team on a regular basis (Monthly, quarterly, annually)
  • Review and do preliminary analysis of the data reported by partners and provide timely feedback to the partners team as required.
  • Ensure quarterly, semi-annual, and annual data entry, verification, and updating of project indicator data (outcomes, outputs, and total reach) in PRIME, SWC reporting templates, and other organizational monitoring systems.
  • Lead data cleaning, aggregation and preparation of datasets for donor reporting, evaluations and research activities.

Accountability:

  • Provide inputs in strengthening accountability mechanism in project
  • Support to establish CFM system at district and PNGOs office.
  • Provide regular support to project team in the areas of accountability, e.g. information sharing and feedback/ complaint reporting mechanism .
  • Review monthly accountability report and coordinate with projects team to ensure feedback/complains are responding on timely manner.

Evaluation and Learning:

  • Coordinate in preparing Terms of Reference (ToR) for conducting mid-term and final evaluation of project
  • Lead outcome-level indicator data collection, compilation, quality review, analysis (including disaggregated analysis), interpretation, reporting, and share findings with project teams
  • Coordinate project evaluation and ensure evaluation reports are produced as per agreed standards (SCI quality criteria)
  • Create learning sharing platform at project
  • Identify learning agendas with coordination of program and field office team.
  • Conduct action research in field based on learning agenda
  • Dissemination and sharing of Mid-Term Review (MTR), Endline (EL), evaluation and learning findings with project team, project stakeholders at local, district and provincial levels to promote evidence-based planning and learning .

Coordination, representation and others:

  • Lead MEAL component of SC Norway funded Projects and working closely with the counterpart of SC Norway.
  • Coordinate with MEAL colleagues within filed office and program operation.
  • Ensure PNGOs staff are familiar with SCI MEAL system
  • Provide capacity building support to PNGO MEAL staff
  • Provide MEAL support during emergencies .

Other

  • Supports Save the Children’s dual mandate of development programmes in emergency preparedness and response for children.
  • Support field office on the tasks/ responsibility given by Supervisor in adhoc/need basis as and when required.
  • Represent SC within district, province, national and international forum as per need.

Qualifications and Experience:

  • Master’s degree in information technology, Statistics, Development Studies or related field with minimum 3 years (2 years for GSI group) of experience in monitoring and evaluation and/or research experience preferably at INGOs. Experience of developing MIS with good report writing skill will be an added advantage or Bachelor’s degree with minimum of 5 years of (3 years for GSI group) experience.
  • Strong project/program development and overall project management skills
  • Higher degree of taking initiatives and has ability to work independently.
  • Strong leadership and managerial capability.
  • Proactive and assertive
  • Sound interpersonal skills with ability to work in a multi-cultural environment.
  • Proficiency in writing technical and programmatic reports that document program directions and results
  • Partnership Management (both government and non-government) skills
  • Ability to work in a stressful and changing environment with limited resources
  • Interpersonal communication
  • Program design
  • Coordination
  • Negotiation
  • Reporting writing
  • Analytical skills

Required No.(s): 1 (Nepalese Citizen Only)

Contract length: Short Term

Location: Surkhet Field Office

The Organisation

We employ approximately 25,000 people across the globe and work on the ground in over 100 countries to help children affected by crises, or those that need better healthcare, education and child protection. We also campaign and advocate at the highest levels to realise the right of children and to ensure their voices are heard.

We are working towards three breakthroughs in how the world treats children by 2030:

No child dies from preventable causes before their 5th birthday
All children learn from a quality basic education and that

We know that great people make a great organization, and that our employees play a crucial role in helping us achieve our ambitions for children. We value our people and offer a meaningful and rewarding career, along with a collaborative and inclusive workplace where ambition, creativity, and integrity are highly valued.

Application Information

Please apply using a cover letter and up-to-date CV as a single document. Please also include details of your current remuneration and salary expectations.

We need to keep children safe so our selection process, which includes rigorous background checks, reflects our commitment to the protection of children from abuse.

All employees are expected to carry out their duties in accordance with our global anti-harassment policy.

Save the Children does not charge a fee at any stage of the recruitment process.

About Us

The Organization

We employ approximately 25,000 people across the globe and work on the ground in over 100 countries to help children affected by crises, or those that need better healthcare, education and child protection. We also campaign and advocate at the highest levels to realise the right of children and to ensure their voices are heard. We are working towards three breakthroughs in how the world treats children by 2030:

  • No child dies from preventable causes before their 5th birthday
  •  All children learn from a quality basic education and that,
  •  Violence against children is no longer tolerated

We know that great people make a great organization, and that our employees play a crucial role in helping us achieve our ambitions for children.We value our people and offer a meaningful and rewarding career, along with a collaborative and inclusive workplace where ambition, creativity, and integrity are highly valued.

JOB POSITION: Project Officer
REPORTS TO: Project Coordinator
LOCATION: Kapilvastu

KOSHISH, established in 2008, is a non-governmental organization dedicated to promoting mental health and psychosocial well-being in Nepal. Embracing the spirit of “NOTHING ABOUT US WITHOUT US,” KOSHISH advocates for the inclusion and meaningful participation of individuals with mental health issues and psychosocial disabilities. KOSHISH actively engages in community-based mental health programs, awareness campaigns, and advocacy efforts. Our overarching goal is to mainstream mental health within the broader health system and societal domains, such as social protection, education, inclusion, and employment.

Job Summary

The Project Officer, based in Kapilvastu, is responsible for implementing, coordinating, monitoring, and reporting project activities under the Strengthening Health Systems for the Provision of Mental Health and Psychosocial Support Services in Rural Municipalities of Sudurpaschim and Lumbini Province (Supported by GIZ) project. The role works closely with local governments, health facilities, community stakeholders, and the central project team to ensure the effective delivery of project activities, strengthen partnerships, and achieve project objectives in compliance with donor and organizational standards.

Duration: Initially one year with possible extension based on performance and funding availability.

Key Result Areas and Responsibilities

Project Management:

  • Lead implementation of project activities as focal person for assigned municipality.
  • Develop annual and periodic plans with team and Project Coordinator.
  • Conduct review, monitoring, and reporting of progress against project indicators.
  • Capture learning and document best practices.
  • Work with Admin/Finance Officer to ensure program-finance linkage.
  • Facilitate partner and donor visits.

Coordination, Collaboration, and Advocacy:

  • Build collaboration with local governments, health facilities, CBOs, and organizations of persons with disabilities.
  • Advocate for mainstreaming rights and services in local government plans.
  • Support provincial advocacy with Project Coordinator and Program Manager.
  • Represent KOSHISH in local forums with approval from Project Coordinator.
  • Documentation, Database Management and Reporting:
  • Maintain detailed project documentation and databases.
  • Submit timely reports to Project Coordinator.
  • Develop success stories and capture learning and beneficiary data.
  • Ensure donor-compliant and timely reporting.

Safety & Security

  • Ensure safety of project team during field implementation.
  • Identify and manage potential risks in collaboration with central office.

Safeguarding:

  • Promote safeguarding practices within all project activities.
  • Ensure compliance with all safeguarding and prevention of sexual exploitation, abuse and harassment (PSEAH) policies.

Personal Competencies

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Good planning and organizational ability.
  • Proactive, adaptable, and accountable.
  • Team player with strong coordination skills.
  • Commitment to integrity, inclusion, and safeguarding.

Technical Competencies

  • Knowledge of project implementation, monitoring, and reporting.
  • Stakeholder coordination and partnership management.
  • Report writing and documentation skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Understanding of MHPSS, safeguarding, and donor compliance is an advantage.

Applying Procedure:

Interested candidates are requested to fill the application form- Click Here

We will be reviewing the applications on a Rolling Basis, however, the last date to submit your application is 23rd July 2026.

Note:

Eligible candidates with disabilities, women and from the local area are encouraged to apply. We are committed to ensuring the safety, dignity, and well-being of all individuals and maintains zero tolerance for sexual exploitation, abuse, harassment, or any other form of harm. All shortlisted candidates will be required to comply with KOSHISH’s Safeguarding Policy and Code of Conduct and will undergo appropriate safeguarding checks KOSHISH reserves the right to accept or reject any or all applications without assigning any reason.

Position: Communications Officer – HICAS
Location: Kathmandu

Background

The Communications Officer is being hired under the Integrated Climate Adaptation Solutions for the Hindu Kush Himalaya Region (HI-CAS) project at ICIMOD, funded by Global Affairs Canada (GAC) – Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development (DFATD). The project aims to improve climate-resilient, nature-positive livelihoods while strengthening the leadership and agency of rural women and Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs) in Bangladesh, Bhutan, and Nepal.
The project is housed under Strategy Group Resilient Economies and Landscapes which focuses on delivering environmentally sustainable, but economically viable and socially inclusive solutions for some of the world’s most marginalised and vulnerable people. With a focus at both local and landscape levels, it builds understanding of the implications of rapid socio-economic, climate and environmental changes on mountain livelihoods and economies that are challenged by their remote, fragile, and hazard-prone settings. The project itself will be implemented through the adoption of gender-responsive and locally tailored climate adaptation solutions – grounded in feminist and human rights-based methodologies – that place the rights, agency, and leadership of IPLCs at the centre. These evidence-informed Integrated Adaptation Solution Packages (IASPs) will combine springshed management, renewable energy technologies to power irrigation systems, and agrobiodiversity practices. They will be complemented by capacity building and knowledge sharing to drive institutional policy and action. These initiatives will also help to attract sustainable investments in climate action and development – particularly in the water, energy, and agrobiodiversity sectors – with a strong focus on women’s and IPLCs’ leadership.
The HI-CAS project will be implemented in two upazilas (sub-districts) in Bandarban and Khagrachari districts in the Chittagong Hill Tracts of Bangladesh; two gewogs (blocks) in Paro and Punakha dzongkhags in Bhutan; and two municipalities, Roshi and Dhankuta, in Nepal. Over its four-year duration, the project aims to benefit approximately 40,000 IPLCs and other vulnerable groups by reducing their exposure and sensitivity to climate change impacts while fostering ecosystem health and climate mitigation outcomes.

Position Overview

The Communications Officer will be responsible for boosting awareness of the outcomes and impact of the HI-CAS project by supporting the documentation and communication of the project results in line with the commitments ICIMOD has made in the HI-CAS Project Performance Measurement Framework, which is nested within our Strategy 2030: Moving Mountains and our Medium-Term Action Plan V (2023-2026):Embracing Change and Accelerating Impact and related Results Framework 2023-2026. It will also contribute to the wider communications efforts of ICIMOD’s Strategic Group: Resilient Economies and Landscapes, where the position sits.

ICIMOD’s Communications Officers are creative, result-driven and motivated professionals who are passionate about the power of amplifying impact and driving policy and practice change through strategic communications, influencing and engagement. Working under the direct supervision of the Senior Project Coordinator HI-CAS, and co-supervised by the Head of Communications, the post holder will work closely with communication staff in the Strategic Group (and its respective Action Areas and Interventions) teams, and with the ICIMOD central Communications Unit, to deliver communication and knowledge products for the HI-CAS project. The role holder will ensure communications facilitate access to knowledge and the ability for ICIMOD to use evidence-based advocacy and influencing messaging in policy dialogues. All efforts will be guided by a strong GESI commitment, rooted in a feminist approach that challenges power imbalances, promotes women’s leadership, and centres the rights, voices, and knowledge of IPLCs.
You will be focused on working directly with communities, partners, and ICIMOD staff to tell the story of HI-CAS through a variety of media. You will be tasked with amplifying community experiences and evidence generated by the project to inform wider advocacy efforts and drive the uptake of approaches used in the project. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who values delivering impact through written, oral, and visual communication and engagement approaches and methods, is passionate and experienced in feminist solutions for mountains and people, and is committed to helping build a greener, more inclusive, climate-resilient Hindu Kush Himalaya as ICIMOD moves into its next Strategy 2030 period. It will require very close teamwork and strong collaboration skills. The ideal candidate should have knowledge of feminist methodologies, GESI-responsive communication strategies, and experience working with IPLCs.

Requirements

Person Specification/Competencies

Essential

  • Master’s degree in journalism, marketing, research for development approaches, communications, or other relevant field with a minimum of 3 years of experience in a similar Communications role or bachelor’s degree in combination with four years of qualifying experience may be accepted in lieu of a master’s degree.
  •  Understanding and experience of GESI-related issues and activities and how these can be effectively communicated. Awareness of indigenous people-centric communication methods and approaches.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.
  • Demonstrated ability to produce and edit various forms of content.
  • Proficiency in using digital and social media platforms.
  • Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
  •  Proficiency in MS Office Suite, content management systems, and basic graphic design software.

PREFERRED

  •  Knowledge of the languages and cultures of the HKH region
  • Experience working on multi-cultural, multi-country communication tasks
  • Excellent interpersonal and time-management skills

Benefits

Remuneration

This is an international position at ICIMOD. The starting annual gross salary for this position is USD 21,419/- (negotiable based on experience and qualifications). Gross salary comprises basic salary, provident fund, family/post adjustment allowance, and child/dependency allowances. Salaries and benefits at ICIMOD are competitive compared with other international organisations. We offer a comprehensive benefits package which includes insurances (medical, life and accidental), children’s education grant for a maximum of two children below 18 years of age, severance pay, paid leave (30 holidays and 10 public holidays per year) and a daycare facility.

For expatriates, there is a tax exemption in Nepal; they are responsible for their home country’s tax payments. Expatriate staff are entitled to housing allowance, annual home leave ticket, shipment of personal effects, and an installation and repatriation allowance.

Method of application

Applicants are requested to apply online before August 04, 2026 (11:59 PM Nepal Standard Time) through .

International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development -Current Openings
Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Detailed TOR Link: ToR Communications Officer HI-CAS july2026.pdf

Terre des hommes (Tdh), headquartered in Lausanne with an office in Zurich, is the largest Swiss children’s rights organization, with over 2,000 staff members worldwide. We are committed to working alongside children to guarantee their rights, protect their lives, and improve their well-being. We achieve this through our innovative programs in the areas of health, migration, and access to justice, designed to have a positive and lasting impact.

Founded in 1960 and active in Nepal since 1985, Tdh has implemented impactful interventions in Maternal and Child Health, Migration, and WASH in Institutions, with a strong focus on Child Protection. All initiatives are carried out in close collaboration with government bodies and civil society organizations to ensure sustainable and child-centered outcomes.

Position: Resource Mobilization Coordinator

Expected Start date: 1st week of September 2026

Duration: 1 year and possible extension

Activity rate: 100%

Workplace: Kathmandu Nepal

Application deadline: 28th July 2026

Overview

The Resource Mobilisation Coordinator is responsible for leading and coordinating strategic fundraising, donor engagement, partnership development, and business development initiatives for the Tdh Nepal Country Office. The position supports sustainable portfolio growth through proactive donor intelligence, strategic positioning, proposal coordination, and development of high-quality technical and financial submissions aligned with organizational priorities and evolving donor expectations. The role plays a central coordination function in identifying funding opportunities, strengthening donor relationships, supporting consortium development, and ensuring competitive positioning of the organization within the humanitarian and development funding landscape.

The position is responsible for coordinating the preparation of both technical and financial proposals in close collaboration with programme, finance, MEAL, safeguarding, and operations teams, ensuring that all submissions fully comply with donor templates, eligibility requirements, compliance standards, and submission guidelines. The role also contributes to strengthening organizational systems for fundraising, donor intelligence, costing frameworks, knowledge management, and strategic partnerships while promoting innovation, localization, climate resilience, accountability, and safeguarding within proposal development processes.

MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES

Strategic Resource Mobilisation & Donor Intelligence

  •  Lead implementation and periodic revision of the Country Fundraising Strategy.
  • Conduct proactive donor intelligence and horizon scanning to identify institutional, foundation, bilateral, multilateral, corporate, and innovative financing opportunities.
  • Track emerging donor trends, thematic priorities, and funding mechanisms relevant to Nepal and the region.
  • Analyze donor strategies, funding histories, and competitive landscapes to support strategic positioning.
  • Support pre-positioning efforts with donors and strategic partners prior to major call announcements.

Proposal Development and Coordination

  •  In coordination with program and regional PREX and Fundraising Advisor, provide support to prepare quality and compelling concept notes and full project proposals and ensure their timely submission in the donor’s prescribed templates.
  • Coordinate end-to-end proposal development processes including concept notes, expressions of interest, and full proposals.
  • Lead the development and implementation of proposal retro plan in coordination with program, MEAL, finance, operations, and PREX and regional fundraising advisor to ensure effective cross-functional collaboration and timely delivery of submissions.
  •  Ensure donor compliance requirements, eligibility criteria, and submission standards are fully met.
  • Ensure effective management of proposal archives, donor documentation, and institutional knowledge repositories through SharePoint to strengthen knowledge retention, accessibility, compliance, and cross-functional collaboration.

Partnership & Consortium Development

  •  Identify and cultivate strategic partnerships with NGOs, INGOs, UN agencies, academic institutions, private sector actors, networks, and consortium partners.
  •  Support consortium-building initiatives and partnership negotiations.
  • Coordinate due diligence documentation and partnership compliance requirements such as Teaming Up agreement, MoU, Letter of Support in coordination with the Organization Development Manager and Program Teams.
  • Contribute to equitable partnership approaches aligned with localization principles.
  • Represent the organization in donor meetings, partner forums, and coordination platforms as delegated.

Donor Relationship Management

  •  Maintain positive & professional relationships with existing & prospective donors.
  •  Coordinate donor communications related to funding opportunities/submissions.
  • Support donor engagement meetings, presentations, capacity statements, and strategic positioning materials.
  • Ensure timely responses to donor requests and clarification queries.
  • Contribute to donor visibility and stewardship strategies in coordination with communications officer.
  • Knowledge Management, System & Innovation
  •  Ensure accurate maintenance of grants follow-up tool and funding opportunity systems in Engage360 and other internal platforms.
  • Strengthen institutional knowledge management for fundraising and proposal development.
  • Actively participate in Fundraising Communities of Practice and other resource mobilization learning platforms organized by Headquarters and regional teams and contribute to organizational learning by sharing country office experiences, good practices, lessons learned, donor intelligence, and innovative fundraising approaches.

Desired profile of the Candidate

Qualifications & Experience:

  • Minimum Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., MBA, political science, international relations, etc.) from a recognised institution.
  • Training in gender, project management and leadership is an advantage
  • At least 7 years’ experience in the field of International Fund Raising
  • Solid understanding and experience of child rights with at least 5 years’ experience in developing project proposals, managing programmes and developing expertise and knowledge;
  •  Experience coordinating multi-stakeholder proposal development processes and consortium submissions.
  • Strong understanding of international donor compliance frameworks and grant acquisition processes.
  • Expertise in advocacy techniques and strategies is an advantage;
  • Ideally, the candidate will have already carried out similar work in an organisation working at the international level.
  • Experience in both humanitarian and development programming contexts desirable.
  • Knowledge of Nepal’s development and international donor landscape strongly preferred.

Languages

Excellent English speaking and writing skills (English native speaker not essential)

IT Knowledges

Ability to operate Microsoft Office 365 suite: Word, Excel & Power Point, SharePoint.

Tdh General Code of Conduct and Ethical Policies :

  • Commits to promoting and complying with the Global Code of Conduct, systematically reporting any violations of the Code through Tdh’s formal reporting mechanisms and dealing with any violations of procedures and processes appropriately.
  • Raises awareness of the Foundation of the risks of policy non-compliance, violence and abuse, and related rights, towards children, community members and our own employees.
  • Undertakes to comply with key ethical policies and guidelines, including the Safeguarding Policy, the Anti-Fraud and Corruption Policy, the Conflict-of-Interest Policy and other policies related to the prevention of terrorist financing and criminal activities, as well as workplace behavior.
  • Commits to putting values and principles into practice through exemplary behavior, in full compliance with the Code of Conduct, and always respecting established procedures and processes.
  • Commits to developing a compliant and enlightened management culture within the organization and in our work with children and the communities in which we operate.

Procedure:

Only complete applications (CV + covering letter) received through this link will be considered and processed. Additional documents (diplomas, work certificates, etc.) will be requested at a later date. We will only contact those candidates selected for further steps of the recruitment.

References :

Please note that all job offers will be subject to satisfactory references and checks (criminal record and anti-terrorism funding check). Terre des hommes will ask the Human Resources of previous organisations to provide references on any findings of sexual exploitation, sexual abuse and/or sexual harassment as well as fraud and corruption, during employment, or incidents under investigation at the time the candidate left employment.

Position: Finance and Admin Coordinator

The Why: NEPAL aspires to graduate from least developing country to a lower middle-income country by 2026. However, the development community’s excessive focus today on socio-political empowerment has not yielded the desired economic transformation. In reaching the 2026 national target, shall we wait for institutions of power to become accountably productive or shall we bolster innovations of people to bring inclusive prosperity? And, shall we strengthen grassroots-government collaboration in shaping an equal and enterprising future?

The How: DAAYITWA, with a focus on economic empowerment, envisions an enterprising Nepal where every youth has opportunities for a prosperous future. Daayitwa is a part of the Daayitwa Abhiyaan (DA) which consists of four organizations: Daayitwa, Governance Lab, Nepal Leadership Academy based in Nepal and Nepal Rising (Daayitwa-US) based in the U.S. Inspired by shared values of innovation, collaboration, and service, it promotes the socio- economic development of Nepal. The movement fosters inclusive and equitable economic growth by accelerating grassroots innovations, strengthening government institutions, and revitalizing a virtuous cycle of democracy and development through grassroots-government collaboration.

Our focus Areas:

1. Enterprise-led employment creation

2. Design evidence-based policies for improved decision-making

3. Build state capability for effective policy implementation

4. Foster stakeholder collaboration for governance innovations

Finance and Administration Coordinator will play a key role in ensuring effective financial management and administrative operations across Daayitwa Abhiyaan (DA) and its initiatives.Daayitwa, Governance Lab, Nepal Leadership Academy, and Nepal Rising. Working directly with the Executive Director and Program Director across DA, the Finance and Administrative Coordinator will provide strategic leadership in day-to-day finance and administration functions for multiple programs, coordinating closely with program and field teams, and supporting compliance, reporting, and organisational systems.

Key Responsibilities

A. Financial Management

  •  Maintain accurate financial records, including cash book, ledger, subsidiary ledger, and fixed asset register
  • Prepare financial reports and supporting documents as required by management and donors in a timely and systematic manner
  •  Consolidate financial data and produce donor-mandated reports
  • Prepare annual budgets and financial forecasts
  •  Provide support in budgeting for project proposals
  • Manage accounts payable, including payroll processing and timely payments
  •  Set up and manage a petty cash system
  • Conduct monthly bank reconciliation and report discrepancies
  •  Maintain proper records of funds received, spent, and balances

B. Accounting and Audit

  •  Perform monthly bookkeeping tasks
  •  Coordinate with external accountants and auditors for audits and financial reporting
  •  Ensure timely completion of organizational and project audits
  •  Support preparation of financial reports for the management.

C. Administrative Management

  •  Oversee general office administration, including procurement, inventory, and record management
  • Maintain updated office inventory and asset registers; guide field offices to do the same
  •  Manage administrative processes such as events, logistics, banking transactions, and payroll support
  •  Administer annual maintenance contracts and office systems

D. Procurement and Contract Management

  •  Manage procurement processes in line with organizational policies
  •  Negotiate, finalize, and administer contracts and purchase orders with vendors
  •  Monitor procurement compliance across the organization

E. Compliance and Coordination

  •  Ensure compliance with government regulations, including tax and social security requirements
  • Liaise with government departments, banks, auditors, tax agents, and legal advisors
  •  Ensure all employees are registered with relevant authorities and statutory deductions are made
  •  Support program teams in complying with donor contractual and financial guidelines
  •  Monitor budget vs. expenditure and coordinate with program staff on variances

F. Reporting and Systems

  •  Develop and maintain a monthly financial reporting mechanism
  •  Provide regular financial updates to management and stakeholders
  • Ensure adherence to organizational policies, procedures, and internal controls
  •  Recommend improvements to financial and administrative systems

G. Other Duties

  • Perform any other tasks assigned by the Line Manager.

Qualifications

  •  Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field. At least 4-5 years of relevant work experience is preferred.
  •  Demonstrates high standards of ethics, integrity, and professionalism.
  •  Ability to work independently on complex tasks, exercising creativity, judgment, and problem-solving skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and familiarity with accounting software.
  •  Practical knowledge of financial management, accounting principles, preparing financial statements, and following Nepalese tax policies.
  •  Strong numerical and analytical skills with meticulous attention to detail.
  • Proactive, practical, and solution-oriented approach to challenges and conflicts.
  • Team player with the ability to communicate openly and collaborate across teams and field offices. Strong organizational and time-management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines under pressure.
  • Resilient and capable of making informed decisions in uncertain or challenging situations.
  • Eagerness to learn, share knowledge, and foster a learning environment.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills in Nepali and English.
  • Strong client orientation, diplomacy, and results-driven mindset.
  • Understanding of social and economic development issues, including income generation and community-driven initiatives.

Additional information

Compensation: Daayitwa will provide compensation on a monthly rate basis. The rate will be mutually agreed upon, commensurate with the level of the required deliverables, the qualifications and experience of the candidate.

If you’re keen on this position, kindly complete the form below by 29th July 2026. To access the page, please click on the provided link and right-click to go to the link.

https://forms.gle/VfA3RyVcY5Uk2RTdA

In the subject line, put “Job Title followed by your first and last name (e.g., “Finance and Admin Coordinator– Sabina Nepali)” and attach the following documents. All documents must be compiled in a single file, in the same order and saved in PDF format:

  • Cover letter (one page max)
  • CV
  •  Academic transcripts/certificate for most recent qualification

Deadline: 29th July 2026. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. The vacancy may be closed prior to the deadline if a suitable candidate is found. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Daayitwa is committed to promoting workforce diversity and actively encourages applications from individuals belonging to marginalized and discriminated groups, including women, Dalit, Janajati, Madhesi/Terai, and other minority communities. The organization values inclusive representation and strives to create a diverse and equitable working environment.
