Posiiton: Customer Relationship Specialist
Location: Lalitpur

At Alaya, we pride ourselves on our guiding values of Care, Passion, and Oneness. We offer tailored solutions to our clients, helping them achieve their financial goals with confidence. As a member of our team, you will collaborate with like-minded individuals, celebrate your unique talents, and forge lifelong connections. Our dedication to excellence has earned us numerous awards, including the 2023 HRD Asia 5-Star Employers of Choice Award.

About The Role:

The Customer Relationship Specialist plays an important role in maintaining strong relationships with clients after the settlement of their home loans. This role is focused on providing excellent post-settlement support, proactively communicating with clients, resolving queries, identifying opportunities for loan reviews, and supporting client retention.

You will work closely with clients, brokers, lenders, and internal teams to ensure clients continue to receive the right support after their loan has settled. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys customer service, relationship building, problem-solving, and working in a fast-paced environment where proactive communication and attention to detail matter.

What You Will Be Doing:

  • Client Relationship Management: You will build rapport with clients after loan settlement, regularly touch base with them, and act as a trusted point of contact for their post-settlement needs.
  • Post-Settlement Support: You will assist clients with loan-related queries, progress payments, fixed-rate expiry, loan feature changes, and other after-settlement concerns.
  • Loan Review and Retention: You will monitor client loans, review and re-price loans on a semi-annual basis, negotiate interest rates with banks, and support client retention by identifying refinancing or repeat business opportunities.
  • Proactive Communication: You will ensure clients are updated regularly and do not need to chase for updates. You will also make sure emails and client queries are responded to within the expected timeframe.
  • Lead Generation and Business Support: You will identify opportunities for new business, referrals, product reviews, and repeat business while supporting brokers with relevant client information.
  • Team Collaboration: You will work closely with team members, cross-train where needed, and support the team in completing tasks accurately and on time.

About You:

At Alaya, we hire based on our Guiding principles:

  • You enjoy building strong relationships and providing helpful, timely, and professional support to clients.
  • You take ownership of your work and understand the importance of accuracy, follow-up, and proactive communication.
  • You are solution-focused and can remain calm and professional while handling client queries, objections, or complex situations.
  • You are eager to learn, open to feedback, and willing to understand loan-related processes in depth.
  • You work well with others and believe in supporting the team, sharing knowledge, and contributing to collective success.
  • You are comfortable working under pressure and can manage multiple tasks while maintaining a high level of service quality.
  • If you prefer to only respond when asked or wait for instructions before taking action, this role may not be the right fit.

What We Are Looking For:

  • Qualification: Bachelor’s degree required.
  • Experience: Experience in customer service, client communication, administrative support, or a similar role would be preferred.
  • Communication Skills: Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to connect with clients professionally through phone calls and emails.
  • Customer Focus: A genuine interest in helping clients, understanding their needs, and providing a positive customer experience.
  • Attention to Detail: Ability to manage client information, loan details, emails, and follow-ups with accuracy and care.
  • Problem-Solving Skills: Ability to identify issues, manage objections, escalate red flags when needed, and focus on practical solutions.
  • Tech Readiness: Experience using Gmail and Google Workspace tools such as Google Drive, Docs, and Sheets.
  • Work Style: Able to work with minimum supervision, manage pressure, stay organised, and complete tasks within expected timelines.
  • Mindset: A great attitude, strong desire to learn, accountability, dependability, and willingness to grow within a collaborative team.

Why Alaya?

At Alaya, our team is our most valuable asset. We are passionate about creating a work environment where care for one another, passion for excellence, and a sense of oneness drive everything we do. Here’s what you can expect:

  • Growth Opportunities: We are committed to your personal and professional development.
  • Inclusive Culture: Be part of a team where your ideas and contributions are valued, and where you can make a meaningful impact.
  • Meaningful Work: Play an important role in supporting clients beyond loan settlement and helping them continue their financial journey with confidence.
  • Oh also, did we mention, we only work 5 days a week? Mon-Fri.

Besides the list of benefits that the Labour Law mandates, we also offer:

  • Social Security Fund (SSF)
  • Exclusive leaves and bonuses
  • Festival, profit, and book reading bonus
  • Team building activities and social events
  • Accident and medical insurance coverage
  • International working environment exposure
  • Continuous learning and development opportunities
  • Customer Referral – Refer your friends and relatives in Australia to use our services and we’ll reward you!
  • Work hours: 6:00 am to 2:00 pm, including a 1-hour lunch break.

Ready to Join Us?

If you’re passionate about client service, enjoy building long-term relationships, and are eager to grow with a company that values your contribution, we’d love to hear from you.

Disclaimer:

By submitting your job application, you are consenting to the retention of your personal data in our database for recruitment purposes. Your data will be held securely and will only be accessible to authorized personnel.

Posiiton: Data Entry Support – Contractual (1- year)
Location: Lalitpur

About Us:

At Alaya, we pride ourselves on our guiding values of Care, Passion, and Oneness. We take pride in offering tailored solutions to our clients, helping them achieve their financial goals with confidence. As a member of our team, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with like-minded individuals, celebrate your unique talents, and forge lifelong connections.

Our dedication to employee well-being and success has earned us numerous awards, including the 2023 HRD Asia 5-Star Employers of Choice Award, the 2021 Liberty Australian Brokerage of the Year Award and the Best Community Engagement Program. 2024.

About The Role:

As a Data Entry Support (1 year contract), you will work with a group of self-sustained people in a fast-paced environment. You will spend your first week in training in a fully supported learning environment. During this time, you will be fully trained in completing all end-to-end processes that are required for the smooth processing of loan applications for our partners in Australia. Your responsibility would be to liaise with the brokers’ support team, and other stakeholders for processing the home loan applications of our Australian clients. You will be required to order and track the valuation for the client’s property as well as complete all data entry tasks as per the business partner’s requirement.

About You:

You thrive in a steady, supportive environment, where attention to detail and cooperation are valued. You take accountability and demonstrate a sense of ownership in your work, ensuring meticulous accuracy and quality in every task. While you appreciate fun moments that make the workplace enjoyable, you remain focused and committed to achieving outcomes—not just completing tasks.

You don’t settle for “good enough” and take initiative when something needs to be improved—even if it’s not assigned to you. You are adaptable, resilient, and approach challenges with a calm and positive mindset.

At Alaya, we hire based on our Northstar principles—Never Settler, Owner Mindset, Relentless Learner, Together First, and Honestly Courageous. You naturally demonstrate these through how you work and collaborate.

You are also open to learning and using AI tools to improve efficiency and accuracy. You speak up respectfully when needed, support your team, and act with integrity at all times.

If you prefer to only follow instructions or do the minimum required, this role may not be the right fit.

If you’re seeking a role where your steady pace, supportive nature, and high standards are valued, join us in our commitment to excellence.

Major Deliverables:

  • Efficiently input and validate data in CRM software and online platforms.
  • Facilitate property valuation and pricing requests for clients.
  • Coordinate and process home loan applications for partners in Australia.
  • Stay updated with industry changes and compliance requirements.
  • Actively participate in training programs and team discussions.
  • Liaise with brokers and stakeholders to ensure smooth operations and service delivery.

Requirements

Apply if you have:

  • Bachelor’s degree with an internship or volunteer experience (preferred).
  • Strong Attention to detail – as we are selling dreams and crunching numbers, we must be extra precise and have an eagle eye for details.
  • Data Entry skill – it may sound like you simply input in the computer but it serves a larger purpose of preventing huge losses. You’ve just saved a day!
  • Sound verbal and written English communication skills
  • Solution-oriented mindset and ability to work with minimal supervision
  • Resilience and consistency in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong time management and organizational skills
  • Strong work ethics and accountabilityBenefits

Why should you join us?

At Alaya we love the work we do and the colleagues we work with. We offer great growth and amazing career advancement opportunities for our employees. While we operate in a high-paced environment, we ensure a healthy work-life balance through flexible practices and a supportive culture.

We also believe in celebrating our wins—big or small—with team activities, lunches, and engagement initiatives. And yes, we work only 5 days a week!

What we offer:

Besides the list of benefits that the Labour Law mandates, we also offer;

  • Salary + Social Security Fund (SSF)
  • 5-day work week (Mon to Fri)
  • Exclusive paid leaves
  • Conducive and Fun working environment –
  • Festival, profit, and book reading bonus
  • Accident and medical insurance coverage

When you step into the role, you will find your niche in our welcoming work environment. Develop your skills, chart your career growth, and make a meaningful impact on clients’ financial journeys.

Plus, take advantage of our Customer Referral program to earn rewards by recommending our services to friends and relatives in Australia.

Work hours: 6:00 am to 2:00 pm (non-negotiable) including a 1-hour lunch break

Our office is open, so if you’re selected, we’d be happy to work with you in person!

Disclaimer: By submitting your job application, you are consenting to the retention of your data in our database for recruitment purposes. Your data will be held securely and will only be accessible to authorized personnel.

Ready to Apply?

If you’re looking for a role where your attention to detail and consistency can make a real impact, we’d love to hear from you.

Join us and be part of a team that values growth, collaboration, and excellence.

We are seeking dedicated and experienced professionals to join our team in Pokhara for the position of Fitter / Mechanical Assistant.

Location: Pokhara, Nepal
Qualification: Diploma / Pre-Diploma in Mechanical Engineering

Experience Required:

  • Diploma Holders: Minimum 2–3 years of relevant experience
  • Pre-Diploma Holders: Minimum 4–5 years of relevant experience
  • Salary: Negotiable

Key Responsibilities

  •  Perform mechanical fitting, maintenance, and repair activities.
  • Diagnose and troubleshoot equipment issues.
  •  Ensure compliance with safety and quality standards.
  •  Support smooth operation and preventive maintenance activities.

Requirements

Posiiton: Senior HR Associate (Nepalese Nationals Only)
Location: Kathmandu

Position Overview

Under the guidance of the Head of HROD, the position is responsible for managing end-to-end HR administration functions related to appointments, contracting, onboarding, employee records, payroll coordination, staff benefits, insurance, HR reporting, and separation processes for staff and other personnel. The role ensures timely and compliant contract administration, maintenance of accurate HR records, efficient onboarding and employee services, and effective coordination with Finance and other units on payroll, benefits, and workforce matters. The position also supports HR process improvement and digitalisation initiatives, contributes to workforce analytics and reporting, and upholds high standards of professionalism, confidentiality, service delivery, and compliance in all HR operations.

Requirements

Person Specification/Competencies

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Management, or a related field; a Master’s degree will be an advantage.
  • Minimum 5 years of progressively responsible experience in HR administration, operations, or personnel management, preferably in an international or multicultural organisation.
  • Strong knowledge of HR administrative processes, employment contracts, onboarding, personnel records management, payroll coordination, staff benefits, and HR compliance.
  • Demonstrated experience working with ERP systems and digital HR platforms, with strong attention to data accuracy and process efficiency.
  • Experience in maintaining confidential HR records and handling sensitive employee information with professionalism and discretion.
  • Strong organisational and coordination skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
  • Excellent analytical, reporting, and documentation skills, including preparation of HR reports, trackers, and workforce data.
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to provide high-quality employee support and collaborate effectively across teams.
  • Ability to identify process improvement opportunities and contribute to the streamlining and digitalisation of HR operations.
  • High level of integrity, accountability, professionalism, and customer-service orientation.
  • Excellent command of written and spoken English.

Benefits

Remuneration

This is a national position at ICIMOD. The starting annual gross salary for this position is NPR 115,727/- (negotiable based on experience and qualifications). Gross salary comprises of basic salary, provident fund, and family/post adjustment allowance. We offer a comprehensive compensation package which includes a basic salary, provident fund, family/post adjustment allowance, child/dependency allowances, insurances (medical, life and accidental), children’s education grant for a maximum 2 children below 18 years of age, severance pay, paid leave (30 holidays and 10 public holidays per year) and day care facility.

Posiiton: Project Coordinator

Project Location: West Rukum (Bafikot and Tribeni Rural Municipalities), Nepal
Service type: Mother and Child Health Integrated with Livelihoods
Project Duration: Five years (annual renew as per performance)
Post Level: Mid-level management
Reports to: Program Manager
Supervisory role: 5-7 staff
Qualifications: Master’s degree in public health, Social Science, Development Studies or a related field.

Professional

Experience

  • Minimum of 5 years of relevant professional experience in MNCH, livelihood and entrepreneurship development programs.

Organisation Background:

  • Human Development and Community Services (HDCS) is a faith-based NGO working in three main sectors of health, education, and community development since 1991. Currently, it manages various projects across Nepal especially in rural regions and provides services through hospitals, health camps, public health

programs, disaster preparedness and response, school development programs, teacher training, educational daycare centres for children with special needs, and community radio stations. HDCS is committed to attract and retain the best employees from all ethnicities, gender and backgrounds in its vision to transform the
communities.

Purpose:

The Project Coordinator (PC) is responsible for the overall planning, implementation, coordination, monitoring, documentation, and reporting of the SHINE Project in Rukum West. The position leads quality project delivery in close coordination with local governments, Training Centers, Municipal Health Sections, Municipal Agriculture Sections, and other relevant local government stakeholders to strengthen local health systems, promote sustainable agriculture, livelihoods, and entrepreneurship, and ensure the achievement of project objectives. The role mainstreams gender equality, disability inclusion, safeguarding, and social

inclusion, ensuring equitable access to quality health services, resources, participation, and decision-making opportunities for women, persons with disabilities, and marginalized communities while contributing to sustainable community development.

Responsibilities:

1. Project Leadership, Planning & Coordination (25%)

  • Lead the planning, implementation, coordination, and monitoring of the SHINE Project to ensure achievement of project objectives, quality standards, and donor requirements.
  • Develop and implement Detailed Implementation Plans (DIPs), Annual Implementation Plans (AIPs), Annual Procurement Plans (APPs), work plans, and implementation schedules for integrated MNCH, livelihood, and entrepreneurship interventions.
  •  Coordinate and collaborate with local governments, health, education, agriculture, and other stakeholders to integrate project activities into local government plans, secure required approvals, and ensure effective implementation.
  • Ensure timely implementation of project activities, achievement of project outputs and milestones, effective risk management, and timely resolution of implementation challenges.
  • Represent the project in coordination forums, technical working groups, and stakeholder meetings to strengthen partnerships, collaboration, advocacy, and resource mobilization.

2. Programme Quality & Technical Oversight (20%)

  • Lead and ensure the quality implementation of integrated MNCH, livelihood, and entrepreneurship interventions in accordance with project design, national guidelines, organizational standards, and donor requirements.
  • Plan, implement, and oversee project activities through regular field monitoring, supportive supervision, quality
    assurance, and evidence-based review to ensure achievement of project targets and outcomes.
  • Strengthen the technical capacity of project staff, health workers, HFOMCs, teachers, farmers, entrepreneurs, and partner institutions through training, coaching, mentoring, technical guidance, and supportive supervision.
  •  Provide technical leadership and practical solutions to address implementation challenges, improve service quality, and ensure timely and effective delivery of project activities.
  •  Promote innovation, adaptive management, and the integration of gender equality, disability inclusion, safeguarding, climate resilience, and community participation to enhance programme quality, sustainability, and project impact.

3. Monitoring, Evaluation, Documentation & Reporting (15%)

  • Monitor project progress against approved plans, indicators, and the project logframe through frequent field visits, supportive supervision, and data verification.
  •  Ensure project staff collect, verify, and submit timely, complete, and accurate data through Kobo Toolbox and other approved monitoring systems.
  • Prepare high-quality monthly, quarterly, annual, and donor reports based on evidence and verified project data.
  • Document case studies, success stories, impact stories, lessons learned, and evidence of project achievements.
  • Maintain accurate project records, databases, Kobo Toolbox data, and supporting documentation.
  • Use monitoring findings, field evidence, and data to support evidence-based decision-making and improve programme performance.

4. Stakeholder Engagement & Partnership (10%)

  • Build strong partnerships with local governments, communities, development partners, and other key stakeholders to ensure effective project implementation.
  •  Coordinate with local level and other relevant government sections to integrate project activities into municipal annual plans and programmes and obtain necessary approvals and endorsements.
  •  Facilitate coordination meetings, review workshops, joint monitoring visits, and multi-stakeholder forums to strengthen collaboration and accountability.
  •  Represent the project in government, technical, and coordination meetings, and maintain effective communication with key stakeholders.
  • Strengthen community participation, local ownership, and partnerships to promote sustainability, resource mobilization, and effective service delivery.

5. Financial & Resource Management (10%)

  • Monitor project budget utilization, expenditure, and burn rate in close coordination with the Finance and Programme departments to ensure efficient use of project funds.
  • Lead procurement planning and oversee the timely acquisition of goods, services, and equipment in accordance with organizational and donor procurement policies.
  • Ensure efficient, transparent, and accountable management of project resources, assets, and inventories.
  •  Review expenditures against approved budgets, work plans, and procurement plans, and recommend corrective actions to address variances.
  •  Ensure compliance with organizational financial policies, donor requirements, procurement procedures, and audit standards.
  •  Identify opportunities for resource mobilization and collaborate with local governments, development partners, private sector actors, and other stakeholders to leverage additional resources and maximize project impact.

6. Team Leadership & Capacity Development (10%)

  • Supervise, coach, mentor, and manage project staff to ensure high performance, accountability, and achievement of project objectives.
  •  Conduct regular performance reviews, provide constructive feedback, and support individual performance improvement and professional development.
  •  Assess staff capacity development needs and facilitate appropriate training, coaching, mentoring, and learning opportunities to strengthen technical and managerial competencies.
  •  Empower project staff by promoting delegation, ownership, teamwork, innovation, and continuous learning to enhance programme quality and organizational effectiveness.
  • Promote a positive, inclusive, and collaborative work environment by applying the Do No Harm approach and fostering teamwork, mutual respect, and adherence to HDCS values, policies, and code of conduct.

7. Compliance, Safeguarding & Inclusion (5%)

  •  Ensure compliance with organizational policies, donor requirements, government regulations, and project guidelines.
  • Mainstream safeguarding, gender equality, disability inclusion, social inclusion, and accountability across all project activities.
  •  Promote ethical conduct and adherence to the HDCS Code of Conduct, organizational values, safeguarding policies, and accountability standards
  •  Identify, report, and address compliance and safeguarding risks, ensuring timely corrective actions and continuous improvement.

8. Knowledge Management & Organizational Support (5%)

  •  Develop and disseminate IEC materials, project communication products, and visibility materials in line with the organizational Knowledge Management and Communication Strategy.
  • Promote project visibility through radio programmes, social media, audio-visual materials, and impactful videos for awareness, advocacy, learning, and resource mobilization.
  • Capture, document, and share best practices, innovations, lessons learned, research findings, and success stories to strengthen organizational learning and evidence-based programming.
  •  Contribute to building a organization learning culture by promoting knowledge sharing, cross-learning, reflection, and continuous improvement across projects and teams.
  •  Perform other duties assigned by the Line manager in support of organizational priorities.

Essential Skills & Competencies:

  • Project Leadership & Management: Ability to lead, coordinate, implement, and manage integrated development projects effectively.
  •  Technical Competence: Sound knowledge of MNCH, livelihood, entrepreneurship, and community development programmes.
  •  Communication & Representation: Excellent verbal, written, presentation, facilitation, organizational and interpersonal skills in Nepali and English.
  • Planning & Organizational Skills: Strong planning, coordination, prioritization, and time management skills.
  •  Partnership & Teamwork: Ability to build effective partnerships, collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, and foster a positive work environment.
  •  Leadership & Capacity Development: Ability to supervise, coach, mentor, and develop project staff while promoting accountability and teamwork.
  •  Problem Solving & Adaptability: Strong analytical, decision-making, and problem-solving skills with the ability to adapt to changing priorities.
  •  Monitoring, Reporting & Digital Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Kobo Toolbox, report writing, data management, and evidence-based reporting.
  •  Knowledge Management & Learning: Ability to document and share lessons learned, best practices, case studies, and impact stories to support organizational learning.
  •  Cultural Sensitivity: Respect for local cultures and the ability to work effectively with women, children, persons with disabilities, and marginalized communities in Rukum West.
  • Integrity & Inclusion: Demonstrated commitment to HDCS values, ethical conduct, safeguarding, gender equality, disability inclusion, social inclusion, accountability, and the HDCS Code of Conduct.
  • Field Orientation & Resilience: Willingness to travel extensively and work effectively in remote and challenging field environments.
  •  Driving License: Valid Category “A” motorcycle driving license with a minimum of two years of driving experience

Posiiton: Senior Project Coordinator – UPLIFT Project
Job Identification: 17069
Job Category: Business Transformation
Apply Before: 07/23/2026, 11:59 PM
Job Schedule: Full time
Locations: FO Surkhet – Field office

The Opportunity

Save the Children believes every child deserves a future. In Bhutan and around the world, we give children a healthy start in life, the opportunity to learn and protection from harm. We do whatever it takes for children – every day and in times of crisis – transforming their lives and future we share. Save the Children expects that anyone associated with it abides by its Safeguarding and other Zero Tolerance Policies.

The position holder will provide strategic, technical, and managerial leadership for the UPLIFT Project, building on the successes and lessons learned from the POWER 4 AY project, funded by Save the Children Italy. The position is responsible for ensuring the effective implementation of the project through strong program leadership, cross-functional coordination, and effective partnership management with government agencies and partner NGOs (PNGOs) under the Surkhet Field Office

Under the supervision of the Head of Field Program Operations (HoFPOs) and with technical guidance from the Livelihoods Specialist, the position holder will establish and maintain strong coordination with key support functions, including Finance, Logistics, PDQI, POD, and MEAL, at both the Field Office and Country Office levels, as well as with the Bhutan team. The position will ensure timely, effective, and high-quality project implementation through close cross-functional collaboration, proactive coordination, and efficient operational support.

The position holder will ensure the development and implementation of high-quality, results-oriented project plans and ensure effective stakeholder coordination at the field level. The position will foster strategic engagement with the private sector to enhance youth employability through vocational training, micro-enterprise development, and innovative livelihood approaches. The position holder will also establish and oversee robust monitoring systems throughout the project cycle to ensure quality implementation, evidence-based decision-making, accountability, and the timely achievement of project outcomes.

The position holder is responsible for ensuring full compliance with Save the Children International’s (SCI) and donor policies, procedures, and standards throughout the project lifecycle, while adhering to the technical guidance and implementation requirements of Save the Children Italy.

The position holder will be responsible for preparing and submitting high-quality project plans, progress reports, quality review reports, and associated action plans to Save the Children Italy (SC Italy) and other relevant stakeholders, as required. In close coordination with the Country Office Award Management team, the position holder will serve as the primary focal point for communication and coordination with the SC Italy Desk.

The position holder will foster an enabling and collaborative environment that empowers partner organizations to effectively implement project activities, ensuring quality delivery, timely reporting, and compliance with project standards. The position holder will strengthen collaboration with government counterparts, the private sector, and other key stakeholders to maximize project outcomes, promote sustainability, and enhance benefits for target groups. Effective partnership management of the SC Italy-funded project is a key responsibility of this position.

In addition, the position holder will work together with Bhutan Office to develop consolidated project report on annual basis ensuring quality and donor standards.

KEY AREAS OF ACCOUNTABILITY:

Planning & Budgeting

  • Lead the planning processes and ensures partners have detailed implementation plan including realistic phased budget outlining major activities and timeline for the funds allocated in required planning templates provided by the organization.
  • Ensures appropriate fund allocation and expenditure monthly and take corrective measures by revisiting plans and communicating timely with technical advisor, finance and Award team.
  • Reviews the project’s BVAs and reports on any discrepancies identified in comparison to the Program BVA shared by the Finance team monthly.
  • Monthly monitoring of fund allocation and expenditure, identify variances, and take corrective actions through plan revisions and timely coordination with Technical Advisors and relevant departments.
  • Coordinates with SCI and partner team to design a robust field visit plan, self-assessments and monitoring systems including follow up on recommendations with relevant department and impact areas.
  • Leads the revision of budgets during budget forecast and on exchange rate fluctuations as per organizational procedures.
  • Prepare and implement a private sector engagement plan, including collaboration with business incubators, based on the findings and recommendations of the market assessment and private sector mapping.
  • Coordinates with Thematic Specialist and Advisors for technical assistance, as required.

Technical Assistance and Quality Assurance:

  • Provide oversight and ensure quality of programmes in the field ensure its optimum benefit to the vulnerable population.
  • Provide technical support to implementing partners to ensure the quality implementation of project activities related to private sector-led job placement and incubation-supported micro-enterprise development, while promoting climate-resilient, green job standards and sustainable livelihood practices.
  • Conduct regular field visits to partner organisations and areas, check and ensure the quality, identify best practices, identify issues to be addressed, and share any issues with the technical advisor to seek solutions/alternatives
  • Coordinate to ensure that all project implementation materials /guidelines are reviewed by relevant technical team
  • Provide technical support to implementing partners on life skills, business incubation approaches, enterprise development, business promotion and marketing, green jobs and green skills, and other emerging approaches and cross-cutting issues related to livelihoods promotion and youth employability.
  • Ensure that project quality benchmarks and standards are established, finalized jointly with MEAL, Technical Advisors, and partners, and consistently applied across all levels of implementation.

Programme Operations & Management

  • Lead market assessments, livelihood opportunity mapping, private sector engagement, and onboarding of strategic private sector partners for the project.
  • Ensures programmatic and financial accountability including tracking of program progress and budget expense in the use of funds and achievement of all related projects, in alignment with Annual Plan and Country Strategy Plan.
  • Ensure the program operations manual (POM) and its components are prepared, understood by relevant staff, and kept in place for monitoring.
  • Ensure and collect the information about project progress from field staff, analyse the expenses Vs activity delivery and submit to Supervisor in a monthly basis.
  • Ensures that programs meet KPIs and /or excels Minimum Operating Standards of key programmatic interventions including Gender and Social Inclusion principles.
    Innovation and Scale
  • Identifies best practices, problems and issues around program implementation and works toward finding solutions for improvements, documenting, disseminating and in consultation with relevant departments and themes.
  • Coordinates with Thematic coordinators/ managers/Specialists, TA providers to promote and pilot innovations in program areas as relevant.
  • Contributes towards developing and implementation of new ideas/innovations and its documentation of processes and its results, based on learning from Nepal’s experience.
  • Ensures effective monitoring and evaluations are carried out for deriving lessons learned, best practices and that monitoring is effective in terms of deriving quick analysis of progresses for immediate corrective actions and measurement of results.
    Identify and engage potential business incubation center within the project areas and support their onboarding to establish and operationalize AI-enabled incubation services for youth entrepreneurs.

Documentation & Reporting

  • As the project lead, update data and information related to the project in the PRIME system
  • Lead the timely preparation and submission of high-quality project plans, progress reports, quality review reports, and other donor-required documentation to Save the Children
  • International and Save the Children Italy, ensuring that achievements, progress, and learning are accurately reflected.
    Coordinate closely with the Livelihoods Specialist, MEAL, Communications and Media teams, and implementing partners to ensure effective monitoring, evaluation, documentation, and dissemination of project achievements, lessons learned, and best practices.
  • Work with the MEAL team to ensure the timely completion of baseline, endline, and other project assessments, incorporating feedback from SCI Nepal, SC Italy, and Country Office teams. Collaborate with the MEAL team and the Livelihoods Specialist to establish and validate baseline values and track progress against project indicators.
  • In close collaboration with the Livelihoods Specialist and the MEAL team, generate evidence, learning, and strategic insights to strengthen ongoing implementation, support adaptive management, and inform future program design.
  • Foster a culture of learning, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement by promoting the documentation and exchange of good practices, innovations, and lessons learned across program and partners.
  • Organize and facilitate field visits for Save the Children, donors, government representatives, and other stakeholders
  • Lead in the preparation of high-quality case studies, learning documents, success stories, and technical reports in line with organizational and donor requirements.
  • Keep the Livelihoods Specialist and HOFO regularly informed of project progress, key achievements, emerging issues, risks, and challenges, and seek technical guidance on strategic decisions and implementation approaches.
  • Work closely with programme and support teams to ensure that all narrative reports and project deliverables are prepared and submitted in accordance with Save the Children and donor contractual requirements, agreed timelines, and quality standards.

Partner’s management

  • Lead partnership management by ensuring compliance with partnership agreements, Save the Children policies, donor requirements, zero-tolerance standards, audit recommendations, and management response plans.
  • Ensure that implementing partners clearly understand and comply with all provisions of partnership agreements, as well as Save the Children’s partnership principles, policies, and procedures, to enable effective and accountable project implementation.
  • Assess partners’ organizational and technical capacity needs, particularly in delivering market-driven employability interventions using innovative approaches such as AI-enabled business incubation, job guarantee-linked employment models, and facilitating access to finance for micro-entrepreneurs through venture capital, credit services, and other emerging financing mechanisms. Develop and implement capacity-strengthening plans to address identified gaps.
  • Build and maintain strong working relationships with government counterparts, implementing partners, development partners, donors, private sector actors, and other key stakeholders at the local, district, and provincial levels to ensure effective project implementation and coordination.
  • Work closely with implementing partners to ensure quality, timely, and results-oriented implementation of project activities, while fostering collaboration, joint problem-solving, and continuous learning.
  • Extend coordination and collaboration with government line agencies, NGOs, CSOs, UN agencies, and other relevant stakeholders to promote integrated programming, resource sharing, and synergies in support of children’s rights and youth employability.
  • Represent SCI in relevant sub national coordination forums, technical working groups, networks, and coalitions, ensuring effective representation of field-level priorities and contributing to sectoral learning and collaboration.
  • Engage with government line agencies and relevant programs to promote the institutionalization and scale-up of successful project approaches and contribute to advocacy efforts that influence child-focused and youth-related policies, strategies, and procedures at the local and provincial levels.
  • Build and maintain strong collaborative relationships across SCI departments and teams to ensure effective cross-functional coordination and integrated program delivery.
  • Document and share project learning, innovations, evidence, and good practices through appropriate national and sub-national platforms, in accordance with Save the Children’s communication and knowledge management protocols.
  • Build and maintain strong coordination and collaboration among implementing partners, provincial networks, government line agencies, and other relevant stakeholders to promote integrated implementation, knowledge sharing, and sustainable project outcome

Representation and Networking

Qualifications and Experience:

  • Master’s degree with 4 years of experience (3 years for GSI) on enterprises development/agriculture ; OR Bachelor’s degree with 6 years of experience (4 years for GSI) on enterprise development/agriculture
  • Prior experience in the I/NGO on empowering youth to create economic opportunities through, minimize the impacts of climate change and promote green job and green skills
  • Demonstrated significant experience in managing projects funded by multiple donors including networks.
  • Proven sound strategic thinking and planning skills, including ability to think creatively and be innovative, set priorities, manage work plans and evaluate progress.
  • Good report writing ad communication skill.
  • Good interpersonal skills including verbal and written communication skills.
  • Teamwork and team mobilization skills.

Required No.(s): 1 (Nepalese Citizen Only)

Contract length: Fixed Term

Location: Surkhet Field Office

Job Title : MEAL Associate
Location : Sankhuwasabha (1) and Gulmi (1)
Reports to : MEAL Coordinator
Supervises : None

Position Overview:

The MEAL Associate is responsible for field level data collection, progress and quality monitoring, and implement other MEAL related priorities of UWS Nepal. The primary responsibility of the position is to operationalize and implement UWS Nepal’s MEAL systems and mechanisms at the district level, in alignment with the guidance, standards, and priorities set by the Country MEAL Team.

The position holder requires a strong understanding of MEAL systems and processes, the ability to manage and analyze large datasets, strong observation skills, and the capacity to verify and validate data to ensure data quality and accuracy. Similarly, the incumbent will conduct periodic data quality assurance and conduct child consultation to understand the perception of the children towards the program. The role also requires close coordination with field program teams, schools, and other key stakeholders to facilitate effective data collection, reporting, and utilization. The MEAL Associate plays a critical role in promoting evidence-based decision-making by ensuring that reliable data is available and used appropriately at the field level.

Key Responsibilities:

1.Monitoring and Evaluation

  • Conduct regular field visits to project sites to monitor activities, utilizing kobo tools.
  • Track project progress against established indicators and activity targets using Indicator Tracking Tables (ITT).
  • Coordinate with school, stakeholders and community members for data collection and guide and supervise enumerators in assessments.

2.Data Collection, Coordination, and Reporting

  • Collect periodic data as per country MEAL team guidelines and facilitate the data collection process in coordination with field program team.
  • Ensure completeness and accuracy of data collection by field staff and verify data with source documents, register and reporting formats.
  • Lead and support timely compilation and submission of monthly and quarterly reports.
  • Maintain proper records and documentation of all monitoring data and reports.
  • Work in close communication with country MEAL team in regards periodic data collection and reporting timeline.
  • Piloting and testing of the tools and templates developed by the country team.

3.Program Monitoring and quality assurance

  • Conduct quality benchmark monitoring of the activities, prepare report, action plan and do necessary follow of with the program team.
  • Compile monthly report of quality benchmark monitoring and share with the field program team.
  • Conduct regular Data Quality Assessments (DQAs) and implement agreed data quality improvement actions.

4.Collaboration, Coordination and Technical Support

  • Serve as the key link between the central MEAL team and field staff to ensure effective coordination and information sharing.
  • Provide technical support and guidance to field staff, volunteers, and enumerators on MEAL tools, data collection, reporting, and data quality standards.
  • Support orientations and refresher trainings on MEAL processes and tools.
  • Follow up with field teams to ensure timely, accurate, and complete data collection aligned with project indicators.
  • Participate in regular coordination meetings and incorporate feedback to continuously improve monitoring processes.

Qualification and Experience:

Education:

  • Minimum bachelor’s degree in Education, or relevant subject

Experience:

  • Minimum 6 months relevant experience in MEAL, data collection and project support.
  • Prior experience of NGO and development sector specially in education and other sector is preferred.

Skills and Competencies:

  • Basic understanding of monitoring, evaluation, and reporting systems.
  • Familiarity in Microsoft Excel, Word, and digital data collection platforms; experience with KoboToolbox or ODK is preferred.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills and have ability to work with multiple stakeholders.
  • Preferred a valid driving license for two-wheeler.
  • Willingness and ability to work in field settings and travel frequently as required.

READ CAREFULLY BEFORE APPLYING

The detail Job Description (JD) of the role can be accessed here – JD – MEAL Associate

Please fill all the required fields. Additionally, please send your updated CV to hr.nepal@uwsglobal.net with the subject line “Application for MEAL Associate”.

The deadline for submitting applications is 23 July 2026 (Thursday). Late applications will not be considered. Only the shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further selection process; canvassing at any stage of the processes shall lead to automatic disqualification. Preference will be given to women and local candidates from minorities and/or indigenous groups.

Posiiton: IT Trainee

Description

WORKSITE LOCATION: Department of Hydrology and Meteorology, Kathmandu with field visits as and when required.

LENGTH OF TRAINEESHIP: Until December 2026

ABOUT MERCY CORPS

Mercy Corps is a leading global organization powered by the belief that a better world is possible. In disaster, in hardship, in more than 40 countries around the world, we partner to put bold solutions into action — helping people triumph over adversity and build stronger communities from within. Now, and for the future. Mercy Corps has been operating in Nepal since 2005, implementing programs in food security, agriculture, market development, financial services, girl’s education, climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction. We take a systems-based approach in our programming, with the aim of improving the lives and potential of vulnerable individuals and communities across Nepal.

PROGRAM/DEPARTMENT SUMMARY

Strengthening Anticipatory Action in Nepal (SAAN) is a 2-year project supporting communities most impacted by frequent flood disasters in Sudurpaschim and Madhesh Provinces by transforming disaster response systems to shift from a traditional reactive approach to a proactive disaster response (known as Anticipatory Action – AA). The program aims to enhance disaster preparedness and response by leveraging established models and flood resilience expertise to partner with communities to proactively mitigate the impact of riverine floods, offering a faster, more cost-effective, and dignified proactive approach compared to traditional reactive humanitarian responses.

The purpose of the proposed project is to provide a swift, cost-effective, and dignified alternative to traditional disaster preparedness, by fortifying communities in Madhesh and Sudurpaschim provinces, as well as local governments, with the necessary knowledge and infrastructure needed for early and timely action in the face of climate-induced floods and droughts. This will support at-risk communities to proactively mitigate the impacts of disasters, while concurrently enhancing the reliability of forecasting by fostering stakeholders’ confidence through more effective utilization of Department of Hydrology and Meteorological (DHM) forecasting systems and working closely with government and private sector partners.

GENERAL POSITION SUMMARY:

The IT Trainee will support the technical development and ongoing maintenance of the Khaderi Monitoring and Guidance Tool; an innovative drought monitoring and forecasting platform developed by the Department of Hydrology and Meteorology (DHM) with technical support from Mercy Corps Nepal. Working closely with the Anticipatory Action Specialist and Meteorologists from DHM’s Agro-Met section, the trainee will support backend and/or frontend programming, bug management, API integration, and continuous system monitoring. This is a hands-on learning opportunity for a recent graduate who is eager to develop professional skills at the intersection of software development, climate science, and humanitarian technology.

As Gender Equality and Social Inclusion (GESI), governance, and environmental compliance are cross-cutting priorities of all Mercy Corps programs, the trainee will be expected to support the integration of these principles into relevant program activities and outputs.

LEARNING AREAS:

1. Technical Development and Programming

  • Contribute to backend and/or frontend development of the Khaderi Monitoring and Guidance Tool using Python (Django) for the backend and Next.js for the frontend, as assigned.
  • Assist in implementing new features and upgrades, including SPI/SPEI drought severity maps, gridded drought monitoring layers, Reference Evapotranspiration (ET₀) calculations, and crop weather calendar functionalities.
  • Support database management tasks using PostgreSQL and assist in maintaining web services via Nginx/Caddy and process management via PM2.
  • Contribute to the development and testing of data pipelines and automated analysis modules for meteorological data from DHM stations.

2. Bug Management & Quality Assurance

  • Identify, document, and resolve software bugs and system errors in the Khaderi Tool in a timely and systematic manner.
  • Conduct regular testing of new and existing features to ensure system reliability, accuracy, and usability.
  • Support the implementation of QC (Quality Control) routines to flag missing data and outliers before analysis, following guidance from the Meteorologist team.
  • Maintain a clear bug log and communicate resolution status to the Meteorologist from DHM, Anticipatory Action Specialist and Senior Project Coordinator.

3. Weather API Monitoring & Integration

  • Monitor and maintain the integration between the Khaderi Monitoring Tool and DHM weather/climate API data sources to ensure uninterrupted data flow.
  • Troubleshoot API connectivity issues, data retrieval failures, and data formatting inconsistencies as they arise.
  • Support upgrades to data interpolation methods (e.g., from Ordinary Kriging to Universal Kriging with trend) and ensure smooth integration with the broader Climate Software platform.
  • Assist in setting up automated pipelines to process daily meteorological data from both automatic and manual stations.

4. Collaboration and Coordination

  • Work collaboratively with the Anticipatory Action Specialist and Meteorologists from DHM’s Agro-Met section on data interpretation, feature requirements, and technical design.
  • Participate in regular technical meetings with DHM staff, Mercy Corps Nepal team, and local implementing partners to discuss progress, challenges, and next steps.
  • Support documentation of technical processes, user manuals, and system design guidelines in both English and Nepali as required.
  • Coordinate with Finance, Compliance, and Operations team members as needed to support procurement or reporting processes related to IT activities.

5. Capacity Building and Learning

  • Actively engage in all learning and training opportunities offered by Mercy Corps Nepal and DHM, including workshops on anticipatory action tools, climate data analysis, and software development best practices.
  • Proactively build technical knowledge in areas relevant to the Khaderi Tool, including drought indices (SPI, SPEI, VHI), FAO Penman-Monteith ET₀ calculations, and agro-meteorological data systems.
  • Seek mentorship from the Anticipatory Action Specialist and Senior Project Coordinator to develop professional skills in humanitarian technology and climate-resilient programming.
  • Contribute to knowledge sharing within the team by documenting learnings, writing technical notes, and presenting progress updates as appropriate.
  • Reflect Mercy Corps’ values of diversity, equity, and inclusion in all collaborative work, actively engaging with team members of diverse backgrounds and expertise.

ACCOUNTABILITY

Reports Directly to: Anticipatory Action Specialist

Works Directly with: Senior Project Coordinator, Anticipatory Action Specialist, other Mercy team, Meteorologist of DHM, Local implementing partner(s); programs, and Operations team members

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS

  • Fresh graduates who have completed bachelor’s degree or master’s degree in computer science, Software Engineering, Data Science, Information Technology or a closely related technical field.
  • Strong willingness to learn, and professional growth.
  • Understanding of web application architecture (backend, frontend, database, web server) and software development life cycles.
  • Basic knowledge of meteorological/climate data
  • Proficiency in Nepali and English (both written and spoken); ability to document technical work clearly in both languages.
  • Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to work independently as well as in a team.
  • Attention to detail and organizational skills.
  • Ability to work collaboratively within a government institutional setting.
  • Good written and verbal communication skills in Nepali and English.
  • Preference will be given to female, persons with disabilities and applicants from the marginalized communities.

PREFERRED/ADVANTAGEOUS

  • Internship or entry-level experience in software development is an advantage, particularly in tools or platforms related to climate, weather, anticipatory action, early warning systems, or related fields.
  • Hands-on experience or academic exposure to backend development (Python/Django preferred) and/or frontend development (JavaScript/Next.js or similar frameworks) is desirable.
  • Commitment to humanitarian values and Mercy Corps’ principles of diversity, equity, and inclusion.

SUCCESS FACTORS

The successful Trainee will be a proactive and motivated learner with a strong interest in applied meteorology, climate services, and disaster risk reduction. They will demonstrate initiative in using Python and data analysis tools to support forecasting, drought monitoring, and early warning activities, while communicating professionally within a government institutional setting.

The candidate should be able to work effectively both independently and within a multidisciplinary team in a dynamic environment, showing adaptability, accountability, and a willingness to learn. They are expected to contribute meaningfully to the technical operations of the Department of Hydrology and Meteorology (DHM) while upholding the professional standards and core values of Mercy Corps.

The most successful Mercy Corps team members have a strong commitment to teamwork and accountability, thrive in evolving and changing environments and make effective written and verbal communication a priority in all situations. Mercy Corps Team members represent the agency both during and outside of work hours when deployed in a field posting or on a visit/short-term assignment to a field posting. Team members are expected to conduct themselves in a professional manner and respect local laws, customs and MC’s policies, procedures, and values at all times and in all in-country venues.

LIVING CONDITION /ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS

The position is based in Department of Hydrology and Meteorology (DHM), and it requires frequent travel to field to support country programs.

ONGOING LEARNING

In support of our belief that learning organizations are more effective, efficient and relevant to the communities we serve, we empower all team members to dedicate 5% of their time to learning activities that further their personal and/or professional growth and development.

DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND INCLUSION

Achieving our mission begins with how we build our team and work together. Through our commitment to enriching our organization with people of different origins, beliefs, backgrounds, and ways of thinking, we are better able to leverage the collective power of our teams and solve the world’s most complex challenges. We strive for a culture of trust and respect, where everyone contributes their perspectives and authentic selves, reaches their potential as individuals and teams, and collaborates to do the best work of their lives. We recognize that diversity and inclusion is a journey, and we are committed to learning, listening and evolving to become more diverse, equitable and inclusive than we are today.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY

Mercy Corps is an equal opportunity employer that does not tolerate discrimination on any basis. We actively seek out diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills so that we can be collectively stronger and have sustained global impact.

We are committed to providing an environment of respect and psychological safety where equal employment opportunities are available to all. We do not engage in or tolerate discrimination on the basis of race, color, gender identity, gender expression, religion, age, sexual orientation, national or ethnic origin, disability (including HIV/AIDS status), marital status, military veteran status or any other protected group in the locations where we work.

SAFEGUARDING AND ETHICS

Mercy Corps is committed to ensuring that all individuals we come into contact with through our work, whether team members, community members, program participants or others, are treated with respect and dignity. We are committed to the core principles regarding prevention of sexual exploitation and abuse laid out by the UN Secretary General and IASC and have signed on to the Misconduct Disclosure Scheme — SCHR. We will not tolerate child abuse, sexual exploitation, abuse, or harassment by or of our team members. As part of our commitment to a safe and inclusive work environment, team members are expected to conduct themselves in a professional manner, respect local laws and customs, and to adhere to Mercy Corps Code of Conduct Policies and values at all times. Team members must complete mandatory Code of Conduct eLearning courses upon hire and annually.

As an applicant, if you witness or experience any form of sexual misconduct during the recruitment process, please report this to Mercy Corps Integrity Hotline (integrityhotline@mercycorps.org).

title.rendered: Office Assistant, Social Mobilizer, Procurement and Logistic Officer, Finance and Administration Coordinator, Technical Officer, GESI Officer, MEAL Coordinator, Technical Coordinator, Senior Program Coordinator

content.rendered:
KIRDARC Nepal is a non-governmental organization (NGO) dedicated to promoting the rights of marginalized and excluded communities through interventions in human rights, democracy, good governance, and inclusive development. The organization works to strengthen the capacities of rights holders, duty bearers, and civil society organizations to advance social justice, equitable access to opportunities and resources, and sustainable community development.

KIRDARC Nepal is implementing the “Breaking Barriers for Girls’ Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) in Dolpa (Kishori Sasaktikaran Pariyojana)” project in Thulibheri Municipality and Tripurasundari Municipality of Dolpa District, with support from Plan International Nepal.

To ensure the effective implementation of this project, KIRDARC Nepal invites applications from qualified, competent, self-motivated, and committed Nepali citizens to fill the following full-time positions:

Posiiton: Senior Program Coordinator – 1 Position based in Dunai Dolpa.

Academic Qualification and Experience: Applicants must have a master’s degree in public health (MPH) with a minimum of three years of relevant professional experience, or a bachelor’s degree in public health (BPH) or a related health discipline with at least five years of relevant experience in NGO/INGO or development projects.

Key Skills and Knowledge required:- Candidate should have experience in managing SRHR or public health programmes, including project planning, implementation, monitoring, reporting, budgeting, and government coordination. They should possess strong leadership, coordination, communication, and report-writing skills, with sound knowledge of SRHR, GESI, SBC, safeguarding, donor compliance, and the project management cycle. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, excellent communication skills in Nepali and English, and a willingness to work in remote and challenging settings are essential.

For more details, visit the detailed job description here.

Position: Technical Coordinator – 1 Position based in Dunai, Dolpa.

Academic Qualification and Experience: Applicants should have a minimum of 2 years of relevant experience with a master’s degree or 3 years with a bachelor’s degree in SRHR or public health programmes. Experience in technical supervision, onsite coaching and mentoring, project implementation, and coordination with government and other stakeholders is preferred.

Key Skills and Knowledge required:– Applicants should have strong leadership, coordination, communication, and report-writing skills, with sound knowledge of SRHR, GESI, SBC, safeguarding, the project management cycle, and donor compliance. Proficiency in Microsoft Office and excellent communication skills in Nepali and English are required.

For more details, visit the detailed job description here.

Posiiton: Finance and Administration Coordinator – 1 Position based in Dunai, Dolpa

Academic Qualification and Experience: Applicants should have a master’s degree in management (specializing in Finance or Accounting) with at least 2 years of relevant experience, or a bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field with at least 5 years of relevant experience.

Key Skills and Knowledge required:- Applicants should have proven experience in financial management, accounting, budgeting, financial reporting, procurement, and administrative operations, preferably in NGO/INGO or donor-funded projects. Experience in ensuring compliance with donor requirements and Government of Nepal financial regulations will be an added advantage.

For more details, visit the detailed job description here.

Position: MEAL Coordinator – 1 Position based in Dunai, Dolpa.

Academic Qualification and Experience: Applicants should have a master’s degree in public health, Statistics, Development Studies, Social Sciences, Public Administration, Management, Gender and Development, or a related field with at least 5 years of relevant experience, or a bachelor’s degree in a related field with at least 7 years of relevant experience in MEAL, preferably in NGO/INGO or donor-funded projects. Experience in SRHR or public health programmes will be an added advantage.

Key Skills and Knowledge required:- Applicants should have sound knowledge of results-based monitoring, MEAL systems, data collection and analysis, safeguarding, accountability, and donor reporting requirements. Strong analytical, report-writing, facilitation, communication, and presentation skills are essential. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, data management tools, and excellent written and spoken communication skills in both Nepali and English are required.

For more details, visit the detailed job description here.

Posiiton:  GESI Officer – 1 Position based in Dunai, Dolpa

Women Development Forum (WDF) is a women-led, non-profit, non-governmental, and non-political organization established in 2062 B.S. by a group of local women in Bajura District. Registered with the Government of Nepal and affiliated with the Social Welfare Council, WDF is dedicated to promoting gender equality, women’s empowerment, and the rights of poor and marginalized communities through a rights-based approach. As a leading community-based organization, WDF works closely with local governments, communities, and development partners to strengthen women’s leadership, promote good governance, and improve access to education, health, nutrition, livelihoods, and protection services. The organization has extensive experience in implementing projects on gender-based violence (GBV), comprehensive sexuality education, nutrition, agriculture, child rights, women’s economic empowerment, and advocacy for policy reform. WDF also supports GBV survivors through the One Stop Crisis Management Center (OCMC) and community awareness initiatives. With strong expertise in institutional capacity building, research, evidence-based planning, and community system strengthening, WDF promotes sustainable development by empowering women, adolescent girls, and vulnerable groups, while fostering strategic partnerships and increasing local investment in women’s rights and social justice.

With the technical and financial support of Plan International Nepal, Women Development Forum (WDF) Bajura is implementing the Sabal Balika Sabal Samudaya (SABASS) Project in Tribeni Municipality, of Bajura District.

Project Brief: Sabal Balika Sabal Samudaya (SABASS).

The Sabal Balika Sabal Samudaya (SaBaSS) Project is an integrated community development initiative implemented by Women Development Forum (WDF) Bajura in partnership with Plan International Nepal. The project is designed to improve the well-being of girls, children, adolescents, and their communities by addressing the underlying causes of poverty, gender inequality, and limited access to quality basic services. The project adopts a multi-sectoral approach, focusing on Early Childhood Development (ECD), Education, Nutrition, Health, Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) and DRR. Through close collaboration with local governments, schools, health facilities, HMG, parents, and Child Club, the project aims “To improve the well-being, protection, and holistic development of vulnerable young children and strengthen the resilience of their communities through inclusive, gender-responsive, and climate-adaptive interventions”. SaBaSS places special emphasis on empowering adolescent girls and marginalized children by improving access to quality education, promoting healthy behaviors, strengthening child protection systems, enhancing WASH services in schools and communities, and increasing community awareness of children’s rights and gender equality. The project also supports the capacity of local institutions and community structures to ensure sustainable service delivery and long-term development outcomes and this project is implementing in Tribeni Rural Municipality since January 2026.

Women Development Forum (WDF) Bajura invites applications from dynamic, motivated, experienced, and qualified Nepalese candidates for the following positions under the Sabal Balika Sabal Samudaya (SABASS) project.

Posiiton: Children’s Home Associate
Location: Surkhet, Karnali

“A world where every child is safe, educated, loved”- Kopila Valley Sewa Samaj is a non-profitable NGO registered in Surkhet, Nepal to provide support for children and families in our community. Our mission is to change the world by empowering Nepal’s children. KVSS programs include a residential children’s home, Kopila Valley School (KVS) for inclusive quality education, women’s empowerment, sustainability, health and wellness, and family development programs. We provide our students with free education, educational materials, health treatment and medicine, counseling, and a place to live. With our innovative future program, we strive to provide our children with career guidance, and scholarships for further studies. Our children’s home provides a safe, loving, and stable home for children in extreme cases of need, when all other options have been exhausted. We do this with our funding partner BlinkNow Foundation and in close ties with the local government and communities.

Position Summary-

The Staff working at KVSS should have patient and passionate about working with children will thrive as childcare. Should be enthusiastic about the growth and well-being of Children, sensitive to their needs, able to mix, relax and interact socially with children and be linked and respected by Children. The Children Home Associate at Children’s Home is responsible for the care, wellbeing, and upbringing of the children living within the KV Children’s Home. Your role is to provide basic parental, pastoral, and holistic care to provide the children with the brightest future possible. It is also your responsibility to manage and coordinate with all the other staff members working within the KV Children’s Home, and make sure that all caregiving roles are running as smoothly as possible.

The Employee will be required to perform the following duties and undertake the following responsibilities in a professional manner.

Job Description

The Main Responsibilities

  • Make sure that the children at KV Children’s Home are cared for and supported in the best way possible.
  • Support to manage caregivers and other support team members.
  • Assist in enrichment activities of the children.

Detailed Responsibilities

1.Make sure that the children at Kopila Valley Children’s Home are cared for and supported in the best possible way.

  • Create and update routines for the activities of children in Kopila Valley Children Home, and make sure that they are followed properly.
  • Lead daily gathering (sutsang) for children after dinner.
  • Manage activities that are designed to promote physical, mental, and social development of children.
  • Support children in their education- ensure that they study for an appropriate period regularly, and manage interactive activities and support children to learn, grow and feel proud.
  • Support children to make sure that they are done with all the chores that they are supposed to do before school
  • Performing regular check-in with the children in a one-to-one format in order to provide support and engage their needs.

2.Support to manage caregivers and other support team members.

  • Supervise the work of caregivers at the children’s home daily and provide continued feedback for any improvements required to improve services.
  • Coordinate with the admin team in regards to any necessary items for children.
  • Demonstrate discipline from personal behavior, initiate it for the team to demonstrate, and encourage it to flow from the team to the children.
  • Support caregivers to address any obstacles or challenges they may face.
  • Evaluate the performance of the home caregivers and support them in their professional development.
  • Coordinate with relevant team members to run all other children’s home functions smoothly.

3.Assist in Academic and non academic activities of the Children

  • Support children during their home works and study time.
  • Assist in non academic activities as planned by the Enrichment coordinator.
  • Prepare engagement activities independently during their free time with the support of caregivers
  • Ensure that children’s study time tables are duly followed as per the time table.

 

Additional expectations

  • Take ownership of change and engage and encourage innovation.
  • Build positive working relationships and environment in the organization by bringing solutions to the discussion table.
  • Report to the manager on a weekly basis, and as needed in case of urgent activities.
  • Build and maintain a strong relationship with the children, staff and relevant stakeholders.
  • Attend weekly staff meetings and share operational updates.
  • Always maintain integrity and confidentiality.
  • The above list of responsibilities is not comprehensive, and candidates may be required to take on additional responsibilities, as determined by your supervisor.

Must Have

Education: +2 in Social science or any discipline.
Experience: At least 3 years of proven experience in a residential care or child protection field
Language: A good communicator, fluent in Nepali and basic in English
Skills and Abilities: Ability to manage, mentor and lead children, youth, their activities
Passionate about and cares for children and vulnerable population; initiator, and competent in managing caregivers, children and basic operations.
Works collaboratively and independently with the least supervision required.

Note: This is a residential and immersive role where the Children Home Associate needs to be hands on caregiving as well. Preferred Female.

Key Characteristics

  • You believe that we can change the world, in the blink of an eye
  • You believe in making decisions that put children first
  • You are self-motivated, organized, and approachable
  • You enjoy creating systems, keeping records, and increasing efficiency
  • You are always looking for new ways for our team to work smarter instead of harder
  • You are detail-oriented and conscientious. You make sure nothing slips through the cracks
  • You have a strong work ethic and a willingness to pick up new skills. You are comfortable multi-tasking
  • You are positive, patient, and a clear communicator who is comfortable working with both Kopila Valley staff in Nepal and the BlinkNow staff in the United States
  • You are an empathetic person, with a knack for putting out fires before they start and soothing conflicts brewing beneath the surface
  • You require little supervision and are flexible. You understand that you will be wearing many hats in this role, many of which are not explicitly included in this job description

Application Procedure

If you think this career opportunity will amplify your career growth, we encourage you to apply by clicking “Apply for this Job” and submit all required document. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for further screening process. No telephone inquiries will be entertained. We accept and screen applications on a rolling basis.
