No. of Vacancy: 1
Location: Baglung

Union Multipurpose Cooperative Society Ltd.

Phone No.: 068-522291

KIRDARC Nepal is an NGO committed to promoting the rights of marginalized and excluded communities through initiatives in human rights, democracy, and good governance. Focusing on building the capacities of right holders, duty bearers, and civil society, it aims to foster inclusive growth and equitable development, working toward a just and prosperous society where people have access to and ownership of resources.

In partnership with Plan International, KIRDARC Nepal will implement the “Promotion of Sustainable Health And Nutrition (POSHAN) Project” across three local bodies in Kalikot District: Raskot Municipality, Sanni Triveni Rural Municipality, and Narhari Nath Rural Municipality. KIRDARC Nepal is seeking qualified, dynamic, and motivated Nepali citizens for the following full-time positions to support project implementation in these areas.

Position Description: Program Coordinator (Female only) – 1 Position Based in Raskot Municipality of Kalikot District.

Academic Qualification and Experience: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in public health, Nutrition, Nursing, Health Education, Development Studies, or a related field. Minimum 5 years of relevant experience with a bachelor’s degree or 3 years with a master’s degree.

Key Skills and Knowledge required:- Minimum 5 years of relevant experience with a bachelor’s degree or 3 years with a master’s degree in nutrition, public health, or development projects. Experience in nutrition programming, MIYCN, IYCF, CMAM, WASH, NGO/INGO projects, government coordination, and project monitoring and reporting is required. Strong skills in project coordination, leadership, reporting, training, budget management, MS Office, and communication in Nepali and English are essential.

For more details, visit the detailed job description here.

Position Description: Field Supervisor (Female only) – 3 Positions (One for Each Rural Municipality/Municipality)

Academic Qualification and Experience: ANM, CMA, PCL Nursing, or equivalent qualification from a recognized institution.

Key Skills and Knowledge required:- At least one year of relevant experience in nutrition, public health, community health, or NGO/INGO projects. Basic knowledge of MIYCN and WASH, with skills in community mobilization, coordination, reporting, MS Office (Word and Excel), and the ability to work effectively in remote communities. Experience in supervising community-based activities will be an added advantage.

For more details, visit the detailed job description here.

Interested candidates who meet these criteria should send their Application Letter and KIRDARC Job Application Form to vacancy@kirdarc.org by 8 July 2026, before 5:00 PM. The CV/Resume is not necessary to submit but the prescribed KIRDARC Job Application Form is mandatorily required to apply. The interested candidates can download the KIRDARC Job Application Form from here.

Preference will be given to disadvantaged groups (Female, Dalit, Janajati) and local resident in the recruitment process. The person working with this organization must be committed to following the provisions of the child protection, safeguarding policy, and code of conduct of the organization. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Any telephone inquiries/and or attempts to apply undue influence will result in the disqualification of candidates. KIRDARC Nepal reserves the right to reject any application without assigning any reason whatsoever.

Note: The written test will be conducted in person at various KIRDARC Nepal cluster offices, and candidates will be informed of the available locations to choose the most convenient one.

Position: Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer
Requisition Number: 3NPSR006864
Location: Kathmandu with frequent field travel
Position Status: Full-time, Regular

ABOUT MERCY CORPS

Mercy Corps is a leading global organization powered by the belief that a better world is possible. In disaster, in hardship, in more than 40 countries around the world, we partner to put bold solutions into action – helping people triumph over adversity and build stronger communities from within. Now, and for the future. Mercy Corps has been operating in Nepal since 2005, implementing programs in food security, agriculture, market development, financial services, youth engagement, climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction. We take a systems-based approach in our programming, with the aim of improving the lives and potential of vulnerable individuals and communities across Nepal.

PROGRAM/ DEPARTMENT SUMMARY

The Zurich Climate Resilience Alliance (ZCRA) is an alliance of nine organizations including international NGOs, private sector, and academic organizations, who have come together with the support of the Zurich Foundation to drive better policy and practice to reduce the negative impact of floods on peoples’ and communities’ ability to thrive.

The Alliance is implementing a 12-year vision through 2025. The current phase (phase 3, 2024-2027) represents the first cycle of this long-term vision. Previously, the Alliance completed its first phase in 2018, and its second phase in December 2023 and third phase will run till Dec 2027. During this time, The Alliance works together to achieve 12-year vision by implementing solutions, promoting good practice, influencing policy and facilitating systemic change. In Nepal, the Alliance is comprised of Mercy Corps and Practical Action, working together on joint outcomes aiming at:

  • Building ex-ante resilience of communities at risk of flood and heat impacts across Nepal
  • Improving early warning and early action in advance of probable hazards
  • Enabling people who experience livelihoods losses due to floods and heat to recover quickly through compensation and/or insurance

Mercy Corps is leading the ‘influencing and advocacy’ work within the Alliance, as well as making substantial contributions to work streams on knowledge management, research, and piloting innovative field programming with communities, private sector, and government. In Nepal, the Alliance is comprised of Mercy Corps and Practical Action. The Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer will be part of a dynamic international team and play a central role in advancing Alliance work in Nepal and globally.

GENERAL POSITION SUMMARY

The Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer assume responsibility to lead overall design, management, implementation, and coordination for ZCRA’s policy, advocacy and influencing work in climate resilience in Nepal. The Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer will provide strategic oversight and leadership to help ZCRA in Nepal to achieve its project advocacy and influencing goals with summary of key functions as follows:

Identify and lead formulation of appropriate advocacy and influencing strategies, plans, initiatives, research and implement them at federal, provincial, local and community level as required.
Actively lead the advocacy and influencing work including but not limited to disaster governance, disaster budget influencing and tracking heat governance, risk-informed planning, flood, heat, urban heat, drought private sector engagement in disaster management and climate change adaptation at project’s working areas engaging federal, provincial local and community stakeholders.
Develop advocacy and influencing materials and support the project in relation to its knowledge products.
Establish and strengthen strategic partnerships with government agencies, alliance partner, peer organizations, development partners, civil society, and private sector, think tanks, research institutes and media for advocacy and influencing work.
The Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer primary focus will be on influencing in that country but will also likely engage with global advocacy around some or all of the Alliance’s priority themes (disaster risk reduction, climate change adaptation, governance, early warning system, nature-based solutions, heat, and urban).

Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer will work under the direct supervision of the Program Manager and work closely with Mercy Corps field-teams, Climate Resilience Alliance partner at the country level (Practical Action in Nepal) as well as Mercy Corps’ Regional Program and Advocacy Manager based in Bangkok, Thailand, and Head of ZFRA program based in the UK.

As gender and social inclusion (GESI), governance and environmental compliance are cross-cutting and core components of all MC programs and is required to own these components to ensure GESI sensitivity and integration, governance practices and environmental compliance are integrated into the program activities.

ESSENTIAL JOB RESPONSIBILITIES

The specific duties and responsibilities of the Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer include, but are not limited to the following:

STRATEGY AND VISION (15 %)

Develop advocacy and influencing strategy within the project contributing to country Strategy paper of MCN (Pathways to Possibility)

  • Identify influencing opportunities at federal, provincial and local level for replication and scale up of project activities within and beyond MCN project areas.
  • Provide necessary information on policy landscape and from the projects to Business Development Team for developing
  • projects/programs related to Disaster Risk Management, Climate Adaptation and Emergency Response
  • Support to Identify capacity building needs of partners for influencing and advocacy.
  • Ensure stakeholder mapping and their actions related to climate and flood resilience in federal, province and working municipalities.

PROGRAM MANAGEMENT (10 %)

A. PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION

  • Develop influencing and advocacy tools and capacitate the project team including partner staff to use these tools.
  • Support to localize Climate Resilience for Communities tools and advocate for it.
  • Design capacity building exercises on social mobilization, influencing and advocacy materials for effective project implementation.
  • Support to produce high-quality reports for donors and SWC.
  • Ideate appropriate events, campaigns and other actions for influencing and advocacy.
  • Engage in Advocacy and Influencing related procurement.
  • Work closely with program manager for budget management and reporting to donors and SWC.
  • Support to monitor expenditures against budget and suggest modifications as necessary.

B. TEAM MANAGEMENT

  • Promote accountability, communicate expectations, and provide constructive feedback to partner staff for quality performance and mitigate frequent turnover.
  • Organize periodic capacity building exercises for partners based on capacity needs assessment.

INFLUENCE, ADVOCACY & REPRESENTATION (70%)

  • Ensure Stakeholder mapping and analysis including climate, heat, drought and flood resilience interventions and planning at federal, and provincial level.
  • Develop projects’ influencing and advocacy strategies especially on heat, flood, drought and emerging disaster and support province coordinator and partner led implementation at province and community level.
  • Identify, document, and communicate the changes in context and policy at federal and province level to develop and or modify project’s influencing strategies if necessary.
  • Develop appropriate influencing and advocacy materials for effective implementation of influencing and advocacy strategies.
  • Lead high-quality policy analysis, briefing papers, research, knowledge products, media work and other advocacy content such as talking points, key messages and media releases that effectively communicate and promote ZCRA/Mercy Corps’ policy positions.
  • Lead DRR budget advocacy influencing and tracking work at provincial, local and community level.
  • Regular and on-going coordination with key government, non-governmental, donor, private sector and stakeholders related to the climate and flood resilience agenda at federal, provincial and local level.
  • Regularly convey information to Mercy Corps’ Climate Resilience Alliance team as well as the project team.
  • Network in the federal, province and local level to establish and maintain strategic relations with relevant local and international stakeholders, to continually inform, challenge, and improve Mercy Corps’ advocacy, strategy, and program development.
  • Coordinate and closely work with Mercy Corps programs in support of a climate resilience agenda especially on heat, drought, flood; represent these programs to government and other external stakeholders as needed at province level.
  • Provide representation, at the discretion of the Program Manager and or Country Director with relevant local and international governmental and non-governmental actors in different tiers of governments.
  • Provide strategic inputs for Mercy Corps Nepal’s policy and advocacy related to climate resilience, DRR and climate change adaptation at the provincial, and lead province level influencing and advocacy work in close collaboration with the global team.
  • Present Mercy Corps’ and Zurich work with relevant INGO and government networks to display thought leadership and influence policies and programs related to flood resilience, disaster risk reduction, and climate change adaptation at province level.
  • Contribute to the development of ZCRA’s global reports and policy positions.
  • Monitor and analyze the progress of advocacy and influencing efforts, identifying, and capturing advocacy wins, case studies, gaps and challenges.
  • Develop advocacy and influencing tools, training packages and enhance the capacity of partners and communities.
  • Ensure gender equality and social inclusion in advocacy and influencing work.

ORGANIZATIONAL LEARNING (5%)

  • As part of our commitment to organizational learning and in support of our understanding that learning organizations are more effective, efficient, and relevant to the communities they serve, we expect all team members to commit 5% of their time to learning activities that benefit Mercy Corps as well as themselves.

OTHERS

  • Conduct himself/herself both professionally and personally in such a manner as to bring credit to Mercy Corps and to not jeopardize its humanitarian mission.
  • Mercy Corps as a humanitarian agency is expected to respond as and when a crisis and humanitarian situation engulf. As MC employees, all staff are expected to be part of humanitarian and emergency response in addition to their regular roles and responsibilities.
  • Other duties assigned by the Country Director.

SECURITY

  • Ensure compliance with security procedures and policies as determined by country leadership.
  • Proactively ensure that team members operate in a secure environment and are aware of policies.

ACCOUNTABILITY TO BENEFICIARIES

Mercy Corps team members are expected to support all efforts toward accountability, specifically to our beneficiaries and to international standards guiding international relief and development work, while actively engaging beneficiary communities as equal partners in the design, monitoring, and evaluation of our field projects.
Team members are expected to conduct themselves in a professional manner and respect local laws, customs and MC’s policies, procedures, and values always and in all in-country venues.

SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITY: N/A

ACCOUNTABILITY

Reports Directly to: Program Manager

Works Directly with: ZCRA Alliance III partners – Practical Action, Province Coordinator(s) and Mercy Corps’ other programs

KNOWLEDGE AND EXPERIENCE

  • Master’s degree in climate change, disaster risk management, social sciences or other relevant fields.
  • At least 5 years relevant experience with designing, managing, and implementing policy research, advocacy and influencing initiatives in Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Change Adaptation.
  • Strong knowledge and understanding of Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Change Adaptation including system change approaches.
  • Excellent ability to conduct policy and stakeholder analysis through rigorous document reviews and network development, and articulate policy recommendations for advocacy and influencing.
  • Ability to scope, design, and lead research to build evidence base for programming and advocacy as well as program impact evaluation.
  • Ability to summarize and persuasively communicate complex information in written and verbal form to different audiences, including experience leading the production of external written policy products.
  • Team player can coordinate and facilitate tasks for program outcomes both internally and externally, with stakeholders including Alliance partners, national/sub-national/local governments, community groups, INGOs, NGOs, donors, UN, private sector and others.
  • Ability to demonstrate an openness and willingness to learn about the application of gender, women’s rights, diversity and inclusion for all aspects of program work.
  • Strong organizational skills and ability to effectively handle multiple tasks, work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.
  • Proficiency in written and spoken English and Nepali.
  • Demonstrated proficiency with the MS Office software (i.e., Word, Excel, PowerPoint), design tools is required.
  • Ability to frequently travel to remote areas and work effectively with an ethnically diverse team in a sensitive environment.

SUCCESS FACTORS

A successful Senior Advocacy and Influencing Officer will be able to develop and execute effective advocacy and influencing strategies backed by solid communication products and knowledge management to achieve the project’s outcome. S/he will be able to coordinate, collaborate and strongly influence community, local, provincial and federal stakeholders to amplify projects’ influencing agenda, replication, and scale of project’s achievement. S/he should have the ability to travel frequently to remote areas and the capacity to develop and deliver training packages and tools as required in the project districts.

The most successful Mercy Corps staff members have a strong commitment to teamwork and accountability, thrive in evolving and changing environments and make effective written and verbal communication a priority in all situations.

LIVING CONDITIONS / ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS

The position is based in Kathmandu, and it requires up to 50% field travel to support country programs.

ONGOING LEARNING

In support of our belief that learning organizations are more effective, efficient and relevant to the communities we serve, we empower all team members to dedicate 5% of their time to learning activities that further their personal and/or professional growth and development.

DIVERSITY, EQUITY & INCLUSION

Achieving our mission begins with how we build our team and work together. Through our commitment to enriching our organization with people of different origins, beliefs, backgrounds, and ways of thinking, we are better able to leverage the collective power of our teams and solve the world’s most complex challenges. We strive for a culture of trust and respect, where everyone contributes their perspectives and authentic selves, reaches their potential as individuals and teams, and collaborates to do the best work of their lives.

We recognize that diversity and inclusion is a journey, and we are committed to learning, listening and evolving to become more diverse, equitable and inclusive than we are today.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY

Mercy Corps is an equal opportunity employer that does not tolerate discrimination on any basis. We actively seek out diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills so that we can be collectively stronger and have sustained global impact.

We are committed to providing an environment of respect and psychological safety where equal employment opportunities are available to all. We do not engage in or tolerate discrimination on the basis of race, color, gender identity, gender expression, religion, age, sexual orientation, national or ethnic origin, disability (including HIV/AIDS status), marital status, military veteran status or any other protected group in the locations where we work.

SAFEGUARDING & ETHICS

Mercy Corps is committed to ensuring that all individuals we come into contact with through our work, whether team members, community members, program participants or others, are treated with respect and dignity. We are committed to the core principles regarding prevention of sexual exploitation and abuse laid out by the UN Secretary General and IASC and have signed on to the Interagency Misconduct Disclosure Scheme. We will not tolerate child abuse, sexual exploitation, abuse, or harassment by or of our team members. As part of our commitment to a safe and inclusive work environment, team members are expected to conduct themselves in a professional manner, respect local laws and customs, and to adhere to Mercy Corps Code of Conduct Policies and values at all times. Team members are required to complete mandatory Code of Conduct eLearning courses upon hire and on an annual basis.

As an applicant, if you witness or experience any form of sexual misconduct during the recruitment process, please report this to Mercy Corps Integrity Hotline (integrityhotline@mercycorps.org).

Posiiton: Trainee ( Program Funding Associate) at DCA Nepal
Number: 3963
Deadline: 16/07/2026, 23:55
Location: 44600, Kathmandu, Nepal
Department: CO Nepal
Vacancy is: National

DCA is a Danish Development and Humanitarian organisation with a vision of a world without hunger, poverty and oppression. We have been supporting development work in Nepal since the 1980s. Currently we focus on the following themes to improve the life of the most marginalised communities in some of the most remote and vulnerable regions of Nepal, applying a rights-based approach:

Save Lives (Focusing on Disaster Preparedness and Humanitarian Response)
Build Resilient Communities (Focusing on Resilient Livelihoods and Sustainable Food Security)
Fight Extreme Inequality (Focusing on Governance, Human Rights and Empowerment)
According to the Country Programme Strategy (2022-2026) of Nepal Office, we are committed to two cross-commitments: (i) Gender Equality and (ii) Climate Action.

DCA Nepal seeks application from candidates who is keen to build foundation for challenging and rewarding career in an international development environment through hands-on knowledge and practical experience as Trainee in the capacity of Programme Funding Associate. This is an opportunity starting immediately for a year based at DCA Office, Kathmandu.

The Trainee will support proposal development by researching and tracking the potential funding opportunities; collecting and analysing information, carrying out literature reviews ; drafting capacity statements; contributing in project information management, knowledge management and communication for positioning the organisation, supporting technical detailing and designs for programmatic interventions as needed; coordinating for event management, supporting programme advocacy and research; taking minutes, drafting reports and communication products, ; proof reading, updating and sharing programme reports/proposal; and maintaining data management system. The Trainee will report to Program Funding Manager.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Review and update project documents, reports and capacity statements on various thematic sectors and sub-sectors.
  • Support in pre-design phase; needs assessment, provide technical detailing and designs, lead in reviewing the literature, data collecting and analysing information relevant for proposal design.
  • Support to proofread documents (proposals and reports) as and when required.
  • Research the potential funding opportunities, prepare summary and share with relevant programme team.
  • Provide support in thematic interventions designs, including at partner level.
  • Coordinate with implementing partners and communications focal point to produce relevant learning and impact documents.
  • Update the project information management system, including contributing in meeting minutes, case studies, photos, videos, partners reports and final knowledge products.
  • Support thematic managers and communications focal point with knowledge materials drafting and publication.

What you bring:

  • Master’s degree in related discipline such as Media and Communication, Social Sciences, Disaster Risk Reduction, Environment Science/NRM and Development Studies
  • Some experience in supporting proposal development, knowledge management, and communication.
  • Ability to use MS Office packages, and AI applications
  • Research and reports writing skills
  • Effective verbal and written communications both in English and Nepali languages
  • Good coordination, networking and interpersonal skills
  • Creative and independent as well as team player with the ability to adhere to deadlines and flexibility

Interested candidate irrespective of age, gender, race, religion or ethnic affiliation are encouraged to apply for this opportunity.

Please click on Apply button at the end of this vacancy announcement to apply.

Applications consisting of the below will only be considered:

Cover Letter (max 1 page)
CV (max 2 pages)

Only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

Closing date for application will be Thursday, 16 July 2026

Telephone inquiry will not be entertained

All interested candidates irrespective of age, gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, abilities or ethnic affiliation are encouraged to apply for the vacancy. DCA conducts an anti-terror check as part of the recruitment process. It is a prerequisite that you can pass this check and maintain this status throughout your employment with us. Everyone applying for a job with DCA must be ready to comply with our Code of Conduct, Staff Policy on Prevention of Sexual Exploitation, Abuse and Harassment and our Child Safeguarding Policy.

HERD International is a research and development agency dedicated to generating and utilizing evidence to improve the health and well-being of people. Our mission involves empowering individuals and communities through collaborative efforts with policy practitioners, researchers, and concerned stakeholders. We are committed to promoting evidence-informed and contextually tailored solutions to address local issues, strengthen systems, and establish best practices, with the ultimate goal of enhancing the quality of life.

Position: Senior Communication and Monitoring Officer: Agroecology and Pollination for Central Office

Must have:

  • A Master’s degree in Agroecology, Entomology, Horticulture, or a related discipline with a strong focus on quantitative research methods from recognized institution.
  • At least five years of progressive experience in communication and monitoring activities within agricultural research/development projects.
  • Excellent written and spoken communication skills in Nepali and English.

Instructions for applying:

Interested and qualified individuals are requested to use the below link for the application to be completed by July 12, 2026.

Online Application Form

Fill out the online application form and attach your latest CV and a Cover letter.

Only short-listed candidates will be invited to participate in an assessment process. Any effort to influence the selection process will result in disqualification.

We encourage applications from women and people from diverse groups across ethnicity and geography.

Position: Business Development Officer
Location: Pulchowk

Business Development Officer plays a key role in strengthening eSewa’s strategic relationships with banks and financial institutions (BFIs). The position is responsible for managing the complete partnership lifecycle, coordinating cross-functional initiatives, driving transaction and revenue growth, and identifying new business opportunities. The role requires a commercially driven, analytical, and relationship-oriented professional who can effectively collaborate with both internal teams and external banking partners to achieve business objectives.

Qualification and Experience

  • Master’s degree in Business Administration (MBA), Finance, Banking, Management, or a related discipline.
  • Minimum 3–5 years of relevant experience in banking, financial services, fintech, or digital payments.
  • Hands-on experience in bank/BFI relationship management, partnership management, or business development.
  • Experience in data analysis, commercial negotiations, and stakeholder management is preferred.
  • Prior experience at eSewa or within the digital payment ecosystem will be an added advantage.

Job Description

  • Bank & Financial Institution Partnership Management
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with partner banks and financial institutions.
  • Manage the complete partnership lifecycle, including onboarding, integration, performance monitoring, contract renewals, and business expansion.
  • Represent eSewa in meetings, negotiations, and business review sessions with partner institutions.

Operational Coordination

  • Coordinate API integrations, system migrations, and new service implementations with BFIs.
  • Act as the liaison between banking partners and internal teams such as Product, Technology, Finance, and Compliance.
  • Resolve operational issues related to transaction flow, settlement, and service availability.
  • Ensure timely documentation, compliance, and audit readiness for all banking partnerships.

Business Growth & Performance Management

  • Drive transaction growth, revenue generation, and partner performance across the banking portfolio.
  • Analyze transaction data to identify business opportunities, trends, and performance gaps.
  • Develop and execute data-driven growth initiatives, promotional campaigns, and partnership strategies.
  • Prepare dashboards, MIS reports, and performance reviews for management and partner banks.

New Partnership Development

  • Identify and acquire new banking and financial institution partners.
  • Prepare business proposals, presentations, and negotiate commercial agreements.
  • Expand existing partnerships by introducing new products and services.

Cross-functional Collaboration

  • Collaborate with Marketing on co-branded campaigns and promotional activities.
  • Work closely with Product teams to enhance banking-related products and services.
  • Coordinate with Finance to ensure accurate settlements, reconciliations, and pricing reviews.

Required Skills

  • Excellent communication and presentation skills
  • Strong stakeholder and relationship management abilities
  • Analytical and problem-solving mindset
  • Negotiation and influencing skills
  • Planning, organization, and documentation skills
  • Cross-functional collaboration and coordination
  • Result-oriented with strong ownership and accountability
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and partnerships simultaneously

Benefits of Working at eSewa

  • Stellar opportunity to work with the rising company
  • Amazing and passionate young team, beautiful office space
  • Trust of biggest FinTech company.
  • One-of-a-kind company culture and growth opportunities to accelerate your career progression

How to apply?

We are always keen to meet energetic and talented professionals who would like to join our team. Click on the button below and submit your application to apply for the post.

Application Deadline: Jul 31, 2026

Position: Marketing Analytics & Performance Officer
Location: Pulchowk, Lalitpur

Application Deadline: July 31, 2026

The Marketing Analytics & Performance Officer is responsible for driving data-driven marketing decisions by managing campaign analytics, attribution, performance reporting, and KPI governance. This role ensures that marketing investments deliver measurable business outcomes, including user acquisition, activation, retention, revenue growth, and return on advertising spend (ROAS). Working closely with Brand & Marketing, Data Engineering, and Business teams, the incumbent transforms data into actionable insights that optimize marketing performance and support strategic decision-making.

Qualification and Experience

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Information Management, or a related field.
  • Minimum 2–4 years of experience in marketing analytics, performance marketing, business intelligence, or digital marketing analytics.
  • Experience in campaign performance analysis, attribution modeling, and marketing KPI reporting.
  • Hands-on experience with business intelligence tools and marketing analytics platforms is preferred.
  • Experience in fintech, digital products, or e-commerce will be an added advantage.

Job Description

Marketing Analytics & Attribution

  • Own end-to-end marketing attribution and campaign performance analytics.
  • Track customer journeys from campaign exposure through activation and revenue generation.
  • Measure campaign effectiveness using key performance metrics, including ROAS and conversion rates.

Performance Analysis

  • Analyze marketing funnels, including registration-to-KYC, activation, conversion, and repeat usage.
  • Monitor customer behavior and identify trends to improve marketing effectiveness.
  • Develop customer cohorts and segmentation for acquisition, retargeting, and cross-selling initiatives.

KPI Governance & Reporting

  • Own monthly, quarterly, and annual marketing KPI tracking aligned with organizational objectives.
  • Develop and maintain interactive marketing dashboards and performance reports.
  • Prepare daily business performance reports for Brand Managers and leadership.

Cross-functional Collaboration

  • Work closely with Brand & Marketing, Data Engineering, Product, and Business teams to ensure data accuracy and availability.
  • Support data governance initiatives to improve marketing measurement and reporting.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to define and monitor marketing success metrics.

Performance Optimization

  • Provide data-driven recommendations for campaign optimization, budget allocation, and scaling.
  • Identify opportunities to improve customer acquisition, activation, retention, and overall marketing efficiency.
  • Support continuous improvement of attribution models and marketing measurement frameworks.

Required Skills

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Data-driven decision-making mindset
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills
  • Attention to detail and accuracy
  • Stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration
  • Time management and organizational skills
  • Proactive approach to identifying business opportunities
  • Ability to translate complex data into actionable business insights

Benefits of Working at eSewa

  • Stellar opportunity to work with the rising company
  • The amazing and passionate young team, beautiful office space
  • Trust of biggest FinTech company.
  • One-of-a-kind company culture and growth opportunities to accelerate your career progression.

How to apply?

We are always keen to meet energetic and talented professionals who would like to join our team. Click on the button below and submit your application to apply for the post.

Posiiton: Gold Tester (Contractual Basis)

Job Summary:

The Gold Tester will be responsible for assessing the quality and authenticity of gold items, ensuring accuracy and compliance with the Bank’s or organization’s standards and procedures. This role requires precision, attention to detail, and expertise in testing techniques and tools.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Conduct tests to determine the purity, weight, and authenticity of gold and other precious metals.
  • Use appropriate testing equipment such as acid tests, electronic gold testers, and XRF machines.
  • Maintain accurate records of test results and prepare required reports.
  • Ensure compliance with organizational standards and regulatory guidelines during all testing procedures.
  • Safeguard and maintain the integrity of gold items during testing.
  • Provide expert advice on gold quality and valuations to stakeholders.
  • Identify and report counterfeit or substandard gold items.

 

Required Job Knowledge Competencies:

  • Conduct tests to determine the purity, weight, and authenticity of gold and other precious metals.
  • Use appropriate equipment and methods, such as acid tests, electronic gold testers, and XRF machines.
  • Maintain accurate records of test results and prepare reports as required.
  • Ensure compliance with organizational standards and regulatory guidelines in all testing procedures.
  • Safeguard and maintain the integrity of all gold items during testing.
  • Provide expert advice on gold quality and valuations to relevant stakeholders.
  • Identify and report counterfeit or substandard items.
  • Strong knowledge of gold testing techniques and tools.
  • Familiarity with industry standards for gold purity and valuation.
  • Attention to detail and precision in handling precious items.
  • Good understanding of safety measures and proper handling procedures.
  • Proficiency in record keeping and report preparation.
  • Ability to work independently and manage time effectively.
  • Strong ethical standards and trustworthiness.

Prerequisites for applying:

  • Minimum Education Qualification – SEE/SLC.
  • Age minimum 25 years as of 8th July 2026.
  • Candidates must have at least two years of work experience in a related field.
  • Candidates must be proficient in handling PC.

OTHER ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA

  • In case of any educational qualification from foreign Universities /institutions, equivalence certificate from competent authority of Nepal is must.
  • Candidate applying for any above position must be willing to work anywhere in Nepal as per the Bank’s requirement.
  • All candidates must be proficient in written/spoken Nepali and English language and be computer literate with working knowledge of MS Office applications.
  • Candidates are expected to have smart and pleasant personality, be articulate with superior interpersonal skills and desire to excel. The candidates must have positive attitude and be a team player but be able to work independently and take initiatives.

Application Deadline: 9th July 2026

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further selection process. The Bank reserves the right to reject any/all applications without assigning any reason.

Canvassing in any form or at any stage of the process shall lead to automatic disqualification.

Posiiton: Manager Client Due Diligence Operation
Requisition Number: 57145
Job Location: Kathmandu, NPL
Global Grade: Band 7
Work Type: Office Working
Employment Type: Permanent
Posting End Date: 15/07/2026

Job Description:

Job Summary

The role will be responsible for managing Client Due Diligence (CDD) operations and coordinating with clients and business teams to complete required CDD activities for existing bank clients. The role will also review and approve CDD cases in the system, including high-risk individual and SME banking clients, and provide CDD advisory support for new account opening, periodic reviews and trigger-based reviews.

Key Responsibilities

  •  Manage overall risk of the client.
  •  Assess whether the risks are accurately identified, assessed and appropriate risk rating is applied.
  •  Understand the client CDD profile and the inherent risk. Review whether conduct of account is in line with the CDD profile.
  •  Ensure CDD information completed by the maker/checker is current and valid, as per the required standard.
  • Review and ensure Client documents are complete and updated in core banking system
  • Assess adverse media searches carried out adequately by the CDD team
  •  Review Quality of Statement of Work (SOW) and corroboration is adequate and carried out as required
  •  Review the Politically Exposed Person (PEP) assessment to ensure compliance to Local and Group Standards
  •  Review account activity conducted by Individual CDD / Relationship Manager (where required)
  •  Review completed Tax assessment for High Net Worth (HNW) clients
  • Follow-up on deferral and exception requests – proceed with or stop onboarding
  •  Collaboration with Relationship Manager/ Sales channel or other responsible parties for CDD guidance
  •  Handle different Group & internal management reports/MIS
  •  Manage to maintain expected threshold / target set for reviews and Request For Information (RFI)
  •  Handle additional responsibilities assigned by Line Manager, as and when deemed required,
  •  Ensure confidentially, integrity and availability of the information.
  •  Update transaction referrals, referred by the transaction monitoring unit on time.
  •  Ensure to update quality & complete information in line with Anti Money Laundering (AML) perspective in transaction referrals from transaction monitoring unit
  •  Ensure timely response to Financial Crime Compliance unit/Financial Crime Surveillance Operations raised
  •  Ensure timely response to Business/Regional Sanction Team on any query raised
  •  Ensure timely understanding and compliance with all the applicable regulations of the Bank, group and country including Money Laundering Prevention and KYC.
  •  Apply Group and Country CDD policies and processes (AML surveillance, client screening, risk assessment) to manage risks.
  • Provide CDD advisory in client level to business in relations to AML/Counter Terrorist Financing (CTF) matters
  • Assess risks arising from products / segments / geographies / customers / transactions.
  •  Timely escalation of issues and ensure that necessary actions are taken quickly to remediate and/or activities are ceased.
  •  Undertake enhanced due diligence on new and existing medium/higher risk
  •  Review and/ or escalate new or emerging areas of risks for all clients and Group Sensitive Clients relevant clients such as adverse media/sanctions review
  •  Timely review of customer’s profile and related risk in relation to AML/CFT matter
  •  Make recommendations to relevant stakeholders on possible risk management responses to identified risks and/ or findings of concern from investigations- at client and portfolio level
  •  Escalation points for generic procedure related enquiries.
  •  Manage escalations on issues requiring additional assessment and controls.
  •  For Audit (Internal & external), manage checks, queries and any other information requested by auditors.
  •  Review and manage monthly assurance check and complete submission on time.
  •  Assist Line Manager to keep track of and provide advice to relevant stakeholders on the interpretation and application of policies related to CDD.
  •  Ensure timely understanding and compliance with all the applicable regulations of the Bank, group and country including Money Laundering Prevention and KYC.

Strategy

  •  To align Role and deliver as per Wealth and Retail Banking (WRB) strategy.
  • Assist to ensure internal resources, systems, procedures and controls are in place, documented and operating effectively.
  •  Work closely with project teams/stakeholders to drive projects/initiatives related to unit to ensure successful and timely delivery
  •  Partner and actively collaborate with all relevant stakeholders across WRB to build robust and sustainable operational and execution excellence
  •  Use of data analysis and leverage on best-in-class market capabilities (e.g. predictive modelling, AI, digitisation, and other emerging technologies) to deliver a best-in-class services
  •  Drive ideation around country innovations and enhancements to digitisation and emerging technologies that give real time decisive client outcomes

Business

  • WRB Nepal
  •  WRB Operations

Processes

  • CDD Account Opening and Periodic/ Trigger Review/ Transaction Referrals

People & Talent

  •  Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values. Set appropriate tone and expectations from their team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners.

Risk Management

  •  Execution of responsibility for identifying, assessing, monitoring, and mitigating financial crime risk, sanctions risk and reputational risks to the Group. Also, an awareness and understanding of the main risks facing the Group and the role the individual plays in managing them.

Governance

  •  Ensure the CDD processes are followed as per Group and Local requirement.

Regulatory & Business Conduct

  •  Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct.
  •  Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct.
  •  Lead the CDD Operations to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles: [Fair Outcomes for Clients; Effective Financial Markets; Financial Crime Compliance; The Right Environment.]
  • Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters.
  •  [Central Bank e.g. NRB prescribed responsibilities and Rationale for allocation].
    [Where relevant – Additionally, for subsidiaries or relevant non -subsidiaries]
  •  Exercise authorities delegated by the Board of Directors and act in accordance with Articles of Association (or equivalent)

Key stakeholders

  •  WRB Relationship/ Branch Managers
  •  WRB Compliance Team
  •  Financial Crime Compliance Unit, Financial Crime Surveillance Operation

Skills and Experience

  •  Business Facilitation
  • Manage Change
  •  Management of Frontline Risk
  •  Business Governance & Support
  • Strategy & Business Model
  •  Service Delivery & Operations
  • Business Facilitation

Qualifications

  • Education: Master’s degree
  • Training: Proficient in MS Office applications
  • Languages: Fluent in English and Nepali

About Standard Chartered

We’re an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we’ve worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you’re looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can’t wait to see the talents you can bring us.

Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you’ll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion.

Together we:

  • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do
  • Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well
  • Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term

What we offer

In line with our Fair Pay Charter, we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing.

Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance.
Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum.
Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits
A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning.
Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies – everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.

Posiiton: Data Analyst
Location: Lalitpur

Application Deadline: Jul 31, 2026

Data Analyst is responsible to turn data into actionable insights that drive business decisions. The role involves creating dashboards, reports, and analytical models to support strategic initiatives and enhance performance across the organization.

Qualification and Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.
  • 3-4 years of experience as a Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or similar role.
  • Hands-on experience with Apache Superset (dashboard design, filters, chart customization).

Job Description

  • Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports in Apache Superset and Power BI.
  • Write and optimize SQL queries for data extraction, transformation, and analysis.
  • Perform data cleaning, preprocessing, and statistical analysis using Python (pandas, NumPy, matplotlib, seaborn).
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to define and monitor business metrics and KPIs.
  • Create and automate Excel reports and summaries for quick business insights.
  • Work with large datasets from multiple sources (databases, APIs, flat files).
  • Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and availability across analytics systems.
  • Support in building and maintaining data pipelines for regular reporting needs.
  • Communicate findings effectively through data storytelling and visualization.

Required Skills

  • Experience with ETL workflows or data engineering concepts.
  • Familiarity with version control systems (Git).
  • Understanding of data governance and data quality principles.
  • Experience integrating Superset with SQL databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.).
  • Strong proficiency in SQL (joins, subqueries, CTEs, aggregations, window functions).
  • Expertise in Power BI (DAX, Power Query, and data modeling).
  • Strong Python skills for data manipulation and automation.
  • Excellent command of Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query).
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
  • Ability to communicate technical findings to non-technical stakeholders.

Benefits of Working at eSewa

  • Stellar opportunity to work with the rising company
  • The amazing and passionate young team, beautiful office space
  • Trust of biggest FinTech company.
  •  One-of-a-kind company culture and growth opportunities to accelerate your career progression.

How to apply?

We are always keen to meet energetic and talented professionals who would like to join our team. Click on the button below and submit your application to apply for the post.

One Heart Worldwide (OHW) is an International Non-Governmental Organization that has been working in the area of Maternal and Neonatal Health (MNH) since 2011. OHW works with local communities and local health service providers to develop a culturally appropriate Network of Safety around mothers and infants, by raising awareness, teaching good practices, and distributing essential equipment to ensure that mothers and infants survive delivery and the first months of life.

Details / requirements:

Position: Communication Officer

Duty Station: Kathmandu with frequent field travel (up to 50%)

The Communication Officer supports efforts to strengthen One Heart Worldwide (OHW)’s visibility and impact by turning field stories, program results, and impact into compelling content. Working with Nepali and global teams, the role helps to manage digital presence, media relations, and stakeholder communications, ensuring consistent, high-quality messaging. It bridges implementation and public engagement while amplifying OHW’s identity, advocacy, and knowledge sharing.

Main Duties and Responsibilities:

Content Development

Create compelling success stories, case studies, newsletters, reports, donor visibility materials, and strategic communication content.
Work with the programs team to capture and document program achievements, impact stories, lessons learned, and stakeholder experiences.
Conduct interviews and collect photos, videos, testimonials, and field evidence to showcase program impact.
Strengthen donor and technical reporting through engaging storytelling, visuals, and evidence-based communication.

Digital Communication

  • With support from the Communications Lead, oversee the management of social media and digital communications platforms to strengthen organizational visibility, engagement, and advocacy.
  • Design and implement digital campaigns that amplify program impact, organization presence, and audience reach.
  • Create, manage, and coordinate engaging social media content in collaboration with Nepal & global teams.
  • Monitor digital performance, analyze audience insights, and optimize communication strategies for greater impact.

Public Communication

  • Cultivate strategic media partnerships to enhance organizational visibility and public engagement.
  • Support media relations by managing inquiries, securing coverage, and identifying high-impact publicity opportunities.
  • Develop and execute communication plans, key messages, and media materials to support organizational priorities.
  • Prepare and review external communications, including crisis and sensitive messaging, ensuring accuracy and organizational integrity.
  • Support the development and implementation of communications, engagement, and strategy campaigns that meet specific program, revenue, or recruitment-driven objectives.

Audio-Visual Production and Documentation

  • Document program activities, events, workshops, campaigns, and interventions through photography and videography.
  • Prepare, edit, and manage audio-visual materials for organizational, partner, and public communication purposes.
  • Ensure high-quality and impactful visuals support communication products.
  • Maintain and organize an archive of photos, videos, publications, and communication assets.

Knowledge Management

  • Collect, organize, and disseminate lessons learned, project achievements, success stories, and best practices.
  • Maintain communication databases, digital archives, and resources for future reference and reporting purposes.
  • Support organizational learning through systematic documentation and information sharing.

Compliance and Advocacy

  • Ensure high-quality, donor-compliant communications and develop impactful visibility materials that strengthen organizational branding and stakeholder engagement.
  • Lead advocacy, public engagement, and communication initiatives by coordinating with government agencies, partners, communities, media, and other key stakeholders.
  • Document and showcase program impact through field visits, strategic storytelling, timely reporting, event support, and other communication assignments aligned with organizational priorities.

Required Qualifications and Skills

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mass Communication or a related field.
  • Minimum four years of relevant experience in communications, media relations, public relations, or development-sector communication.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Nepali.
  • Strong storytelling, report writing, editing, and content development skills.
  • Experience managing social media platforms and digital communication campaigns.
  • Proficiency in photography, videography, and multimedia content production.
  • Experience in graphic design and multimedia tools.
  • Strong interpersonal, networking, and stakeholder engagement skills.
  • Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and meet deadlines.
  • Creative self-starter who takes initiative to propose new ideas and takes on responsibilities with minimal oversight
  • A high level of care and attention to detail; manages resources efficiently and checks work to ensure completeness and accuracy
  • Willingness for frequent travel to remote field areas.
  • Experience with social media management software (Sprout Social, Buffer, Hootsuite) and web analytics will be preferred.

WHAT WE VALUE

At OHW, we value diversity, equity, and inclusion, and we understand the value of developing a team with different perspectives, educational backgrounds, and life experiences. We prioritize diversity within our team, and we welcome candidates from all gender identities, castes, religious practices, sexual orientations, and abilities – among many others. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply for positions at OHW

Application Procedures:

Interested candidates who meet the above requirements are requested to visit the Application link and submit their application through the online portal by July 11, 2026.

OHW reserves the right to cancel, postpone, or modify the whole recruitment process and reject any/all applications without specifying any reasons whatsoever.

Position: Project Coordinator

Responsibilities

1. Product & Project Planning

  • Assist Product Managers throughout the product lifecycle, including ideation, development, testing, launch, and post-launch support.
  • Gather, analyze, and document business and stakeholder requirements, translating them into actionable project plans, user stories, and product backlogs.
  • Develop and maintain project plans, schedules, milestones, and deliverables aligned with product objectives.

2. Project Coordination & Execution

  • Coordinate activities across Product, Engineering, Design, QA, DevOps, Operations, Marketing, Sales, and Support teams to ensure timely execution.
  • Monitor project progress, track deliverables, identify bottlenecks, and follow up on pending actions to ensure smooth Agile delivery.
  • Facilitate Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, retrospectives, and backlog refinement.

3. Release & Delivery Management

  • Coordinate release planning and Go-Live activities by collaborating with QA, DevOps, and business stakeholders.
  • Ensure product readiness through proper testing coordination, deployment planning, documentation, and stakeholder communication.
  • Support internal teams with release documentation, training materials, and knowledge-sharing activities.

4. Stakeholder Communication & Documentation

  • Serve as the primary coordination point for project stakeholders and maintain effective communication throughout the project lifecycle.
  • Prepare and maintain project documentation, meeting minutes, action items, status reports, product documentation, and knowledge repositories.
  • Provide regular reports on project progress, KPIs, team velocity, and overall delivery performance.

5. Risk, Quality & Governance

  • Identify, assess, and proactively mitigate project risks, issues, and dependencies.
  • Monitor resource utilization and support budget tracking while identifying opportunities for operational efficiency.
  • Promote project governance by ensuring adherence to quality standards, Agile best practices, and organizational processes.

6. Product Improvement & Continuous Enhancement

  • Gather and analyze customer feedback, support tickets, product usage metrics, market trends, competitor analysis, and user insights to support product improvements.
  • Recommend process improvements that enhance product quality, team productivity, and delivery efficiency.

7. Team Collaboration

  • Foster effective collaboration across cross-functional teams to achieve project and product objectives.
  • Support and mentor team members by encouraging knowledge sharing, accountability, and continuous learning.

Requirement:

  • Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or any other relevant discipline.
  • Prior Years of Experience required: 3-4 years.

Required Technical Skills:

  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and Agile software development practices.
  • Proficiency with Project Management tools (e.g., Jira, Trello, Zoho Projects).
  • Familiarity with Product Management and collaboration tools (e.g., Confluence, Figma, Miro)
  • Basic understanding of software development principles, programming concepts, and Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC).
  • Knowledge of version control systems (e.g., Git), issue tracking systems, release management, and technical debt.
  • Working knowledge of databases, APIs, web technologies, and cybersecurity fundamentals.
  • Strong documentation, reporting, analytical, and coordination skills.

Required Behavioral Skills:

  • Strong interpersonal, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Excellent planning, organizational, and time management abilities.
  • Analytical thinking with strong problem-solving and conflict-resolution skills.
  • High attention to detail, accountability, and quality orientation.
  • Adaptability, curiosity, and a continuous learning mindset.
  • Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional Agile teams.

Why Join Us?

  • A stellar opportunity to work with the rising company
  • The amazing and passionate young team, beautiful office space
  • The trust of the biggest FinTech company.
  • One-of-a-kind company culture and growth opportunities to accelerate your career progression
  • Company-provided lunch facility

title.rendered: District Program Coordinator, Project Officer, Health Technician

content.rendered:
Group of Helping Hands (SAHAS) Nepal is a non-profit making non-governmental-organization founded in 1996 since then engaging social mobilization, grassroots organisation/Community-Based Network Organisation (CBNO) formation and enhancing their capacity through partnership and implementing diverse programme and projects in the sectors of sustainable agriculture, livelihood, food security, climate justice, advocacy and lobby, quality education, youth development in 21 districts of Nepal.

SAHAS Nepal is looking the potential candidates for the following positions:

Position: District Program Coordinator

Project: Building Empowered and Resilient Communities by Strengthening Livelihoods, Inclusion, Education and Food Security (BELIEF) Project

Duty station: Rolpa district

Required number: 1

Academic qualification and experiences:

  • Master’s degree in Social sciences, development studies or in Education with at least two years’ experience in relevant sectors, Bachelor’s degree in Social sciences, development studies, Education with at least five years’ experience in relevant field.
  • Experience of project management including planning, budgeting, monitoring and reporting, and developing proposals.
  • Experiences on participatory research, development approach, lobby and advocacy with understanding of local context and sensitiveness
  • Good coordination, communication and enthusiasm for working with vulnerable communities living in project areas
  • Good communication and analytical skills
  • Knowledge on financial management and staff supervision and capacity building
  • Good inter-personal, analytical, communication and facilitation skills
  • Fluent in oral and written Nepali and English language
  • Good coordination skills and efficient team player

Major Responsibilities

  • Provide technical support to the district project team in planning, budgeting, implementation, monitoring and reporting of the field activities in line with the project goal and objectives
  • Keep all the project staffs updated on project requirement; implementation guideline, tools and procedure for their understanding and use
  • Prepare and submit monthly, quarterly, semi-annually and annually report to the focal person
  • Review project budget, track the expenditure of budget regularly
  • Organise review meeting of the project at district, identify the gaps and issues and take the action accordingly for the further improvement
  • Coordinate with rural municipality level line agencies and like-minded organisations to develop the synergy in programs

Position: Project Officer

Projects: Local Initiatives for Food Security Transformation Project (LIFT VI)

Duty station: Udayapur district

Required number: 1

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • Bachelor’s degree in agriculture from the recognized university with at least 2 years’ experiences in resilience livelihood, agriculture, food security and nutrition
  • Excellent knowledge in statistical tools, data analysis, monitoring and report writing skill
  • Good coordination skills in community development, empowerment, networking, lobby and advocacy
  • Leadership, effective communication and time management
  • Knowledge on financial management and staff supervision, development and efficient team player
  • Fluent in oral and written Nepali and English language

Major responsibilities:

  • Planning Yearly, Quarterly and Monthly of the project activities based on the need assessment of target groups
  • Review project budget, track the expenditure of budget regularly
  • Provide technical support to the district project team in planning, implementation, monitoring and reporting of the field activities in line with the project goal and objectives
  • implementation of the planned activities in line with project budget and time frame and analysis of target vs achievement and burn rate.
  • Design and organize trainings and workshops for technical capacity building of Community Based Organisations (CBOs), Community Based Network
  • Organisation (CBNO) partner and smallholder farmers
  • Provide technical backstopping and supervision to the field staff in project related activities
  • Assist to prepare proposal to CBNOs and report writing
  • Follow up, monitoring and report writing of project progress in relation to activities, outputs, outcomes and impact (based on log frame/Outcome Impact Orientation)
  • Represent rural municipality, district level agencies for coordination and cooperation and access resources.

Position: Health Technician

Projects: Local Initiatives for Food Security Transformation Project (LIFT VI)

Duty station: Achham district

Required number: 1

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • Staff Nursed/ANM passed from the recognized College/University
  • Two years working experiences in the Community Development sectors especially in community health and nutrition
  • Knowledge in health and hygiene and ability to conduct training and development with capacity building
  • Good facilitation and coordination skills
  • Ability to work in remote areas communities
  • Efficient team player

Major responsibilities:

  • Effective delivery of training related to project activities
  • Proven ability to provide sound technical support and effective problem solving
  • Coordination and communication with Rural Municipalities/Municipalities and stakeholders
  • Prepare quality reports of the activities in Nepali language

Salary and Benefits: As per the rules and regulations of SAHAS Nepal.

Interested Nepali citizens are requested to send their updated CV with cover letter showing their interest including mailing address and telephone number along with two references to Founder and Executive Director by 7th July 2026 to sahasnepalvacancy@gmail.com.

Telephone enquiries will not be entertained. Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for further screening process. SAHAS Nepal reserves the right to reject any or all applications without assigning any reasons.

SAHAS Nepal promotes workforce diversity and applies positive decimation to candidates from discriminated groups. Applications from and members of indigenous and disadvantaged groups are highly encouraged.

(“SAHAS Nepal is committed to safeguarding and a zero-tolerance organization related to abuse, corruption, bribery, fraud.”)

Human Rights and Environment Development Centre (HuRENDEC) Nepal, Kalikot, is a non-governmental organization established in Kalikot that has been serving the Karnali region for more than three decades. The organization implements need-based, action-oriented, and multidisciplinary development initiatives through community engagement and social mobilization. Its major areas of work include human rights promotion, sustainable livelihoods, microenterprise development, climate change adaptation, cash-for-work programs, disaster risk management, health service, WASH, natural resource management, eco-friendly infrastructure development, advocacy and policy engagement, community empowerment, and infrastructure development.

Human Rights and Environment Development Center (HuRENDEC Nepal), in consortium with KIRDARC Nepal, is going to implement the “Promotion of Sustainable Health and Nutrition (POSHAN)” project in partnership with Plan International Nepal in three municipalities of Kalikot District: Naraharinath Rural Municipality, Sanni Triveni Rural Municipality, and Raskot Municipality.

The overall goal of the project is to improve the nutritional status and economic resilience of vulnerable households in the targeted communities through strengthened capacity and collaboration among civil society organizations (CSOs) and local governments.

To implement this project, HuRENDEC Nepal, Kalikot, invites applications from qualified, result- oriented, and highly motivated Nepali candidates for the following position, subject to the availability of project funds.

Position: Field Supervisor -3

Duty Station: Municipality Level (Raskot Municipality, Sanni Triveni RM, and Naraharinath RM)

Reports to: Project coordinator and EE officer

Position Summary

The Supervisor will support day-to-day field implementation, supervision, monitoring, and quality assurance of project activities at community level.

Key Responsibilities

  • Supervise field implementation of nutrition, WASH, livelihood, and advocacy activities.
  • Support mobilization of FCHVs, Health Mother Groups, Father Clubs, youth clubs, and farmer groups.
  • Monitor activity implementation and provide technical backstopping.
  • Verify beneficiary selection and participation processes.
  • Ensure quality delivery of training, awareness sessions, and community events.
  • Support collection of monitoring data and field-level reporting.
  • Conduct regular field visits and provide feedback to project staff
  • Assist in organizing meetings, workshops, and review sessions.
  • Ensure safeguarding, inclusion, and accountability principles are followed.
  • Support documentation of best practices, success stories, and lessons learned.

Qualifications and Experience

  • Inter level/ +2 level (Bachelors preferred) in Agriculture, JT, JTA, Social work, or related field.
  • Minimum 3 years of field-level community mobilization experience (for ISc AG or JT (agr, vet) and 4 years for others.
  • Preferred to EDF (Entrepreneurship Development Facilitator) course completed candidates
  • Experience in community mobilization and supervision.
  • Good understanding of nutrition, WASH, livelihood, and local governance.
  • Strong communication and reporting skills.
  • Ability to work in field level movement
  • Commitment to safeguarding, inclusion, and community empowerment

Application Procedure

Interested and result-oriented candidates who meet the above qualifications and experience are requested to submit an application along with an updated CV to vacancy.hurendec@gmail.com by 7th July, 2026.

Women, Dalit, Indigenous, Madhesi, and local candidates are strongly encouraged to apply and will be given preference in the selection process. Applicants must clearly mention the applied position in the subject line of the email. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further selection processes.

Any form of canvassing shall lead to automatic disqualification.

Position Title: Disability Inclusion Officer (Position Reserved for a Qualified Person with a Disability)
Project: SAMBHAV JEEVAN: Enabling Better Lives Through Sight and Sound (CBM Global Funded)
Reports To: Project Manager & NNJS Central Office
Duty Station: Surkhet (with frequent travel across Karnali Province)

Position Statement:

In accordance with the SAMBHAV JEEVAN project design and NNJS’s commitment to disability inclusion and equal employment opportunities, this position is exclusively open to qualified persons with disabilities. Applicants are encouraged from all disability groups who meet the required qualifications and are able to perform the essential functions of the position with or without reasonable accommodation.

Job Purpose:

To ensure meaningful inclusion of persons with disabilities across all project components by integrating disability-inclusive approaches in planning, implementation, monitoring, and reporting, in alignment with NNJS and CBM Global standards.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Disability Inclusion Mainstreaming:

  • Integrate disability inclusion into all project activities, plans, and strategies.
  • Ensure accessibility (physical, communication, information, and attitudinal) in project interventions.
  • Promote inclusive eye and ear health services targeting persons with disabilities.

2. Technical Advisory Role:

  • Provide technical guidance to the project team on disability-inclusive programming.
  • Develop tools, guidelines, and checklists for inclusive practices.
  • Support implementation of disability-inclusive service delivery models.
  • Provide technical support to other CBM Global partner if necessary.

3. Capacity Building:

  • Conduct training and orientation for staff, partners, and stakeholders on disability inclusion.
  • Sensitize health workers and community-level stakeholders on inclusive practices.

4. Coordination & Networking:

  • Coordinate with Organizations of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs), government agencies, and stakeholders.
  • Work closely with the CBM Global Nepal team based in Surkhet.
  • Maintain regular communication with NNJS Central Office for approvals and technical guidance.
  • Coordination and Collaboration with OPDs
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with local, district, and provincial OPDs.
  • Organize regular meetings with OPDs to include their inputs in project planning and implementation.
  • Ensure OPDs are actively involved in decision-making, reviews, feedback processes, and accessibility audits.
  • Support OPDs to connect with eye health services and local government bodies.
  • Facilitate linkages between OPDs and referral systems to increase access for persons with disabilities.

5. Monitoring & Reporting:

  • Develop and track disability inclusive indicators and implementation..
  • Ensure disability-disaggregated data collection and reporting.
  • Document best practices, case studies, and lessons learned.

6. Safeguarding & Rights-Based Approach:

  • Promote a rights-based, gender-responsive, and inclusive approach in all project activities.
  • Ensure safeguarding and protection of persons with disabilities in accordance with NNJS safeguarding policies.

Required Qualifications & Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Social Work, Rehabilitation, Development Studies, Disability Studies, Public Health, or a related field (Master’s degree preferred).
  • Minimum 2–3 years of experience in disability inclusion, rehabilitation, inclusive development, or disability rights programmes.
  • Experience working with NGOs/INGOs and disability-focused projects is preferred.
  • Strong understanding of disability rights, accessibility, and disability-inclusive development.
  • Excellent communication, coordination, and reporting skills.

Eligibility Criteria:

This position is reserved for a person with a disability. “Applicants must”:

  • Be a person with a disability as recognized under the prevailing laws of Nepal.
  • Clearly mention their disability type/category in the application.
  • State the Disability Identity Card category (Red, Blue, Yellow, or White Card) issued by the Government of Nepal.

NNJS is committed to providing reasonable accommodation throughout the recruitment process and employment in accordance with its Disability Inclusion and Equal Opportunity principles.
